title: Towards Urban Digital Twins: A Framework Integrating CARLA with 3D City Models
authors: Shunsuke Kamioka, Taku Yamazaki, Takumi Miyoshi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Digital twins, which reproduce real-world urban environments in virtual space, have recently attracted increasing attention. In Japan, the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism (MLIT) is developing nationwide 3D city models through Project PLATEAU. Although PLATEAU provides detailed static information, it lacks dynamic elements such as pedestrian and vehicle motion and therefore cannot function as a fully dynamic urban digital twin on its own. Meanwhile, CARLA, a widely used open-source road-traffic simulator, offers a highly realistic simulation environment but does not provide an efficient mechanism for ingesting and updating real-world data. Existing studies have explored integrating external data into CARLA to construct digital twins; however, these approaches primarily focus on generating actors from external data and struggle to update large numbers of actors in real time, limiting their applicability to city-scale digital twins. In this paper, we propose a method for constructing a dynamic urban digital twin by importing 3D city models into CARLA and continuously updating actor states based on externally acquired vehicle information. We implemented the proposed method and demonstrated that it can reproduce large-scale urban environments while maintaining stable real-time updates for a large number of vehicles.

title: Design and Implementation of a Smart Agriculture STEAM Program in Higher Education
authors: Hirooki Aoki
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper reports on the design and implementation of a short-term STEAM education program for smart agriculture. We invited two faculty members and three undergraduate students from the Institute of Technology of Cambodia and conducted intensive training that integrated image electronic engineering and agricultural engineering. The program consisted of three modules: LED matrix control for agricultural signage, three-dimensional plant measurement using an RGB-D camera, and water quality monitoring in an IoT-based aquaponics system. Undergraduate and graduate students at our university designed and delivered the classes in English. Through project-based group work, participants gained hands-on experience with core technologies for agricultural DX and developed international communication skills.

title: CNN-Based Point Cloud Geometry Compression with Feature Extraction for Object Recognition
authors: Hideaki Kimata
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Many studies have advanced to improve performance in both compression coding and object recognition for point clouds. A point cloud comprises numerous points, resulting in large data volumes, thus efficient compression methods are desirable. However, high compression using CNNbased methods is anticipated to reduce the object recognition performance. Point cloud compression techniques suitable for object recognition are necessary. We propose a novel compression method that improves object classification performance even at low coding rates by training the network to perform feature extraction for object recognition, achieved through modifying the loss function during DNN training for point cloud geometry compression. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can enhance object classification performance.

title: An Architecture to Provide Sensory Expansion for Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing Individuals in Digital Television
authors: Juliana C. Silva, Claudio F. M. Pinheiro, Eddie B. de Lima Filho, Rômulo Fabrício, Rafaell G. Santana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Television systems offer accessibility features for people who are deaf or hard of hearing, such as Closed Captioning or sign language, but these resources often lack personalization for different levels of hearing loss. This paper presents a system architecture that leverages smartphones connected to digital TV receivers to capture audio from the tuned service and perform real-time audio processing and tactile vibration generation. The proposed architecture enables personalized audio adaptation based on user hearing profiles and explores the use of smartphone-based haptic feedback to enrich television accessibility. Rather than focusing on clinical validation, this work aims to describe the design principles, processing pipeline, and integration mechanisms required to support future assistive television applications for deaf and hard-of-hearing users.

title: Fuzzy IoT Temperature Regulated Storage System with Consumer-Oriented Dashboard
authors: Ryan Rhay P. Vicerra, Lance Raphael T. Beltran, Marcus Allen P. Gomeri, Arthur Caleb L. Co, Rennan G. Baldovino, Ethan Bryce S. Kaw
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper presents a scalable fuzzy-based IoT temperature-regulated storage system designed to optimize conditions for temperature-sensitive products using intelligent control. The proposed system integrates IoT sensors to continuously monitor environmental parameters and applies a fuzzy logic algorithm to adjust cooling mechanisms in real time. A consumer-oriented dashboard provides users with live data visualization, alerts, and remote-control capabilities for system management. Test results show temperature stability and enhanced user engagement compared to conventional control methods.

title: Edge Computing Energy Management with Reinforcement Learning for Smart EV Charging
authors: Sin-Hang Chang, Yao Chiang, Hung-Yu Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: The rapid rise of electric vehicles (EVs) and distributed energy resources (DERs) has increased the complexity of local energy management, creating a need for intelligent coordination at the edge. This paper proposes a three-layer edge computing framework that integrates photovoltaic (PV) generation, an energy storage system (ESS), and multiple EV chargers under a unified cost objective. We adopt a multiagent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) algorithm for EV charging control, while the ESS follows a rule-based dispatch strategy. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme effectively reduces total operating cost while maintaining grid stability and acceptable EV charging performance.

title: Vector Perturbation Through SNR Maximization for Uplink Overload MIMO Spatial Multiplexing
authors: Ryoki Honjo, Satoshi Denno, Yafei Hou
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper proposes vector perturbation techniques in overloaded MIMO spatial multiplexing suitable for uplinks of wireless systems where the number of transmit antennas is less than that of receive antennas. The number of the spatially multiplexed signal streams is increased to that of the receive antennas in spite of the number of the transmit antennas. The proposed vector perturbation comprises a lattice reduction-based transmit power reduction with modulo function and brute force search for the signal to noise power ratio (SNR) maximization. The performance of the overload MIMO with the proposed vector perturbation is evaluated by computer simulation in terms of the average SNR and the bit error rate (BER). The simulation results show that the proposed vector perturbation achieves better performance than the conventional vector perturbation in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 4$</tex> overloaded MIMO channel where the number of transmit antennas and that of the receive antennas are 2 and 4.

title: Enhancing LLM Reliability and Efficiency with Query Refinement in Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Aya Mohamed Abd ElHamied, Ahmed A. AboelFarag, Yasser El-Sonbaty
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Large language models have achieved great success in various natural language processing tasks, yet they still face several serious challenges, such as errors and hallucinations, and high inference cost. The contribution of this work is an extension framework called Refined Query Retrieval Augmented Generation (RQ-RAG) to enhance query handling through rewriting, decomposition, and disambiguation. The algorithm works with a specifically chosen dataset of some 42,810 samples, and search results from DuckDuckGo are used to provide answers that seemingly make sense in context. We are interested in exploiting LLaMA-4 Maverick, a state-of-the-art model with 17B parameters, as their base. We demonstrate on RQ-RAG dataset how the query refinement technique impacts upon facts' accuracy. On the single-hop (ARC-C, OBQA, POPQA) and multi-hop (HotpotQA, 2Wiki, MuSiQue) experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the baseline models and previous state-of-the-art retrievalaugmented approaches in terms of Accuracy and Match Score as well as F1 Score. The results show that query refinement can increase the reliability, robustness, and efficiency of LLM enabled systems.

title: Time-Variant Transformer for Predicting Alzheimer's Disease Progression from Longitudinal Data
authors: Habiba Assem Sharaf, Mohamed Hamed Kholief, Hany Hanafy Mahmoud Said
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease gradually affects cognitive functions such as memory, reasoning, and daily activities. Although transformer-based models have improved disease progression forecasting, they often face limitations in handling uneven visit times, incomplete records, and diverse data modalities. This study introduces a Time-Visit Transformer (TVT) tailored to predict Alzheimer's progression by explicitly modeling the timing gaps between patient follow-ups. TVT (i) encodes each visit, (ii) augments representations with Time2Vec to capture the temporal phase and scale, (iii) adjusts attention using the real-time intervals between visits delta-Time-FiLM, and (iv) incorporates site-specific context through Relational-FiLM. An attention layer then reads the sequence of visits in order and can gracefully handle missing entries. In head-to-head evaluations on longitudinal cohorts, TVT achieves an AUC-ROC of 0.9331 for conversion from cognitively normal (CN) to mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and 0.9529 for MCI to Alzheimer's disease (AD), demonstrating a time-sensitive model suitable for practical clinical use.

title: Enhnacing the Production Scheduling in a Multi-Product Powdered Mix Factory
authors: Lina Ismail, Farida Haytham Wagdy, Mayar Essam Yassin, Khaled S. El-Kilany
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Efficient production scheduling remains a key challenge in modern manufacturing, especially in multi-product systems that rely on shared resources and experience sequencedependent changeovers. This study developed a discrete event simulation model in ExtendSim to enhance production scheduling performance in a powdered mix factory in Egypt. The developed model represented real production processes, including sieving, mixing, and packaging operations considering crucial factors/parameters such as sequence-dependent changeovers, machine breakdowns, and operational constraints. Validation against actual production data showed a deviation of only 6.27 %, confirming the model's accuracy and reliability. Through extensive scenario testing, the enhanced scheduling configuration achieved a 15 % reduction in average cycle time and a 17 % increase in total throughput compared with the factory's existing scheduling method. These improvements highlight the effectiveness of discrete event simulation as a decision-support tool for production scheduling and resource management. The results demonstrate that simulation-based models can significantly enhance system performance in complex, changeover-intensive food production environment.

title: The Impact of Generative AI and Large Language Models on Telemedicine Diagnostics: A Systematic Review
authors: Sarthak Srivastava, Harshvardhan Chandrakar, Harshpreet Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The combination of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) is redefining telemedicine diagnostics by improving diagnostic accuracy, automating routine clinical tasks, and expanding access to quality healthcare for underserved and remote populations. This systematic review compiles recent evidence on the use of these technologies within telehealth environments, focusing on the diagnostic tasks they support and their clinical and operational impacts. Using a structured search and screening process, relevant literature covering language-based reasoning, multimodal image and signal analysis, and workflow augmentation was analyzed to evaluate performance, reliability, and real-world readiness. The findings indicate that current Large Language Models and multimodal systems demonstrate high accuracy in structured diagnostic contexts and can significantly reduce documentation workload. However, existing evidence remains largely preliminary and concentrated within controlled experimental settings. Major challenges hindering safe implementation include model hallucinations, limited interpretability, dataset bias, data governance and privacy concerns, and fragmented regulatory frameworks. This review highlights key research priorities such as specialty-specific validation, integration of explainable AI mechanisms, comprehensive bias auditing, and regulatory harmonization to promote responsible adoption.

title: A Smart Virtual Makeup Try-On and Recommender System Using Artificial Intelligence and Augmented Reality
authors: B. Lanitha, Anushya R, Jagasri V R, Preethi P P, Sowmiya P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The cosmetics industry is rapidly adopting artificial intelligence (AI) and Augmented Reality (AR) to improve customer experience and personalization. Unfortunately, most of the virtual makeup test applications currently in use rely on flimsy overlays and ignore important aspects such as skin conditions, lifestyle, and allergies, which results in sub-par recommendations and unhappy users. In this paper a new AI-based framework for a smart virtual makeup try-on and recommender system that combines deep learning, augmented reality, and computer vision is presented. For high resolution skin analysis (acne, pigmentation, dryness, and redness), the system uses Vision Transformers (ViT/FastViT). For contextual personalization, Tabular Transformers/MLPs are used to process user profile data. Visual and profile features are dynamically balanced by an attention-based fusion mechanism, and recommendations are transparent thanks to Explainable AI (SHAP/LIME). Immersion and interaction are made possible by a real-time AR try on module that superimposes recommended items on the user’s face. In this work, personalized recommendations, AR visualization, and skin health analysis are combined to provide safer, more reliable, and user-focused beauty technology for the cosmetics and e-commerce sectors.

title: Modification and Testing of Patch Antenna for Wireless Communication System
authors: Maklayne Barros de Pontes Santiago, Maryam Liaqat, Maria Luysa Teles Barcelar, Matheus Henrique Barros Da Silva, Frederico Dias Nunes, Antonio Celso Dantas Antonino
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: Osteoporosis is a progressive bone disease characterized by reduced bone mineral density, leading to increased fracture risk. Conventional diagnostic techniques such as X-ray and dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA) involve ionizing radiation and high operational costs, limiting their use for continuous monitoring. Mineral deficiencies, notably calcium and Vitamin D, precipitate a critical decline in bone mineral density (BMD), resulting in structural degradation and heightened fracture susceptibility—a condition exemplified by severe hypocalcemia observed in regional postmenopausal populations (e.g., Peshawar, Pakistan, is below 1300 mg/day WHO standard). The early diagnosis is the solution of this hypocalcemia which can be possible by Microwave Imaging (MWI). Dielectric properties of defected bones can be measured using MWI, non-conventional non-ionizing methods. Furthermore, its reliance on low-cost, miniaturized MWI as an ideal candidate for a portable, point-of-care diagnostic system. The MWI technique offers a pivotal solution for the early-stage stratification and preventative management of osteoporotic defects, mitigating future morbidity associated with pathological bone fragility. In this work, a modified dual-band microstrip patch antenna is proposed for microwave-based bone health assessment.

title: Machine learning methods for SWIPT enabled NOMA networks under imperfect SIC conditions
authors: Vegi Neelima Thulasi Aparna, A Vijayasankar, Peruri Sai Meghana, Surada Sridhar, Yaddanapudi Sarada Devi, Kota Nageswara Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The integration of Simultaneous Wireless Information and Power Transfer (SWIPT) with Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) has shown significant promise for achieving energy-efficient and reliable communications in Internet-of-Things (IoT) networks. However, practical system performance is often constrained by imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC), non-linear energy harvesting (EH), and channel fading, which adversely impact outage probability and overall reliability. In this study, we introduce a machine learning-driven strategy utilizing an oracle policy to minimize outage probability in SWIPT-enabled NOMA networks. This approach dynamically optimizes power allocation and splitting ratios, improving both system reliability and energy efficiency under realistic channel conditions. Simulation results indicate that the oracle-based policy surpasses traditional schemes, offering notable reductions in outage probability and valuable guidance for efficient resource management in next-generation wireless networks.

title: Nonlinear Adaptive Filtering with Robust Adaptive Exponential Functional Link Networks under the Impulsive Interference
authors: Parishmita Deka, Bijit Kumar Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Adaptive filtering for nonlinear system identification has been explored using many different approaches, and adaptive exponential functional link networks (AEFLN) are one of the popular choices. Although performance deteriorates under the impulsive interference with non-Gaussian characteristics. To address this limitation, this work integrates a generalized soft-root-sign (GSRS) cost function with AEFLN, resulting in a robust algorithm termed AEFLN-GSRS. Performance results demonstrate that the proposed AEFLN-GSRS approach effectively mitigates the influence of impulsive noise while maintaining desired convergence behavior and achieves improved steady-state performance and enhanced robustness compared to conventional AEFLN and robust AEFLN algorithms under Gaussian and impulsive interference.

title: Broad and Spectrally-Efficient Beamforming for Uni-polarized Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
authors: Mohammad Javad-Kalbasi, Mohammed Saif, Vasileios G. Ataloglou, Kypros M. Kossifos, Marco A. Antoniades, George V. Eleftheriades, Shahrokh Valaee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) is composed of low-cost elements that manipulate the propagation environment from a transmitter by applying phase shifts to incoming signals before they are reflected. This paper explores a unipolarized RIS with linear shape aimed at transmitting a common signal to multiple user equipments (UEs) spread across a wide angular region. To achieve uniform coverage, the uni-polarized RIS is designed to emit a broad and spectrally-efficient beam featuring a spatially flat-like array factor, diverging from the conventional narrow beam approach. To achieve this objective, we start by deriving probabilistic lower and upper bounds for the average spectral efficiency (SE) delivered to the UEs. Leveraging the insights from the lower bound, we focus on optimizing the minimum value of the power domain array factor (PDAF) across a range of azimuth angles from −π/2 to π/2 . We employ the continuous genetic algorithm (CGA) for this optimization task, aiming to improve the SE delivered to the UEs while also creating a wide beam. Extensive simulation experiments are carried out to assess the performance of the proposed code, focusing on key metrics such as the minimum and average values of the PDAF and the SE delivered to the UEs. Our findings demonstrate that the proposed code enhances the minimum SE delivered to the UEs while maintaining the desired attribute of a broad beam. This performance is notably superior to that of established codes, including the Barker, Frank, Golay, and Chu codes. Moreover, the effectiveness of the proposed code in generating a broad beam is experimentally validated using a RIS prototype.

title: Self-training Based Source-free Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing Scene Classification
authors: Shuo Han, Xu Tang, Jingjing Ma, Xiangrong Zhang, Fang Liu, Licheng Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Remote sensing scene classification (RSSC) is crucial for interpreting remote sensing (RS) images across various applications. Existing deep learning methods have promoted RSSC results to a new level. However, most of them rely on an assumption that training and test data are independently and identically distributed. While this requirement can be readily met under laboratory conditions, it proves to be exceptionally stringent in practical settings, as the collected new RS images may differ significantly in distribution from the RS images used to train the classification model. In this cross-domain case, many successful models would be disabled. To address the above challenges and enhance the practicability of the RSSC model, this paper proposes a self-training based source-free domain adaptation (SFDA) method named STSFDA. First, a multi-task source domain pre-training strategy is developed to provide a strong initialization for the SFDA process. Next, a teacher-student network is adopted to drive the SFDA process. In the teacher network, target RS scenes are divided into two groups based on confidence and then passed to the student network. The student network applies supervised learning to reliable samples and unsupervised learning to uncertain samples. A neighbor-guided pseudo-labeling strategy is also introduced to generate more reliable pseudo-labels with the consideration of neighborhood information. In addition, mixup learning and contrastive learning are applied to all target domain samples to obtain more discriminative feature representations. Experimental results demonstrate that STSFDA consistently outperforms many existing baseline models, highlighting its effectiveness and potential for future research in the RS community. Code is available at https://github.com/TangXu-Group/STSFDA.

title: Service-based RAN Control Plane Decoupling Based on the Fusion of Protocol Semantics and Topological Features
authors: Shangyuan Du, Xin Sun, Chunjing Yuan, Zecheng Fang, Lin Tian
abstract: To address the diversified scenario requirements and stringent real-time challenges of Beyond 5G/6th Generation (B5G/6G) networks, this paper focuses on the problem of insufficient scalability caused by the tight coupling of control plane (CP) functions in traditional radio access network (RAN), and proposes a service-based RAN CP decoupling method based on the fusion of protocol semantics and topological features. First, this study establishes a Context-Operation-State component model to define atomic service units, and introduces a Text Structure Fusion Architecture (TSFA) to extract the fused representation of component semantics and structural interactions. To overcome the limitations of general pre-trained models in understanding vertical domain terminology, this study constructs a communication domain specific dataset named 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) Protocol Semantic Matching (3GPP-PSM), and fine-tunes the Qwen3-Embedding model via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). Finally, the hierarchical clustering algorithm is applied to generate service units with different granularities. Comprehensive experiments conducted using metrics including the elbow method, silhouette coefficient, and latency increment demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms both the single-feature baselines and existing decoupling schemes significantly. The experimental results indicate that the proposed method not only achieves the goal of “high cohesion and low coupling” but also effectively mitigates the latency overhead induced by CP function splitting. This study provides theoretical and technical support for the softwarization and service-based architecture (SBA) of 6G networks as well as the “white-box” transformation of base stations, thereby promoting the evolution of wireless networks toward flexibility, efficiency, and intelligence.

title: Exploration of Optimal Conditions for a Simple Anisotropy Control on Thin-film Element Using Joule Heating
authors: Hiroaki Kikuchi, Shuto Kawasaki, Haruhi Isomura
abstract: We have proposed a simple method for controlling magnetic anisotropy in thin-film magnetic devices by combining Joule heating with permanent magnets. For thin-film magnetoimpedance elements, we confirmed the possibility of controlling anisotropy using Joule heating alone, and analyzed—based on FEM simulations—the differences in properties between Joule heating alone and Joule heating combined with permanent magnets. Furthermore, we experimentally demonstrated that by alternately applying Joule heating alone and Joule heating combined with permanent magnets, it is possible to achieve magnetoimpedance properties close to those obtained by conventional annealing under a static magnetic field. This method also has the potential to provide improved controllability.

title: Scenario Equivalent Decoupling-Based Optimal Cost Control Strategy for Power Systems Considering N–1 Security and Uncertainties
authors: Dapeng Wang, Cong Zhang, Huaizhi Yang, Lili He, Long Zeng, Jiayong Li, Lipeng Zhu, Ke Zhou, Lei Xie, Julong Chen, Zhikang Shuai
abstract: This article proposes a novel N–1 interval optimal power flow (NIOPF) scheme that considers N–1 contingencies and interval uncertainties in renewable generation. Based on the scenario-equivalent decoupling method (SEDM), the NIOPF addresses the problems associated with existing methods, including constraint violations and low solution efficiency. First, the NIOPF model is formulated by integrating security constraints involving interval variables under both N–0 normal and N–1 contingency scenarios, along with scenario transition constraints. Second, a credible domain reduction method (CDRM) based on the Minkowski difference is proposed to handle interval constraints, converting them into tractable deterministic constraints that guarantee the system operates within safe limits across all uncertain scenarios. Then, the SEDM represents numerous N–1 scenarios equivalently as two positive and negative deviation scenarios, effectively reducing the dimensionality of N–1 scenarios. Next, a two-stage optimization model that integrates the N–0 normal operating scenario with the two equivalent deviation scenarios is constructed and solved iteratively to improve computational efficiency. Finally, case studies on the modified IEEE 30-bus system verify that the proposed SEDM ensures constraint satisfaction under uncertainties while improving computational efficiency. Further tests on the modified IEEE 118-bus and PEGASE 1354-bus systems confirm its engineering applicability.

title: Real-Time Seat-Based Sitting Action Recognition Using Soft Pressure Sensors and Embedded Machine Learning
authors: Chi Cuong Vu, Viet Hoang Nguyen, Jooyong Kim
abstract: Seat-based sitting behavior monitoring is an important research area due to its impact on spinal health, work performance, and long-term comfort. However, challenges remain in accuracy, stability, cost, deployment flexibility, and data privacy. This paper proposes an integrated system combining soft pressure sensors, embedded machine learning, and a flexible communication architecture. The pressure sensor, fabricated from conductive textile and Au film, achieves a sensitivity of up to 0.46 kPa⁻¹ with response/recovery times of approximately 60/50 ms. The compact machine learning model (4.34 KB) achieves 99.7% accuracy in recognizing six sitting actions, including trunk tilting and sitting transitions. All processing and inference are performed locally on the embedded board, with only classification results transmitted to the cloud to enhance data security. The system focuses on trunk tilt and sitting transition recognition rather than full-body ergonomic posture assessment. These results demonstrate the feasibility of real-time, resource-efficient monitoring of sitting actions on edge IoT devices. This pilot study used data from one participant, with a fixed chair and sensor setup in a single recording session. Therefore, the results show feasibility under controlled conditions, not generalizability across subjects.

title: Service Orchestration Strategy in STINs: A Multi-Task SFC Mapping Approach
authors: Xiaojing Fan, Huachun Zhou, Tao Zheng
abstract: 3GPP NTN enables deploying network functions in space. However, existing service orchestration frameworks and service function chain (SFC) mapping methods struggle to meet the flexible orchestration of diverse services in satellite-terrestrial integrated networks (STINs). To address this challenge, we propose SOFTIN, a three-plane service orchestration framework based on software-defined networking (SDN) and network function virtualization (NVF). The framework abstracts the mapping from user requirements to service functions into an orchestration workflow. Within SOFTIN, the SFC orchestration problem is formulated as an integer nonlinear programming (INLP) model, and a single SFC mapping algorithm based on double deep Q-network (DDQN) is designed to minimize deployment overhead. To enhance scalability and convergence stability while supporting service diversity and concurrency, we further propose a multi-task parallel mapping algorithm, termed multi-task graph neural network-based actor–critic with proximal policy optimization (MT-GACP). This algorithm integrates a graph neural network (GNN)-based representation learning network with a proximal policy optimization (PPO) module to mitigate convergence oscillations caused by topology changes. Moreover, multiple reinforcement learning agents manage SFC requests for multiple tasks. Finally, experiments on a Linux prototype system validate the SOFTIN framework's feasibility and orchestration capabilities. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithms achieve near-optimal performance across multiple metrics, such as placement success rate and resource utilization, and demonstrate excellent scalability in both multi-layer satellite networks (MLSN) and the Iridium network.
