title: Efficient RTL to GDSII Layout Implementation and Verification of UART Protocol Using SystemVerilog and OpenLane
authors: Dhanavath Rajesh, Arvind Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper outlines the entire process of designing an ASIC for a Universal Asynchronous Receiver and Transmitter (UART), from RTL to GDSII layout. Yosys begins by creating a gate-level netlist using Verilog's RTL design. Functional verification is thoroughly examined using SystemVerilog testbenches to ensure that it is accurate at the RTL level. The OpenLane toolchain, which includes the OpenROAD application, is used to automate the process of floorplanning, placement, routing, and timing closure for the physical design. The paper goes into more detail about the most important design problems that were faced and talks about possible ways to make things even better. We show simulation results with waveform outputs from different testing scenarios, as well as backend results that show the physical layout and Quality of Results metrics, to prove that the process works effectively, validating the capabilities of the open-source EDA toolchain for producing production-ready ASIC layouts. The importance of each phase evaluated in the ASIC development life cycle is discussed in the concluding sections, along with potential terminology for relating the measurement of design performance and potential solutions for future work that aims to achieve an order of magnitude higher operating frequencies.

title: Wavelet Neural Network-based Human Activity Recognition: A Gravity-Aware Hybrid Architecture
authors: Faizal Shanavas Puthiyaveettil, Ramanujam E, Ashutosh Sahoo, Ponnekanty Y Raj Kamal Tutu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using wearable sensors is often restricted by noisy, high-dimensional data. Standard deep learning models frequently fail to capture fine-grained time-frequency details, leading to specific confusion between static postures such as "Sitting" and "Standing". This paper introduce a hybrid model featuring an Auto-Grouped Wavelet Layer that generates 511 features via a Power Set approach, and a dedicated Static Branch using Global Average Pooling. By isolating the gravity vector, the proposed architecture effectively resolves static ambiguity and ensures robust classification accuracy. This study evaluates the proposed architecture on the widely-used UCI Human Activity Recognition (UCI-HAR) dataset,where the proposed WNN-CNN and WNN-LSTM models achieved accuracies of 95% and 93% respectively, demonstrating significant improvements over baseline models.

title: Deep Mixture of Experts Network for Resource Optimization in Aerial–Terrestrial CF-mMIMO Systems Under URLLC
authors: Donggen Li, Chong Huang, Jingfu Li, Pei Xiao, Wenjiang Feng, Dusit Niyato, Zhu Han
abstract: As a critical component of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks, ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) is expected to support real-time and reliable information exchange in low-altitude environments. However, achieving URLLC often incurs significant resource overhead, including increased bandwidth consumption, higher transmit power, and denser access point (AP) deployment, which pose significant challenges to both spectral efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE). Besides, existing iterative optimization algorithms are computationally intensive and struggle to meet the latency requirements of URLLC. To address these challenges, we propose a hybrid aerial-terrestrial cell-free massive MIMO (CF-mMIMO) network to support diverse services, along with a channel prediction network and a deep mixture of experts (MoE) network for uplink optimization. First, we design a channel prediction network (CP-Net) to mitigate channel aging caused by high-mobility user equipment (UE). CP-Net employs three Transformer-based sub-networks for aged channel state information (CSI) prediction, while a channel quality-aware loss function is introduced to improve the prediction accuracy of weak links. Based on the predicted CSI, we develop a deep MoE network (MoE-Net) for power allocation comprising three expert models targeting different objectives. Then, we introduce a weighted gating network (WT-Net) to learn an efficient adaptive combination of expert outputs. The proposed framework better captures heterogeneous UE requirements and improves communication performance under URLLC constraints. Numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Stretchable AgNP/AgNW Co-Deposited Conductive Fiber with PDMS Encapsulation for Patellar Tendon Reflex Strain Sensing
authors: Ying-Nien Chou, Po-Chun Huang, Cheng-You Chi, Kai-Sheng Hsieh, Wan-Jung Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study presents a stretchable conductive fiber designed for dynamic patellar tendon reflex sensing, fabricated through AgNP/AgNW co-deposition on alkali-treated polyurethane (PU) fibers. The alkali pretreatment promotes silver ion absorption and enhances insitu nucleation of AgNPs, while subsequent AgNW deposition forms a continuous conductive network with improved mechanical compliance. To further enhance durability, a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) protective layer is applied, providing resistance against moisture, oxidation, and repeated bending during knee-tapping tests. When the patellar tendon is stimulated, the involuntary muscle contraction induces strain on the fiber, resulting in an immediate resistance change that can be used to quantify reflex onset, peak response, and response latency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed conductive fiber exhibits stable electromechanical performance and reliable signal output during repetitive knee-tapping events, indicating its potential for wearable biomedical sensing applications and neuromuscular function evaluation.

title: Effectivity of Exposure Therapy Simulations in Video Games on Mitigating Social Anxiety
authors: Jasmine L. Gallaron, Marvi G. Fetiluna, Lieudwig A. Abella, Vicente F. Patalita
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study evaluates a 3D game-based simulation designed as a controlled aid for exposure therapy-aligned interventions for social anxiety. The platform integrates a Python-powered facial-emotion recognition (FER) module and IBM Watson-driven dialogue to monitor user mood and dynamically adjust session flow. Grounded in the APA Severity Measure for Social Anxiety Disorder, the research analyzed emotional patterns across varying social scenarios. Results demonstrate that the simulation effectively evokes scenarioappropriate responses: casual interactions yielded higher positive affect, while performance-based tasks-such as public speaking-successfully elicited neutral and negative emotions associated with evaluative stress. Notably, the 6% margin of positive over negative affect demonstrates the system's ability to maintain user engagement without exceeding manageable emotional thresholds. High usability ratings and successful emotional induction indicate the simulation is a viable tool for early-stage exposure-based support.

title: iNoodle: An Artificial Intelligence Seasoning-Packet Recognition System Applied to Packaged Dried Noodle Production Line
authors: Wan-Jung Chang, Ping-Ying She, Chia-Yu Wu, Jian-Ping Su
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper proposes a seasoning-packet recognition system based on an AI-based deep learning model, called iNoodle. By designing an inspection chamber that integrates a camera module and an AI-edge platform with an inverted U-shaped base, iNoodle can be easily and cost-effectively deployed on a packaging line to detect abnormal quantities and types of seasoning packets. Experimental results demonstrate that iNoodle effectively and accurately identifies types and quantities of seasoning packets, enabling dried noodle manufacturers to eliminate the need for manual inspection and decrease inspection errors, thereby reducing production costs and improving product quality.

title: Character MBTI for Quantifying Narrative Atmosphere in Novel Recommendation
authors: Jikang Zhang, Yusong Zhou, Yuanyuan Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Current novel recommendation systems often fail to capture the subtle narrative atmosphere that emerges from a story's characters and interactions. To address this limitation, we introduce a computational framework that quantifies narrative atmosphere using character Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) profiling. The framework uses a character influence model that considers the quantity of dialogue and the semantic importance of speech, as measured by Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF), to weigh character profiles derived from multi-model fusion. Our experiments demonstrate that conventional evaluation metrics are not well-suited to subjective tasks such as this one. Therefore, we propose an alternative evaluation approach based on Kendall's coefficient and paired win counts. It identifies the most effective models and reveals the limitations of simple averaging fusion strategies. Overall, this study offers a fresh perspective on deep text comprehension and provides a more reliable method for evaluating subjective ranking tasks.

title: On Taipei MRT Passenger Flow Prediction by Considering Multiple External Factors
authors: Yu-Wen Hung, Yu-Siou Siao, Kuei-Ping Shih
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper aims to address the key challenge in metro passenger flow prediction: accurately capturing the influence of multiple external factors while overcoming the high computational cost of deep learning models. To balance the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, this paper incorporates model compression techniques and proposes a Factor-Specialized Multi-Teacher Knowledge Distillation (FS-MTKD) framework, in which multiple expert teachers are specialized based on different external factors. Unlike traditional single-model approaches that attempt to learn all factor relationships simultaneously, this paper designs multiple expert teacher models, each dedicated to capturing the impact of a single external factor on passenger flow. Through Knowledge Distillation (KD), a lightweight student model is guided to absorb the heterogeneous knowledge distilled from all expert teachers and to achieve effective balancing among multiple influencing factors. The experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. After applying FS-MTKD, the student model achieves prediction accuracy comparable to that of more complex teacher models, while significantly reducing both model parameters and computational cost.

title: Sparse Matrix Accelerator Based on Dynamic Pruning
authors: Mei-Yun Chen, Yu-Cheng Fan, Ching-Min Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: To accelerate model computation, this paper presents a dynamic token selector based on Vision Transformer, focusing primarily on pruning operations using a self-attention mechanism. To enable the model to adaptively retain key tokens and discard unimportant ones based on the importance of the input data, accordingly decreasing computational load and increasing efficiency, a module is added after the Softmax operation to dynamically identify and retain important tokens, directly using pre-calculated attention as the pruning basis, resulting in simpler hardware.

title: SUROY TA!: A Virtual City Simulator Exploring Historical Landmarks in Cebu City
authors: MA. Angelica Elimanco, Vicente Patalita, Temothy Homecillo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This project introduces an immersive virtual tourism platform that addresses the limitations of current services like Google Street View and YouTube 360 by offering real-time 3D exploration, spatial audio, and virtual tour narration. Focused on key landmarks in Cebu City, the platform enables users to freely navigate interactive environments enhanced with cultural and historical context. Designed for both VR and desktop computers, it prioritizes accessibility, educational value, and user engagement. By combining advanced graphics, virtual guides, and optimized asset use, the system provides a scalable and inclusive alternative to traditional tourism experiences.

title: Experimental Study of Timing Synchronization Techniques for Outdoor Mobile MIMO Transmission
authors: Haowen Bai, Hidekazu Murata, Satoshi Suyama, Huiling Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In outdoor mobile environments, our multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) transmission experimental system experiences continuously varying timing offsets between transmitted and received samples. These variations arise from leading and delayed multipath components, as well as changes in radio propagation distance. As a result, timing synchronization fails, resulting in degradation of the bit error rate (BER). In this paper, we consider two synchronization algorithms that estimate and compensate for timing offsets between transmission and reception samples using correlation functions, and evaluate their performance experimentally. The experimental results show that both algorithms can compensate timing offsets in real time and reduce the BER, while one of them achieves a significantly greater improvement.

title: SpeakBright: A Mobile Application for Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) for Nonverbal ASD Learners
authors: Jessa Donnalyn Reroma, Jovelyn C. Cuizon, Justin Antonio Bolivar, Ethan Ortiz
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and speech impairments face significant communication challenges, impacting their development and quality of life. Existing solutions like the Picture Exchange Communication System (PECS) and Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) apps have limitations, such as a lack of emotional expression support and progress tracking. This study introduces SpeakBright, a mobile application which combines traditional methods like PECS and Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) and modern technologies to provide child friendly learning tool with adaptive recommendations, progress tracking, and completion estimation. The application adapts to a child's developmental stage, which allows children to expand their vocabulary, improve sentence formation, and support positive communication habits, enhancing both verbal and non-verbal abilities. Testing results indicate a high level of acceptability by care providers and ASD teachers of SpeakBright's data-driven functionalities in support of educational intervention.

title: A Novel Quality Model for Education Management Information Systems: Insights From the Openemis Case Study in Jordan
authors: Ahmad Althunibat, Addelrahim Emad Mehyar, Seif hashem Alazzam, Naif Alzriqat, Bassam M. Al-mahadeen
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Education Management Information Systems (EMIS) in modern educational administration, highlighting challenges such as rapid data processing, user accessibility, and advanced analysis tools that limit user satisfaction and data-driven decisionmaking. A mixed-methods approach was employed, involving a questionnaire completed by 519 EMIS users and semistructured interviews with 15 experts from organizations like UNESCO and the Jordanian Ministry of Education. Results indicated a need for improvements in real-time data processing and user accessibility, with statistical analysis showing these factors account for 74.7 % of the variance in user satisfaction. The study demonstrated strong reliability with a Cronbach's Alpha of 0.928, and user satisfaction ratings improved from 2.95 to 3.7 after implementing a proposed quality framework. Despite positive findings, experts emphasized resource constraints and the necessity for ongoing user education. The research aims to enhance EMIS, making it more user-friendly and responsive in educational management.

title: APEX-IDS: Advanced Performance Excellence IoT Detection System Using Deep Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Mostafa Mohamed, Saad M. Ibrahim, Amr A. Abohany, Muhammad Mahmoud
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created unprecedented cybersecurity challenges, necessitating advanced intrusion detection systems capable of identifying sophisticated attack patterns in real-time. This paper presents APEX-IDS (Advanced Performance Excellence IoT Detection System), a novel deep learning framework that integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques for comprehensive IoT intrusion detection. Our multi-layered architecture combines spatial and temporal feature extraction with attention mechanisms to achieve superior detection accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency for edge deployment. Extensive evaluation on multiple benchmark datasets including CICIoT2023, UNSW-NB15, ToN_IoT, NSLKDD, and IoT-23 demonstrates exceptional performance with 99.23 % accuracy, 98.87 % precision, 99.15 % recall, 99.01 % F1score, and 0.996 AUC. The integration of SHAP and LIME explainability techniques provides transparent decision-making capabilities essential for operational deployment. Statistical significance testing confirms APEX-IDS superiority over existing approaches (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>), while maintaining practical deployment feasibility with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 m s}$</tex> detection time and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5. 2 M B}$</tex> memory footprint.

title: Building a Framework Towards Intelligent Robotic Process Automation Capabilities
authors: Doha Rahma, Mohamed Aborizka, Menna Kamel
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: To the best of our knowledge, this work introduces a framework that combines YOLOv7-based deep learning and real-time object detection with robotic process automation (RPA) to enhance decision-making. The goal is to enable bots to “see,” interpret visual elements, and choose actions dynamically instead of following static workflows. Traditional RPA systems often fail when dealing with unstructured data, unexpected layouts, or multilingual interfaces and usually depend on predefined rules, which leads to increased errors and human intervention. Our approach integrates deep learning for perception, transfer learning for adaptability across domains, and AI-driven decision modules to make automation more flexible and context-aware. Experimental results demonstrate notable improvements in accuracy, efficiency, and recovery from unpredictable scenarios. By linking real-time detection with autonomous decisions, the framework pushes RPA toward scalable, intelligent, and adaptive business process automation.

title:
Impact of Oblique Heavy Ion Irradiation on the Transient Response of Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    MODFETs
authors: Gaurav Kumar, Khushwant Sehra, Anupama Anand, Manoj Saxena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This manuscript investigates the influence of Single Event Transients triggered by heavy ion irradiation on β - (AlGa)<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MODFETs. A systematic reliability study of Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MODFETs is carried out by deliberately probing vulnerable device region using particle strikes and by localizing the effects across the Gate – Drain access regions near the quantum well through angled ion strikes. Comparative analysis of the resulting drain current transients reveals an increased sensitivity to angled heavy ion strike towards the drain electrode, leading to a pronounced increase in peak transient current due to enhanced carrier collection at the drain electrode.

title: Next-Generation Biosensing: A Review on TFET-Based Sensors with Machine Learning (ML) Integration
authors: V.Karutharaja, N.B.Balamurugan, G.Vijaya Lakshmi, K.Kokulavani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In this review, summarizes the physics and device topologies of Tunnel FET-based biosensors, dielectric-modulation techniques, Two dimensional material platforms, manufacturing challenges and performance measures. Next, we discuss the growing integration of Artificial Intelligent (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) with biosensor data streams in order to improve the processing signal, false positive, false negative rejection and to achieve rapid classification and quantification of analytes. TFETs have emerged as a strong platform for ultra-low-power, highly sensitive label-free biosensing owing to their steep subthreshold swing and band-to-band tunneling characteristics. Finally, we assess outstanding challenges and future approaches for combining sophisticated machine learning pipelines with advancements in TFET technology to accelerate translational biosensing applications.

title: Hybrid Ensemble Learning for High-Precision DDoS Attack Detection in IoT Networks Using the INDDOS24 Dataset
authors: Usman Mustafa, Minahil Kamran, Muhammad Yousaf Rafique, Alina Akhtar, Ashas Mushtaq, Umer Majeed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) resulted in several connected gadgets in the world never seen before, expected to continue to tens of billions by 2025. Although the development of IoT is efficient in smart homes, industrial automation, and healthcare, there is also an increase in the attack surface that cyber threats can attack. IoT devices are also being used more as a large-scale botnet in Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks. In recent times, there have been reports of multi-terabit DDoS attacks, novel IoT botnets (e.g., Mirai-based) with the ability to generate record-breaking volumes of traffic. Conventional signature-based systems (e.g., Snort, Cisco Firepower) have a hard time resisting such advanced attacks. In response to this, we will elaborate a hybrid machine-learning ensemble model based on new INDDOS24 data (an IoT DDoS benchmark). Our voting ensemble is soft (e.g., tuned classifiers, such as XGBoost, Random Forest, and a neural network) to obtain high detection precision. We also conduct a lot of data preprocessing and feature engineering on INDDOS24, use SHAP to have interpretability, and compare our results with the state of the art. Our model achieves more than 98% accuracy and F1-score, which is better than single-model baselines. Explanation analysis displays the important flow characteristics (e.g., the rate of packets, the duration of flows) upon which decisions are made. We comment on deployment efficiency (latency of the inference less than a second) and robustness, in comparison to the legacy IDS benchmarks, and describe their practical implication. Federated and transfer learning methods to real-time IoT security are also to be worked on in the future.

title:
Engineering of L-channel Tunnel Field Effect Transistor (L-Channel TFET) with high k material to reduce the I
                    <sub>off</sub>
                    current
authors: Sudha Rani, Ashish Raman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Proposed paper represent a novel approach modified L-channel tunnel field Effect Transistor (L-channel TFET) structure doped silicon source, which improve the subthreshold swing (SS), reduced I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf>, and the combination of dielectric material (Sio2) and the Molybdenum (Mo) create excellent effect of leakage current reduction. Using the low k-material at the drain to reduce the capacitance of gate–drain. It also improving in terms of downscaling Tunnel Field Effect Transistor.

title: A Comparative Performance Analysis of Fog-Assisted vs. Cloud-Only Architectures for a Real-Time Mood-Aware IoT System
authors: Prashanth Ragam, Harathi Chavala, Ashish Nerusu, Sai Ganesh Reddy Venna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Real-time internet of Things (IoT) applications based on continuous multimodal inputs, such as EEG and camera streams intended for mood detection, put demanding requirements in terms of latency, efficient bandwidth usage, and controlled energy consumption. Traditional Cloud-Only architectures are not suitable to meet these requirements due to the long network path and high transmission load involved. This paper presents a performance comparison between Fog-Assisted and Cloud-Only deployment models for a real-time mood-aware IoT system using iFogSim. Two scenarios are simulated by varying module placement: (1) Fog-Assisted, where computation happens on a local fog node, and (2) Cloud-Only, where processing will be offloaded completely onto the cloud. Key performance indicators-measured end-to-end latency, network usage, and total energy consumption under identical conditions-demonstrate that the Fog-Assisted model significantly reduces latency and WAN bandwidth usage and presents more efficient system-level energy consumption for continuous real-time workloads. These results highlight the suitability of fog architectures for intelligent, delay-sensitive IoT applications, and the presented work will lay the ground for future enhancements, including adaptive module placement, mobility support, and AI-driven edge inference. In comparison to the Cloud-Only model, the Fog-Assisted model demonstrated a 98% reduction in latency (4 ms vs 212 ms), an 80% reduction in network usage (10,000 units vs 50,000 units), and a 99.6% reduction in energy consumption (1.67 × 10 Ws vs 4.45 × 10 Ws). These results confirm fog computing’s suitability for intelligent and delay-sensitive applications and show its obvious superiority for real-time mood-aware IoT workloads. This work also lays the groundwork for future studies on AI-driven edge intelligence, mobility-aware processing, and adaptive module placement.

title: Analysis of Historical Speech Based on the Decision-Making Process of Political Leaders Using Natural Language Processing
authors: Varsha Mittal, Narendra Bisht, Kamola Oltiboyeva, Bakhromjan Djalalov, Madirimova Saodatjon Madirimovna, Nafisa Khudayberganova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Historical speeches of political leaders have served as among the most powerful parts in influencing the globe. Speeches of the textual form were imprinted into history. These kinds of conversations have a significant impact on the general population and their activities in the days ahead. Additionally, if allowed unsupervised, political persons or groups may generate severe issues. In numerous situations, there may be an indicator that the governing body has to adjust its regulations and simultaneously attend to the populace. Recognizing the sentimental analysis of a political statement is vital, as they can be preliminary signs or alerts for future worldwide emergencies, alliances, battles, and future wars. This study emphasized the prime ministers of the United States and India, categorizing their speeches according to their emotional content and context. In this study, optimism is a supplementary emotion, while happiness and upset are the main ones. Beyond categorizing the speeches depending on context and emotion, among the primary tasks of this study present a database of political speeches containing 2010 speeches annotated with context-based and emotion-based historical speeches. The speeches that are being focused on are quite lengthy. This study introduces EMPOLITICIAN-Context, an adaptive voting classification technique for context-based categorization, and EMPOLITICIAN-Emotion, an adaptive voting algorithm for emotion categorization of political speeches. The suggested EMPOLITICIAN-Context approach has obtained 73.2% accuracy in the area of context-based categorizing, and the EMPOLITICIAN-Emotion approach accomplished 53.1% accuracy in categorizing the emotion of the historical speeches of political leaders.

title: Unsupervised Deep Learning-based Keypoint Localization Estimating Descriptor Matching Performance
authors: David Rivas-Villar, Álvaro S. Hervella, José Rouco, Jorge Novo
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Retinal image registration, particularly for color fundus images, is a challenging yet essential task with diverse clinical applications. Existing registration methods for color fundus images typically rely on keypoints and descriptors for alignment; however, a significant limitation is their reliance on labeled data, which is particularly scarce in the medical domain. In this work, we present a novel unsupervised registration pipeline that entirely eliminates the need for labeled data. Our approach is based on the principle that locations with distinctive descriptors constitute reliable keypoints. This fully inverts the conventional state-of-the-art approach, conditioning the detector on the descriptor rather than the opposite. First, we propose an innovative descriptor learning method that operates without keypoint detection or any labels, generating descriptors for arbitrary locations in retinal images. Next, we introduce a novel, label-free keypoint detector network which works by estimating descriptor performance directly from the input image. We validate our method through a comprehensive evaluation on four hold-out datasets, demonstrating that our unsupervised descriptor outperforms state-of-the-art supervised descriptors and that our unsupervised detector significantly outperforms existing unsupervised detection methods. Finally, our full registration pipeline achieves performance comparable to the leading supervised methods, while not employing any labeled data. Additionally, the label-free nature and design of our method enable direct adaptation to other domains and modalities.

title: Frequency-Space Metasurface Inverse Design Through Huygens Scattering-Assisted Deep Learning
authors: Haoman Wang, Chao Qian, Liming Xu, Hongsheng Chen, Haogang Wang
publish: IEEE Journal on Multiscale and Multiphysics Computational Techniques
abstract: Deep learning has greatly advanced metasurface design by overcoming the slow, inefficient and experience-driven limitations of traditional numerical simulations. However, inverse design of metasurface arrays for electromagnetic scattering control in the frequency-space domain entails significantly higher degrees of freedom, which leads to increased computational complexity and prohibitive full-wave simulation costs. To address this, we integrate universal Huygens scattering into a deep learning framework to build a fast simulation surrogate. This physics-informed approach accelerates optimization while circumventing solution ambiguity. Through a two-stage inverse design strategy, multi-objective optimization is decomposed to lower computational burden and reduce inter-objective trade-offs. Our method supports the realization of arbitrary scattering profiles with high design flexibility. Experimentally, the designed metasurface achieves 7.6-29.8 dB RCS reduction at normal incidence over 3.7-18 GHz (131.8% bandwidth) and maintains over 6 dB average reduction within ±30° in two orthogonal planes. This work establishes an efficient route to inversely design broadband, wide-angle scattering-controlling metasurface arrays.

title: Augmenting Human Hazard Situational Awareness With Haptic Interface for Heterogeneous Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Yang Xing, Ziyue Wang, Xiangqi Kong, Weisi Guo, Antonios Tsourdos
abstract: As vehicle autonomy increases, human operators become more susceptible to distractions and a loss of situational awareness (SA) due to cognitive limitations. Rapidly enhancing human SA in hazardous situations is, therefore, critical for timely hazard perception and collision avoidance, particularly in human-vehicle teaming contexts that demand fast, accurate hazard reasoning. This study evaluates the efficiency of a low-cost vibrotactile interface for enhancing hazard SA of human operators when teaming with heterogeneous autonomous vehicles, including both ground and aerial autonomous vehicles. To do so, we evaluate the effectiveness of a vibrotactile interface in challenging time-critical scenarios considering adversarial attacks to better understand the cognitive constraints faced by human operators. Our quantitative analysis, based on the data collected from 39 participants, demonstrates that: first, haptic cues can significantly enhance human hazard SA across various metrics for the ground and aerial scenarios; second, perception of aerial attacks in a 3-D environment is more challenging than ground risk perception.

title: A Multiple Spatial–Frequency EM Tag for Identification Improvement
authors: Yiwei Wang, Zihao Ning, Mengmeng Li, Yonggeng Zhu, Jihong Gu, Davide Ramaccia, Alessandro Toscano, Filiberto Bilotti, Dazhi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Benefiting from the precise electromagnetic (EM) wave manipulation capability of metasurface (MTS), MTS-based EM tags exhibit significant potential for advancement in the field of radio frequency identification (RFID). This article presents the theoretical analysis, design, and experimental verification of an MTS-based tag employing spatial–frequency coding. Unlike conventional RFID tags and prior MTS-based tags, the proposed tag is an active MTS-based identification platform integrating phase modulation circuits, power amplifiers (PAs), and polarization conversion antennas. By exploiting these functionalities, the tag modulates the interrogation signal to generate tunable harmonics, whose spatial combinations encode the tag information. The backscattered response is further polarization-converted and amplified, rendering it cross-polarized with respect to environmental clutter and extending the read range. An X-band prototype is fabricated, featuring a 700-MHz bandwidth and a 10-dB enhancement in backscattered power compared to a metallic plate of the same aperture. Experiments under strong reflection and long read range conditions verify the feasibility of the proposed tag.

title: Straggler-Aware Cross-DC Distributed Training for Large-Scale Models over OTNs
authors: Meng Lian, Yongli Zhao, Xin Li, Qiaolun Zhang, Memedhe Ibrahimi, Francesco Musumeci, Jie Zhang, Massimo Tornatore
abstract: Large-scale machine learning models (LMs) demand massive computational and communication resources, motivating cross–data center (Cross-DC) distributed training over optical transport networks (OTNs). Even though OTNs provide energy-efficient and fine-grained bandwidth for gradient transmission, conventional federated learning (FL) for distributed training typically relies on a single–parameter-server (SPS) architecture. Such an SPS-based design encounters a compute bottleneck, as the limited bandwidth to access the parameter servers (PS) restricts scalability. Semi-decentralized federated learning (SD-FL) alleviates this issue by partitioning workers into multiple clusters, each communicating only with a local PS, thereby avoiding the SPS bottleneck. However, SD-FL also introduces further straggler effects due to intra- and inter cluster resource competition, which significantly degrade training efficiency (e.g., training throughput). To overcome these limitations, we formulate the SD-FL-over-OTN resource allocation problem as a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model and propose a scalable heuristic algorithm named straggler-aware parameter server assignment (SAPSA). SAPSA integrates cluster-diameter-based pruning of candidate PSs with an iterative straggler-aware optimization to achieve efficient PS placement and balanced resource allocation, thereby improving on-time task completion (task success rate). Simulations across multiple OTN topologies show that SAPSA achieves near-optimal performance (within 10% of MILP). Compared with competitive heuristic baselines, SAPSA improves task success rate by up to 7%, mitigates straggler waiting time by over 28%, and reduces runtime by 48% via pruning. These results demonstrate that SAPSA enables scalable and straggler-resilient Cross-DC federated training for LMs over OTNs.

title: Fault Detection for Quadruped Robots Based on Dynamic Decision-Making and Layered Elastic Weight Consolidation
authors: Peng Han, Xiaochen Xie, Yunjiang Lou
abstract: Quadruped robots operating in dynamic, unstructured environments demand robust fault detection to ensure safety and autonomy. Traditional model-based approaches, while interpretable, often fail to capture the complex nonlinear dynamics of legged locomotion due to unmodeled interactions and environmental uncertainties. Data-driven methods, particularly deep learning-based techniques, have shown promise in extracting fault patterns from high-dimensional sensor data. However, existing methods often struggle with complex spatio-temporal fault patterns, limited adaptability, and catastrophic forgetting when learning new faults. To address these challenges, we propose a Dynamic Spatio-Temporal Learning framework for Fault Diagnosis in Quadruped Robots, integrating Heterogeneous Graph-Augmented Spatiotemporal Convolutional Network (HG-STCN) and Deep Reinforcement Learning. The HG-STCN explicitly models the robot's topological structure for accurate spatio-temporal fault diagnosis, while a DQN dynamically optimizes computational resource allocation to balance real-time performance and reliability. In addition, Layered Elastic Weight Consolidation) enables incremental learning, allowing the system to adapt to new faults without forgetting prior knowledge. Experimental results demonstrate superior detection accuracy, robustness, and adaptability compared to baseline methods. This work advances the reliable, long-term autonomy of quadruped robots in challenging environments.

title: FGAIM: Identifying Drug-Target Activation and Inhibition Mechanisms via Inductive Graph Neural Networks Based on Fine-Grained Interaction Strategies
authors: Yi Tang, Yongxian Fan, Guicong Sun, Mengxin Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Distinguishing the activation and inhibition mechanisms between drugs and targets can reveal the potential regulatory pathways of target functions, which is crucial in drug discovery and development. Although numerous deep learning based computational methods have been proposed, most of them extract drug and target features independently while neglecting interactions between drug molecules and protein residues. In addition, existing methods approach drugs in a relatively simple way and predominantly rely on protein sequence information. Deep learning, particularly graph neural networks (GNNs), has demonstrated unique advantages in processing data with complex graph-structured relationships. Motivated by these limitations, this study proposes a novel computational computational method named FGAIM, which designed to effectively identify activation and inhibition mechanisms between drugs and targets. First, a multi-scale GNN module in FGAIM is employed to learn expressive drug molecular embeddings. At the same time, protein representations are constructed by integrating pre-trained language model (PLM) embeddings and structural information derived from 3D conformations. Subsequently, FGAIM leverages a GraphSAGE module to extract features from both the primary drug graph and the fine-grained drug-protein interaction graph. Finally, the resulting drug and target embeddings are fused and fed into a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) for classification prediction. Comparative experiments on two public datasets demon strate that FGAIM significantly outperforms existing computa tional approaches and exhibits strong generalization capabilities. Further case studies reveal the advantages of FGAIM in min ing previously unrecognized activation/inhibition relationships. Building upon the fine-grained interactions, we further explored the model's interpretability at the molecular structural level through analysis of attention weights. Additionally, visualization analyses confirm that FGAIM effectively captures underlying structural patterns in the data and identifies discriminative features across different categories.

title: Enterprise Resilience of Semiconductor Supply Chains With Critical Materials and Extreme Weather
authors: Davis C. Loose, Negin Moghadasi, Zachary A. Collier, Elvie Sellers, Igor Linkov, James H. Lambert
abstract: Supply chains of semiconductors are integral to the global digital economy though are vulnerable to disruptions. This article examines the cascading effects of disruptions to semiconductor supply chains through a discrete event simulation (DES) framework. The paper explores two scenarios: 1) Weather-induced disruptions, such as extreme weather events that affect logistics and material availability, and 2) material supply shortages coupled with natural disaster risks, such as flooding and high winds. The approach describes how weather-related disruptions affect transportation and logistics, potentially leading to increased operational costs and delays, while material shortages impact production capacities directly. By employing DES, we assess the effectiveness of several mitigation strategies, including inventory management and supplier diversification. The results highlight the importance of adaptive strategies to enhance the resilience of semiconductor supply chains. The article, thus, illuminates complexities and interdependencies of these supply chains and provides insights for assuring supply chain functionality in the face of disruptions.

title: Research on Electromagnetic Measurement and Assessment of Fast-Moving Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer System
authors: Yanling Li, Qin Zhang, Linxin Zhou, Yuxiao Fan, Shunpan Liu, Yong Li, Zhengyou He
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) systems have been widely used in electrified transportation, raising concerns about electromagnetic exposure as a key public safety issue. Existing research provides extensive guidance for quasi-static field measurements in electric vehicles. However, the DWPT systems exhibit non-uniform electromagnetic field (EMF) distributions and transient current surges, posing challenges for advancing electromagnetic assessment. This paper aims to analyze the impact of dynamic speed and segmented coils on the EMF distribution in the DWPT system. By identifying areas with significant field variations, the sizes and functions of sampling and evaluation areas are determined. By ranking all sampling points using Pearson correlation analysis, the key measured points for predicting the spatially averaged and maximum magnetic flux density (MFD) in the DWPT system are proposed. A rotating prototype of the fast-moving DWPT system is constructed, and the experimental values at key measured points are fitted to compute the spatially averaged and maximum MFD in the evaluation area. Compared with the scanning values, the maximum errors for the fitted spatially averaged and maximum MFD are 8.9% and 8.4%, respectively, validating the effectiveness of the proposed key measured points.

title: Graph Neural Network with Dual-Stream Attention for Global Maximum Power Point Tracking under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Pengcheng Hu, Qianhui Ma, Abhisek Ukil
abstract: Due to the complex nonlinear coupling between circuit topology and environmental changes, precise maximum power point tracking under partial shading conditions remains a challenge. Existing data-driven methods typically view photovoltaic (PV) arrays as unstructured feature vectors or Euclidean grids, which fail to capture the fundamental series parallel constraints that control the distribution of current and voltage. This study proposes a graph neural network with dual stream attention (GNN-DSA), which is a physical perception framework that can strictly decouple static topologies from dynamic environmental signals. Static streams encode arrays into message-passed directed graphs with specific edge types that capture series-parallel circuit constraints, while dynamic streams capture irradiation patterns through the Transformer. A novel bidirectional cross-attention mechanism explicitly simulates the interaction between circuit constraints and environmental stress. Hardware verification indicates that the tracking efficiency of GNN-DSA on real PV panels is 99.1%–99.6%, on solar array simulators is 99.99%, and the response time is less than 100ms. Comparative analysis shows that compared with the multilayer perceptron baseline, GNN-DSA reduces the mean squared error by 45.6%, and compared with the state-of-the-art Crossformer model, it reduces it by 22.3%.

title: APOFormer: Adaptive Geometric Primitive Optimization Transformer for Building Polygon Extraction from remote sensing imagery
authors: Qinglie Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The automatic generation of building polygons from remote sensing imagery has significant applications but confronts challenges due to complex urban scenes, frequent occlusions, multi-scale features, and diverse geometric morphologies. Segmentation-based methods are constrained by irregular boundaries and imprecise contours, often requiring extensive postprocessing. Geometric primitive-based approaches typically overlook geometrical primitive interdependencies and contextual semantic correlations, causing suboptimal accuracy and topological errors. To address these issues, this study proposed a novel approach, APOFormer (Adaptive Geometric Primitive Optimization Transformer), for building polygon extraction from remote sensing imagery, combining the advantages of the convolution neural network (CNN) and Transformer. APOFormer represents polygons as vertex-query-based sequences and establishes interactive mechanisms for primitive correlations using positional embedding and image context. In feature extraction, the CNN encoder incorporated learnable corner-edge detection operators to explicitly enhance building primitive features. Additionally, a dynamic vertex query mechanism and the multi-vertex collaborative deformable attention module are developed to improve position prediction accuracy and efficiency using the Transformer-based decoder. Furthermore, an edge-context-guided topology reconstruction module is constructed to enhance polygon quality. Comprehensive experimental evaluation on WHU, INRIA, and MASB datasets, validated by visualization and qualitative analyses, demonstrates the superiority of APOFormer, achieving an average of 83.79% AP, 27.73 MTA, 88.01% C-IoU, and 93.90% IoU. APOFormer achieves remarkable generalization ability and provides robust solutions for accurate building polygon generation.

title: Study on Power-Saving for Wlans Using Service-Period Interruption
authors: Shinichi Baba, Takahiro Matsumoto, Yasuaki Ohira
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Wireless local area networks (WLANs) have great potential to enable digital transformation (DX) with the Internet of Things (IoT) due to their high performance and ubiquitous deployment. High energy consumption of the WLAN module is one of the issues in applying IoT applications. Target Wake Time (TWT) is a solution developed by IEEE802.11 that delivers an innovative improvement in power-saving. But it is a solution managed by an access point (AP) and requires more computing power on the AP as the number of stations (STAs) increases. In order to overcome that problem, a novel scheme to save energy consumption at a station (STA) with TWT is proposed in this paper. The uniqueness of the scheme is station-oriented. The scheme allows the station to control its activity and energy consumption so that it does not require additional performance on the AP side. A brief performance evaluation of the scheme is shown as the results of a computer simulation. In a certain situation of video signal uploading to an IoT server, a simulation result shows a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 6. 7 \%}$</tex> reduction in energy consumption per STA from the regular TWT. That proves the potential of the proposed scheme.

title: A Quantitative Analysis and Comparison of Performance of Renewable Energy System with Battery Storage System
authors: Sivarajeswari S, Suganya R, Ramani Kannan, Niranjan T K, Avinash A, Rishikeshwaran B, Jenith Joseph A
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This research paper presents a quantitative comparative evaluation of solar photovoltaic (PV) systems in Chennai (India), wind energy systems in Sweetwater (USA), and a hybrid PV-wind system in Cornwall (UK), using a common lithium-ion battery energy storage system. The simulations use location-specific irradiance and wind profiles synthesized from real meteorological statistics to enable controlled and consistent comparison across systems. MATLAB simulations are conducted while maintaining identical load demand, battery capacity, simulation horizon, and energy management strategy for all configurations. The evaluation considers generation profiles, energy curtailment, battery state-of-charge (SOC), and the levelized cost of storage (LCOS). The results indicate that standalone PV and wind systems exhibit significant variability due to fluctuating irradiance and wind speed conditions, whereas the hybrid system demonstrates enhanced supply stability and improved demand-serving capability. The hybrid configuration achieves a load-served fraction exceeding 90%, reduced unmet energy, and more effective utilization of the battery system. Comparative LCOS analysis further demonstrates lower storage stress and improved cost effectiveness for the hybrid system, highlighting its suitability for reliable standalone energy supply under variable renewable conditions.

title: Analysis of Dynamic Response of VSG-Controlled Grid-Forming Inverter Under Fault Conditions
authors: Aminu Jibrin Aliyu, Ramani Kannan, Ho Tatt Wei, David Enemali Abah, Rabby Md Afser Jani, Nguyen Gia Minh Thao
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) has led to a growing dominance of converterinterfaced generation, resulting in reduced system inertia and altered fault dynamics. Grid-forming (GFM) inverters, particularly those employing virtual synchronous generator (VSG) control, are expected to provide grid-supportive functionalities such as voltage and frequency regulation in low-inertia environments. However, their performance under faulted conditions remains insufficiently understood, as the conventional VSG algorithm lacks explicit fault ride-through (FRT) or current-limiting coordination. This paper investigates the dynamic response of a VSG-controlled GFM inverter during normal and faulted grid conditions using detailed MATLAB/Simulink time-domain simulations. The study considers both asymmetrical and symmetrical faults to quantify the inverter's transient behavior when subjected to voltage collapse and current stress. Results show that while the inverter maintains stable operation under normal conditions, fault events trigger significant overcurrents and power oscillations, with voltage control actions conflicting with current limitations. In particular, the absence of coordinated FRT logic causes prolonged overcurrent in faulted phases and delayed post-fault recovery. These findings provide a benchmark of the inherent vulnerabilities of standard GFM control under grid disturbances and highlight the necessity for improved GFMFRT strategies to ensure converter protection and system-level resilience in future converter-dominated grids.

title: Detecting Gaps Between Guidelines and Field Practices Through Non-Image IoT Data: Insights from Experienced vs. Inexperienced Farmers
authors: Takumi Kawamura, Tetsuya Sakai, Mitsuru Kodama, Norifumi Nomura, Ryo Matsumura
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study aims to develop Internet of Things (IoT) systems tailored to mountainous and hilly regions, where cultivation guidelines and oral traditions remain essential, by detecting field management practices without increasing farmers' operational burden. Specifically, we examine the detectability of differences among three field management approaches in field wasabi cultivation in Shunan City, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan: the practices prescribed by recommended guidelines, practices employed by an experienced farmer, and practices used by inexperienced farmers. To address privacy concerns, we measured and analyzed multiple environmental parameters using a non-image-based IoT sensing system. The results indicate that statistically significant differences were observed in greenhouse air and soil temperatures; however, both experienced and inexperienced farmers performed heat-retention activities consistent with the guidelines. These findings indicate that the experienced farmer employed adaptive heat-retention strategies beyond the standardized guidelines. By enabling the extraction of tacit field management knowledge, our approach contributes to refining cultivation guidelines and supports the dissemination of more effective, context-sensitive management practices.

title: Image Processing Based Intelligent Traffic Flow Analysis
authors: Naveen Kumar, Neeraj Gautam, Minal Nagar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Accurate vehicle detection and counting in real-world traffic surveillance is challenging due to occlusions, illumination variations, identity switches, and duplicate counting caused by unstable object tracking. This paper presents a robust and real-time vehicle detection and counting framework that integrates a YOLOv8m-based object detector with a DeepSORT multi-object tracking algorithm. To improve detection reliability under adverse lighting conditions, a preprocessing pipeline combining Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization and gamma correction is employed. A dual-confidence detection strategy is introduced to retain low-confidence detections exclusively for tracker association, thereby reducing track fragmentation and identity loss. Furthermore, centroid trajectory smoothing and a minimum visibility constraint are applied to suppress spurious and short-lived tracks. A global one-time counting mechanism based on canonical track identities ensures that each vehicle is counted exactly once, even in bidirectional traffic scenarios. Experimental results on real traffic video sequences demonstrate improved counting accuracy, reduced duplicate counts, and real-time performance, making the proposed approach suitable for intelligent transportation and traffic monitoring applications.

title: Digital Twin Channel-Based mmWave Spatial Propagation Characteristics Prediction in 6G Multi-Band Communication Systems
authors: Zhen Zhang, Jianhua Zhang, Li Yu, Silu Xing, Kai Sun
abstract: The digital twin channel (DTC) aims to map the physical scene into a real-time digital twin (DT) and enables artificial intelligence (AI)-driven channel prediction. To meet diverse channel acquisition requirements in mmWave systems, we propose TwinPAS, a DT-based hierarchical power angular spectrum (PAS) prediction method for multi-band coexistence communications. TwinPAS enables the base station to convert visual sensing information into spatial channel characteristics through two stages: DT construction and PAS prediction. Firstly, in the DT construction, static scene information is obtained through computer vision techniques, while dynamic environmental features are derived from the estimation of sub-6 GHz channel parameters. To fuse the two heterogeneous features to construct the DT of the physical scene, a novel channel representation method, called channel embedding, is presented. Then, in the PAS prediction, a hierarchical channel prediction network is developed to predict the mmWave PAS in descending order of path powers. For the network output, the embedded mmWave PAS is projected into the virtual distance matrix, thereby solving the problem of uncertain channel parameter dimensions that arises when the number of multipaths changes dynamically. Simulation results demonstrate the superiority and generalization of the TwinPAS approach for PAS prediction, showing its potential for physical layer applications, such as beam selection.

title: Cognitive and Fuzzy Models to Understand Learning Behaviors: AI for Enhanced Learning Pedagogy and Decision Making
authors: Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Srijana Karnatak, Kabulov Kosimboy, Teshaboyev Dilmurod, Rakhmatov Avazbek, Mahliyo Khaydarova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The reason why it is important to know the behavior of the learners is so that adaptive and efficient learning systems will be developed. The conventional learning systems are largely learning platforms that are founded on the outcome measures and have no way of modeling the mental states and uncertainty that prevail in human learning. The current paper suggests the use of the framework steered by artificial intelligence which would integrate cognitive modeling and fuzzy logic to understand the learning behaviors and assist the pedagogical decision making. The cognitive models, that involve the knowledge acquisition, engagement, and the psychological burden of learners and the fuzzy models that address the ambiguity in behavioral and emotional indicators by relying on linguistic reasoning. The proposed system to be discussed involves a fuzzy inference-based mechanism of cognitive states to adaptive instructional decisions viz. content personalization, modification of feedback and intervention strategies. The use of experimental evaluation has demonstrated higher predictability of learner performance, high levels of engagement and decision making, as opposed to the traditional rule-based and machine learning methods. These findings justify the appropriateness of using the combination of cognitive and fuzzy models in improving the pedagogy of learning in the intelligent educational system.

title: Advanced Ensemble Methods for Air Quality Prediction: A Comparative Study with Hybrid Physics-Guided Neural Networks
authors: Kothandaraman D, J Bhavana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The fast development of nations leads to severe public health problems and disease because of deteriorating air quality which makes India one of the countries with the worst pollution worldwide. The research introduces two new ensemble systems which predict Air Quality Index (AQI) through (1) a Weighted Multi-Model Ensemble that uses learned optimal fusion to combine base models XGBoost 65.7% and Gradient Boosting 30.5% for R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9793 and (2) a Hybrid RF-AirPhyNet framework which uses Random Forest statistical power to achieve R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9829 through physics-based residual correction. The research uses seven baseline models which include machine learning approaches (Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Gradient Boosting) and deep learning models (LSTM, GRU, Bi-LSTM) and physics-based neural networks (AirPhyNet) with data from 43 Indian cities across multiple years (2015-2020, 29,531 observations). The experimental data shows that our Weighted Ensemble method delivers 10 times faster training speed than deep learning methods while achieving better results and our Hybrid model produces the highest overall performance through its combination of statistical learning and atmospheric physics knowledge. The feature importance analysis results demonstrate that the engineered temporal features (AQI rolling mean: 86.1%) serve as the primary factors which enable prediction success because ensemble methods with feature engineering outperform deep learning models when analyzing environmental data tables. The research project developed fundamental operational standards which AQI forecasting systems must meet to function properly.

title: Transistor-Level Optimization and Performance Analysis of a CMOS Op-Amp-Based Comparator for Biomedical Signals
authors: Mansi Jain, P. P. Bansod, Rajesh Khatri, Disha Sharma, Mohit Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In this article, we introduce the design, simulation, and layout implementation of a two-stage CMOS operational amplifier employed as a comparator, using SCL 180 nm technology, targeting low-frequency biomedical applications. The design approach emphasizes transistor-level width and length optimization, aiming for an optimal balance between gain, stability, and power efficiency, keeping simplicity in design structure. The proposed design offers an impressive gain of 100 dB, ensuring proper detection of low-amplitude differential signals, and boasts an exceptional phase margin of 179°, verifying extremely stable operation, irrespective of oscillation and noise effects. Transient analysis verifies proper operation of the proposed comparator design, while a mere measured peak transient power of 1.955 µW verifies its compatibility for implementation in power-restricted biomedical applications. The proposed design has been implemented at the layout level employing standard CMOS layout design principles, verifying DRC and LVS checks, ensuring proper correlation of the layout layout and technology consistency. The results obtained verify the efficacy of our proposed approach, and it proves that our proposed design qualifies as an apt, stable, and energy-efficient candidate for biomedical analog front-end circuits and process for evaluation.

title: Reliability of GaN - based MISHEMTs incorporating a Buffer - Free Architecture post High Intensity UV Radiation
authors: Priya Kumari, Anupama Anand, Khushwant Sehra, Manoj Saxena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This manuscript presents a reliability study of the GaN based MISHEMTs realized on the Buffer – Free architecture. The impact of SiNx insulation thickness (t<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">insulation</inf>) has been investigated on the electrical characteristics of the MISHEMTS pre - and post - high intensity UV exposure. Comparisons demonstrate a negligible change in DC and RF parameters of the DUT, evincing the superior capabilities of the Buffer – Free MISHEMT. The AlN nucleation layer, which functions as a back-barrier to prevent the production of electron hole pairs (EHPs) after UV exposure, could be the cause of this.

title: Content-Based Image Retrieval for Fine-Grained Aircraft Using Deep Learning and Cosine Similarity
authors: Rukhsana Parveen, Afsana Parveen, Bhavya Alankar, Faraz Doja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Traditional keyword-based retrieval is based on textual metadata, making keyword processing time-consuming, biased, and manual. With the increase in digital images, this system becomes ineffective. To solve this, Content–Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) offers a computerized and more accurate alternative. In this study, we focus on fine-grained classification that distinguishes subcategories within a broader category, such as Boeing 777 and Boeing 737. This is crucial for understanding subtle visual differences. Traditional image search methods for finding specific aircraft in large databases depend on keywords and metadata, which can be inconsistent, incomplete, or misleading. For example, one user might label an aircraft as a twin-engine jet, while another describes it as a commercial aircraft. If the database does not consist of exact terminology or expressions, the system may fail to retrieve visually similar matches. These limitations indicate that existing image retrieval systems are limited in analyzing the actual visual content of images. CBIR replaces keywords with visual search, providing more accurate results—especially when dealing with complex or fine-grained. Our approach involves feature extraction from VGG16’s GAP layer, combined with XGBoost and Cosine similarity, applied to the FGVC-Aircraft dataset, which contains 10,000 images across 100 classes. The model achieves an accuracy of 90.1%, with a Macro-F1 score of 83%, and the top retrieval results show strong visual consistency across aircraft models. Applications include airport security and surveillance, defense and military, the aviation industry, public safety, and commercial sectors. Future improvements could involve feature fusion and advanced similarity metrics.

title: Smart Hiring Platform: An AI-based Resume Analysis and Candidate Matching System
authors: Selva Sheela K, Lokeshwar L, Teja Swaroop N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Hiring via the internet has now become extremely quick and it is becoming difficult to choose the right people, identify any counterfeit content and make the process fair. The majority of Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) are still only searching for keywords and basic rules, therefore, lacking context, latent skills, and emerging job requirements. This paper is all about an AI-based Smart Hiring Platform which OCRs resumes, employs a mixed NLP architecture, fancy semantic embeddings, and scores a candidate using a composite Smart Matching system. Transformer embeddings and similarity checks accomplish all the skill digging, and even the AI is capable of grading role-specific tests with just the right amount of difficulty. We tested the real and the fake resumes and find it to be more accurate, more relevant suggestions, and a significant reduction in the time spent in screening as compared to the old ATS and the National keyword systems. The entire process achieves an approximate accuracy of 91 per cent and can be supported by the maxed out resumes attempting to cheat the system with keyword cramming or other false gimmicks. So system works based on the Combining semantic insights with a solid scoring rubric provides a scalably fair and data-driven method of managing contemporary hiring.

title: Statistical Analysis of Electromagnetic Field Radiated by Printed Circuit Board Inside the Perforated Shielding Enclosure in Time-Domain
authors: Yuan Zhao, Jing Li, Huapeng Zhao, Jiqian Wang, Chenyang He, Guohong Du
abstract: Within a shielding enclosure with apertures, the electromagnetic field radiated by the built-in printed circuit board (PCB) will form a complex internal electromagnetic environment (EME), which potentially impacts the operational performance of the PCB itself and the others. In this article, the electromagnetic compatibility issue is studied through statistical method in time-domain. First, a near-field scanning system was developed to measure the radiated electromagnetic field, which was compared with simulated results to verify the consistency with each other. Second, the statistical characteristics of the internal EME was analyzed based on kernel density estimation and statistic parameter estimation to identify its statistical properties under three distinct scenarios: PCB is excited by different signals; PCB is located at different positions inside the enclosure; and different observation time. It was found that under the aforementioned different conditions, the time-domain magnetic field inside the perforated shielding enclosure exhibits similar statistical characteristics when the PCB is positioned at the same location. These characteristics are independent of the excitation signal and time, and all approximately follow a lognormal distribution.

title: Announcing the Shaw Prize in Computer Science

title: Complex-Valued 2-D Residual ADMM-Net for Robust Super-Resolution ISAR Imaging
authors: Suijun Zhong, Quan Huang, Junxu Wang, Lei Zhang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Deep unfolding networks based on the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) have demonstrated significant potential in super-resolution inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) imaging. However, in dynamic sensing scenarios characterized by varying sparse apertures and low signal-to-noise ratios, existing methods often suffer from limited adaptability and insufficient feature representation. To address these challenges, this letter proposes a complex-valued 2-D residual ADMM network (CV-2D-Res-ADMM-Net) for robust super-resolution ISAR imaging. Distinct from conventional implementations that rely on hand-crafted shrinkage operators, we design a learnable complex-valued 2-D residual module to implicitly learn the proximal mapping, thereby improving the reconstruction accuracy of complex structural details. Furthermore, a multisampling augmentation strategy is introduced during training to enhance the network’s generalization capability against diverse sparse sampling conditions. Experiments on measured data demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior imaging performance and robustness compared to existing ADMM-based methods.

title: Non-volatile and Volatile Memristors for Hybrid Logic-in-Memory Computing and Analog Neuromorphic Computing
authors: Tong Li, Qingxin Chen, Zhentao Yue, Jialin Meng, Tianyu Wang
abstract: This study proposes an in-memory computing circuit for non-volatile and volatile memristors based on respective models and designs related to digital logic and analog neural network circuits. Compared to traditional CMOS circuits, the hybrid circuit requires fewer components and lower power consumption. The logic circuit based on non-volatile memristors achieves stable logic functions, including AND and OR. The volatile memristors realize four key characteristics of biological neurons for neuromorphic computing. A sound localization circuit was developed by simulating the binaural time difference of the human ear, enabling azimuth recognition. These results show great application potential in digital computing and brain-inspired neuromorphic computing.

title: Securing Medical AI: Adversarial Attack Detection and Mitigation in CAD Systems
authors: Nirmal Joseph, Sudhish N George, Ameer P.M, Kiran Raja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Frequency-constrained adversarial attacks introduce imperceptible, high-frequency distortions that undermine the reliability and safety of AI-assisted medical diagnostics, posing a serious challenge to trust and resilience in socially-interactive, human-AI healthcare systems. To address this, we introduce Sentinel, a pioneering framework that detects, classifies, and mitigates adversarial attacks in medical image analysis without requiring training or model specificity. Sentinel integrates a lightweight detection module that uses a confidence scorer to analyze predictions from multiple low-rank approximations of the input image, enabling precise classification of attacks as either gradient-based or frequency-constrained. Uniquely, Sentinel includes a recovery module specifically designed for frequency-constrained attacks, employing adaptive Robust Principal Component Analysis-based denoising to suppress perturbations concentrated in smaller singular values while preserving essential image structure. This allows for effective image reconstruction, an area overlooked by existing methods. Evaluated across four diverse medical imaging datasets, Sentinel demonstrates consistently high detection accuracy, classification performance, and adversarial recovery without the need for model retraining or GPU support, making it a highly practical and scalable solution for integration into clinical AI systems. Additional explainability analyses confirm the reliability and interpretability of Sentinel's predictions, establishing it as a significant advancement in adversarial defense.

title: Ameliorated Cyber Security for Power Electronic Converters Serving Asynchronous Hydro Generating Systems
authors: Rupesh Kumari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: In recent years, the threat of cyber-attacks on industrial process controllers and power electronics converters (PECs) has highlighted the need for enhanced research into the control systems of hydropower plants. These systems have garnered significant attention due to their critical role in energy infrastructure. The IEC Standard 62433, Industrial Automation and Control System Security, provides key recommendations to address these concerns. With PECs now being network-capable, they hold significant potential to integrate into the Internet of Energy (IoE). However, this connectivity also increases their vulnerability to cybersecurity risks. This study proposes a parallel controller strategy (PCS) to protect PECs in hydropower plants from cyber-attacks. The PCS combines a digital and an analog controller to manage large variable hydropower systems effectively. A 250 MW hydroelectric generating unit serves as the basis for this research. The simulation results are analyzed to assess the system's performance against various cyber-attacks across different operating modes of the machine. The simulation and experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed technique.

title: Utilizing In-Orbit CYGNSS Roll Maneuvers For Improved Level-1 Calibration - Part II: Application To Reflected Signal Channels
authors: Mohammad M. Al-Khaldi, Scott Gleason, Christopher Ruf, Joel T. Johnson, Darren S. McKague, Anthony Russel, William L. Wells, Tianlin Wang, Dorina Twigg, Patrick McEnaney, Joshua Synowiec
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: This work develops a methodology for using commanded roll maneuvers by Cyclone Global Navigation Satellite System (CYGNSS) constellation observatories to improve the determination of the nadir antenna patterns that receive GNSS signals reflected from Earth’s surface. The method relies on observation geometry properties in differing roll states that are used as a diagnostic tool to reveal Level-1 calibration errors associated with prelaunch mis-characterization of the nadir antenna patterns and as numerical constraints on algorithms to derive improved patterns that are more representative of in-orbit gain levels. It is shown that the updated gain estimates differ by -3 to 3 dB in a non-uniform fashion. In the companion work (Part III), the effects the use of these updated patterns has in terms of advancing the consistency of the calibration quality of observables is explored. Initial assessments presented here highlight how observable anomalies attributable to inconsistencies in the characterization of in-orbit nadir may be reduced by over 50% using the proposed methodologies.

title: Nonlinear Model Predictive Wide-Area Frequency Control for Inverter-Dominated Systems with IEC 61850-Based Communication Validation
authors: Manuel Bravo-López, Alejandro Garcés-Ruiz, Walter Gil-González, Juan S. Gómez, Catalina González-Castaño, Carlos Restrepo
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources in modern power systems has significantly reduced system inertia, posing major challenges for frequency regulation. While advanced control strategies have been extensively studied, most existing approaches rely on simplified models, linearized formulations, or purely numerical simulations, limiting their practical applicability in real power system environments. This paper presents a real-time wide-area nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC) strategy capable of enhancing inertial, primary, and secondary frequency performance in inverter-dominated systems. To ensure practical viability, the proposed wide-area NMPC framework is validated through an IEC 61850-compliant communication architecture, facilitating interoperability with digital substations and SCADA systems. The proposed strategy is validated through real-time hardware-in-the-loop implementation of a system fully dominated by grid-forming inverters. Experimental results demonstrate that the controller can be executed within real-time constraints and significantly improves transient frequency performance and robustness under severe disturbances. These results confirm the practical feasibility of the proposed control for future low-inertia power systems.
