authors: N/A

title: Towards Collaborative Multi-modal Information Perception: A Hierarchical Feature Alignment Network and Acoustic-Optical Dataset for Underwater Object Detection
authors: Jing Chen, Junjie Xie, Mingxin Liu, Hao Wang, Cong Lin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In the realm of Earth observation, underwater target detection serves as a critical component for characterizing aquatic environments. However, existing acoustic-optical collaborative methods face persistent challenges, including the high calibration difficulty across different modalities and unsatisfactory feature alignment. A core difficulty lies in enabling the network to adaptively capture intrinsic dependencies and achieve efficient fusion of multimodal information during the learning process. To address this, this paper proposes an object detection method with adaptive cross-modal feature alignment. Specifically, the method establishes automatic alignment relationships between sonar and optical images through fine-grained and instance level feature fusion. Furthermore, a novel fusion strategy is designed to enable efficient acoustic-optical information fusion via same-layer feature learning. Finally, to mitigate the issues of scarcity and low quality of multimodal underwater datasets, a data acquisition platform is constructed, and a strictly spatiotemporally synchronized sonar-optical dataset is built. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method simultaneously enhances detection performance on both sonar and optical images. On the created dataset, the proposed method achieves mAP@0.5 scores of 86.9% and 94.4% for acoustic optical collaborative detection, thereby outperforming main stream approaches. In addition, the robustness and generalization of the proposed method are verified in other multimodal object detection scenarios. The proposed AOMU dataset can be found at https://github.com/LaplaceXing/GDOU/tree/master.

title: Online Intrusion Detection for Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems Based on Self-Supervised Predictive Model
authors: Sardar Shan Ali Naqvi, Chunjie Zhou, Peihang Xu, Yahui Li, Muhammad Uzair
abstract: Industrial cyber-physical systems (CPS) demand a reliable intrusion detection despite evolving operational conditions and a lack of labeled attack data. This article proposes a novel context–target predictive self-supervised framework for online intrusion detection in industrial CPS. The models normal behavior by predicting future sensor states from past context, allowing detection of anomalies as large prediction errors. To address concept drift, we incorporate a trust-aware online adaptation mechanism and a robust, statistics-based thresholding strategy that adjusts in real time. We validate the method on a fluid catalytic cracking unit simulation, where it achieves high precision and recall under fully online, unlabeled conditions, outperforming baseline models. The proposed framework provides an adaptive and lightweight intrusion detection systems suitable for autonomous industrial CPS monitoring without human intervention or costly labeling.

title: A Quantum-Assisted Boltzmann Machine for Wind Power Probabilistic Learning
authors: Chengjun Liu, Yijun Xu, Kai Wen, Wei Gu, Shuai Lu, Lamine Mili
abstract: Accurate probabilistic modeling of renewable power is essential for the secure operation and control of modern power systems. However, learning and modeling the data structure with high accuracy remains challenging due to the inherent spatial-temporal correlations of renewables. To address this issue, we propose a novel quantum-assisted Gaussian-Bernoulli restricted Boltzmann machine (GBRBM), which is intrinsically suitable for continuous wind power modeling. Furthermore, we tailor its negative phase to be directly solvable by quantum computing, thereby accelerating the learning process. Experiments conducted on real quantum hardware validate the cost-effective performance of the proposed method.

title: Review for "Descriptor: LYNRED Mobility Dataset Multimodal Detection Subset (LYNRED-MDS)"
authors: N/A

title: An Integrated Approach for Early Detection of Autism Spectrum Disorder using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Frameworks
authors: Rajeshwar Sharma, Harjit Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a major global health issue that calls for prompt and precise diagnostic techniques. This thorough analysis looks at machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques for early ASD detection using a variety of data modalities, such as genetic profiles, electroencephalography (EEG), facial imagery, questionnaire-based screening data, and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). We systematically assess deep neural network architectures (Convolutional Neural Networks, Recurrent Neural Networks, Autoencoders), sophisticated ensemble techniques, and conventional machine learning algorithms (Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, k-Nearest Neighbors, Logistic Regression), with a focus on hybrid frameworks combining federated learning, active learning, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI). While ensemble methods offer balanced precision-recall tradeoffs, deep learning approaches achieve superior accuracy (95–99%), according to performance metrics across multiple datasets. Important results show that while SHAP-based explainability mechanisms improve clinical interpretability, federated learning architectures successfully handle privacy concerns in healthcare data integration. Critical research gaps are identified by this review, including the need for clinician-in-the-loop validation frameworks, dataset heterogeneity, demographic representation, and the viability of real-world deployment.

title: A Reconfigurable Multi-Mode Carry Propagation ALU with Runtime Power–Performance Adaptation
authors: Shubham Patel, Mamta Khosla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper describes a Multi-Mode Carry Propagation Arithmetic Logic Unit (MCCPA-ALU) that can be reconfigurable and has been shown that it has runtime power-performance adaption capability. The design incorporates 3 adder modes namely, Ripple-Carry Adder (RCA), Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) and Carry-Select Adder (CSLA) and chooses the most appropriate configuration depending on the operational needs. This allows it to operate with low power in cases where energy is limited and also allows it to compute with high speed in cases where its performance is imperative. The proposed ALU is coded on a Xilinx Zynq-7000 FPGA with the help of Verilog HDL. It has been shown by experiment that it can reduce dynamic power by up to 48 percent in low-power mode and delay in high-performance mode by 32 percent, using a smaller total number of LUTs (less than 110). These attributes render the MCCPA-ALU an ideal choice as the embedded and IoT systems with dynamic computational efficiency.

title: Exploring the Competences of Blockchain Technology and Machine Learning in Healthcare
authors: Pratik Suresh Deshmukh, Kalyani H. Deshmukh, Amit K. Gaikwad, Gajendra R. Bamnote, Kartik P. Deshmukh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: As cryptocurrencies developed block chain technology, ongoing research has been conducted to broaden its applicability to non-financial use cases. Block chain is expected to have a significant impact on the healthcare business. The research in this subject is young but quickly expanding, therefore health informatics investigators and practitioners are constantly pushed to keep up. This report provides a full overview of existing research on the usage of blockchain technology in the field of healthcare technology. The improvement in it encourages the early diagnosis of illnesses since early detection decreases the impact of diseases to a larger degree. The ease of data availability provides a wealth of information to the clinician, and thanks to recent breakthroughs, illnesses are recognized automatically using classifiers based on medical data. Data storage in IoT devices is very susceptible to assaults or unauthorized access, which may be fatal, and is prevented by using a block chain network, which stores, authorizes, and authenticates data. The illnesses are categorized using a deep CNN classifier that has been efficiently adjusted via optimization, and the performance is measured using the metrics accuracy, precision, and recall. This study also seeks to use deep learning to identify between healthy and PD patients in order to automate the PD diagnostic procedure.

title: Role of IoT & Emerging Technologies in Small Scale Dairy & Cottage Industries
authors: Ishaan Agarwal, Vanshika Mittal, Sanyam Agarwal, Vipin Mittal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The rapid integration of Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and blockchain technologies is reshaping traditional rural industries by improving efficiency, productivity, and sustainability. This paper reviews the role of these emerging technologies in strengthening small-scale dairy and cottage industries, with a focus on rural India. Key applications include precision agriculture, intelligent irrigation, livestock health monitoring, automated dairy management, and blockchain-enabled supply chain traceability. By analyzing recent developments, the paper highlights how IoT sensor networks, machine learning models, and cloud-based platforms can optimize resource utilization, enhance animal welfare, reduce post-harvest losses, and enable direct market access for farmers. Challenges such as limited connectivity, high implementation costs, and resistance to digital adoption are also discussed. The study concludes by proposing feasible strategies for integrating modular, low-cost digital solutions into rural ecosystems, enabling sustainable growth while preserving traditional practices.

title: Tilted Beam Endfire Planar Patch Antenna for UHF RFID Application
authors: Vineet Kumar, Nitesh Kahsyap, Ashish Raman, Ajay Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This study presents a very little tiny antenna that is used for UHF-RFID applications. This antenna is used to propagate signal into a single direction means this antenna is a unidirectional and Endfire antenna with tilted beam make it more attractive to scan RFID tags from portable and handheld scanners. This is a very compact antenna, and the size of the antenna is 25 x 18 x 1.6 mm on FR4 substrate for this antenna is easily placed in compact devices. Proposed antenna designed by computer simulation tool (CST). Antenna works from 820 MHz to 984 MHz, and the bandwidth of this antenna is 164 MHz, and it covers the wide bandwidth for UHF RFID band applications. This antenna is used for tracking products in warehouse, monitoring medical equipment and handheld handling payments in a retail store

title: An Optimized Strassens–Karatsuba Matrix Multiplier Employing CLA using Verilog
authors: Shreya Swaraj, Sudhanshu Kumar, Kundan Kumar, Shruti Priya, Amit Kumar Rana, Ravi Ranjan, Shankar Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Efficient matrix multiplication is a crucial issue of AI, signal processing, and computing systems. This paper proposes an optimized matrix multiplication architecture, which incorporates Strassen’s algorithm and Karatsuba multipliers with carry look-ahead adders (CLA). The aim of the design is to minimize the hardware complexity and maximize the computational speed. At the integer level, Karatsuba multiplication is area and power efficient compared to conventional array multiplication and CLA based addition minimizes carry propagation delay. These optimized multipliers are implemented in a Strassen’s-based framework for matrix multiplication. Synthesis results on a Xilinx 7 series FPGA show that there are significant reductions in LUT utilization and power consumption compared to the traditional matrix multiplication along with improved critical path delay. The proposed Strassen’s-Karatsuba-CLA architecture offers an effective compromise between area efficiency and fast speed performance, and hence is suitable for FPGA-based matrix processing applications

title: An AI-Driven based Hypertension Disease Prediction Model using ML
authors: Kothandaraman D, J Bhavana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The worldwide health crisis of hypertension exists because this condition develops without any warning symptoms which results in dangerous complications that include myocardial infarction and stroke and renal failure. The research introduces Predictive Pulse as an artificial intelligence system which uses machine learning to identify hypertension at its beginning stage and monitor disease progression. The system uses real-time physiological signals from wearable devices which track heart rate and activity level and sleep duration to predict blood pressure fluctuations. The evaluation process included five machine learning models which were Logistic Regression and Random Forest and K-Nearest Neighbors KNN and Gradient Boosting and Support Vector Machine SVM. The SVM model achieved its highest accuracy of 99.9% through hyperparameter optimization performed by researchers. The trained model became available as a Flask-based web application, which allowed both people and medical staff to access it easily. The proposed platform demonstrates how AI-based solutions improve preventive healthcare through its automated data-based hypertension monitoring system which operates continuously.

title: Design and Analysis of 5-Stage Ring Oscillator using Nanosheet FET
authors: Sachin Kumar, Shreya Swaraj, Kundan Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper has designed and performance-analyzed a 5-stage ring oscillator using Nanosheet Field Effect Transistor technologies. The potential for NSFET to be a promising growth alternative to FinFET in future CMOS scaling with better gate control and reduced leakage and increased drive current make the study significant. The design here is on a 5-stage ring oscillator focusing on investigating certain key parameters like oscillation frequency, delay, and power efficiency. Simulation results show that the NSFET designs achieved better operational speed and lower power dissipation than conventional transistor technologies. The results prove that nanosheet FETs will remarkably improve the performance of circuits and therefore become a good candidate for next-generation semiconductor devices in energy-efficient high-speed VLSI applications.

title: Design and Simulation of a Pulse-Driven 1-Bit SRAM Cell Using CMOS Technology
authors: Thrishala M, Panwala Fenil Chetankumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a pulse-driven 1-bit SRAM subsystem using Cadence Virtuoso in CMOS technology. The proposed design integrates a ring oscillator-based clock pulse generator, a 6T SRAM cell, and a differential sense amplifier. The narrow clock pulse generated by the oscillator precisely defines the timing window for read and write operations, ensuring that these actions occur only within the intended time frame. This approach minimizes unnecessary switching activity, thereby reducing dynamic power dissipation and improving control accuracy. The read and write circuits are activated by separate pulses derived from the oscillator output, providing better synchronization and noise immunity. The sense amplifier ensures reliable data sensing by amplifying small voltage differences between the bit-lines, enabling high-speed and stable operation. Simulation results validate the functional correctness of the complete subsystem, demonstrating accurate read/write cycles and stable operation even under short pulse widths. The proposed pulse-driven control method enhances the overall timing efficiency of the SRAM cell while maintaining low power consumption. This architecture not only simplifies timing control but also offers scalability for high-speed, energy-efficient memory design. The proposed design methodology provides valuable insight into implementing compact, pulse-based memory architectures and can be further extended to multi-bit SRAM arrays or integrated into larger low-power memory systems suitable for embedded and VLSI applications.

title: Artificial Intelligence Fuzzy Approaches in History Education: A Detailed Analysis of Digital Archives, Decision Making System and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Niharika Varshney, Jaspreet Singh, Shakhboz Meylikulov, Muyassar Kholova, Turdishov Dauletmurat Xojamuratovich, Gulomjon Bakhtiyarovich Sapaev
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper will be an empirical examination of artificial intelligent fuzzy solutions in history education based on using machine learning, deep learning, and processing the digital archive methodology. The study suggests a cohesive framework that combines fuzzy inferences systems with ML and DL algorithms to overcome uncertainty in the historical data, automate the process of classifying the archives, and improve decision-support systems in the learning process of students. A large dataset of textual documents, digitized images, and student performance indicators were assessed with the help of such models as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, CNNs, BiLSTM, and Transformer designs. Also, Mamdani, Sugeno and neuro-fuzzy models of fuzzy logic were evaluated based on interpretability and ambiguity. The findings indicate that hybrid Fuzzy-DL models have a better level of accuracy with the transparency needed in an educational setup. The results show a great enhancement in the personalized learning recommendation, metadata quality prediction, and the ability to retrieve an archive. Altogether, this paper shows that the integration of AI-based fuzzy systems can be used to transform history education and enhance the data-driven pedagogical decision-making.

title: Crop-Weed Detection under Indian Agricultural Conditions using YOLO-based Models
authors: Kothandaraman D, Yaswanth Agraharapu, S Teja Venkata Naga Sri Sai, Kasarapu Rohan, Kappala Siddharth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In India farmers face challenges identifying the presence of weeds in their crop fields. The traditional method of doing this by hand requires significant labour and time, ultimately causing errors. Untreated weeds in a farm will reduce a farmer’s yield by 20-30 per cent each season. Traditionally, farmers use what they have learned to identify where to find weeds on their fields, however, the variability of the different types of soils, light intensity and crop density affect a farmer’s ability to apply their traditional weed-finding methods effectively so they will not help any farmer improve their farming effectiveness. We evaluated some of the most commonly available YOLO models to determine which model works best to identify weeds in real-time. The models were trained using a dataset that consisted of 1300 images of commonly-grown crops in India, including rice and wheat, and the most commonly-found weeds. Images were collected from various climatic conditions of the Indian monsoon season and in a dry soil environment. Minor adjustments were made to improve the outcome. I experimented with all of the adjustments in the YOLO season for all of the models to achieve the best outcome.

title: Spatio-Temporal Node Localization in Wireless Sensor Networks Using Hybrid Recurrent-Graph Neural Network
authors: Adhitiya Sharma, Abhishek S. Shamkuwar, Uddeshay Anand, Rohit Kumar, Naveen Kumar, Manjeet Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In wireless sensor networks (WSNs), thousands of sensor nodes may be distributed across wide geographic regions. Generally, a limited number of nodes possess localization hardware. This creates a major barrier to achieving accurate network-wide node positioning. In this work, A hybrid deep learning approach is introduced, combining Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to estimate the positions of both static and mobile nodes. The architecture integrates Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) and Graph Attention Networks (GATs) with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) models to capture spatial relationships and temporal patterns simultaneously. A custom method is also developed to generate realistic simulation data for model training and validation. Among the tested combinations—GCN-LSTM, GCN-GRU, GAT-LSTM, and GAT-GRU—the GAT-GRU configuration delivers the highest accuracy, achieving a minimum Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.1459 and a peak Pearson Correlation Coefficient (PCC) of 0.9958 under clean conditions with 10% mobility. This setup also remains stable under both Line-of-Sight (LOS) and Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) high-noise environments. The findings highlight the advantage of integrating attention-based spatial learning with recurrent temporal modeling for precise and scalable node localization in dynamic wireless systems.

title: Game Coaching System Using 3D CNN and LLM
authors: Ryogo Akie, Yunhao Tu, Kazuyuki Murase, Fady Alnajjar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: As the e-sports market has continued to expand in recent years, replay analysis has become a common method for players seeking to improve their skills. Nevertheless, novice and intermediate players often struggle to independently identify and articulate areas for improvement. This barrier to skill acquisition is a potential cause of player churn, preventing new players from fully experiencing the core appeal of a game. This paper presents an “AI coaching system” that integrates a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (3D CNN) with a Large Language Model (LLM). The proposed system first utilizes the 3D CNN to extract candidate moments for improvement from gameplay footage. An LLM then performs a secondary evaluation and generates a brief summary for each candidate. Following a user confirmation step via a web application, the LLM produces the final, detailed coaching feedback. To validate the proposed approach, we constructed a custom gameplay video dataset for training and testing purposes. Experimental results show that the developed CNN model achieved a balanced accuracy of approximately 71% on the test set. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of a novel approach that automates gameplay analysis and feedback generation, thereby enhancing player skill development and potentially improving player retention.

title: Sequential Industrial Defect Image Generation via DDPM and Real Defect Imitation
authors: Pei-Yu Huang, Ya-Hui Tsai, Bo Hsiao, Chuen-Tsai Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study proposes a sequential industrial defect image generation method based on Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM), real defect imitation, and prompt control. The system allows users to input a base image, manually define defect regions, and provide real defect samples with corresponding labels as texture and structure references. In addition to prompt-guided control over defect color and orientation, the system can automatically extract and apply real defect features for adaptive synthesis. A novel sequential defect generation mechanism is introduced, enabling each newly generated defect to be conditionally influenced by the previous one, thereby simulating realistic defect propagation or correlated formation patterns in industrial materials. The DDPM-based framework preserves the original background details while seamlessly embedding high-fidelity and diverse defects into specified regions. The proposed method supports batch generation of multiple defect images, significantly enhancing dataset diversity and improving model robustness in industrial defect detection tasks.

title: Micro-Batch Harvesting: Path Planning for Tomato Robots Using Safe-Cylinder Strategy and PC-CVRP
authors: Kazuhisa Nakasho, Hirokazu Madokoro, Stephanie Nix
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Labor shortages in agriculture have become a critical global issue, necessitating the rapid development of autonomous harvesting robots for soft fruits like tomatoes. However, optimal path planning in complex environments cluttered with stems and leaves remains computationally expensive, hindering practical deployment. Furthermore, to balance fruit quality preservation with time efficiency, it is essential to consider capacity constraints for holding a specific amount of fruit near the end-effector for batched transport. This paper proposes a high-efficiency and safe path planning framework for tomato harvesting robots. First, to bypass complex collision detection, we define a geometric “Safe-Cylinder” and introduce a strategy that ensures physical collision avoidance via rectangular trajectories. Second, we formulate the harvesting task as a Precedence and Capacity-Constrained Vehicle Routing Problem (PC-CVRP) using a “Depot Cloning” approach and derive an optimized sequence minimizing total travel cost using a mathematical optimization solver. Path simulations demonstrate that the proposed method generates batched harvesting routes that avoid physical contact with obstacles while significantly reducing travel costs compared to a simple greedy algorithm.

title: Hybrid and Self-Sustainable RIS for Mobile Communications: A Location-Aware Energy Scheduling and DRL-Based Optimization
authors: Junqi Liu, Yi Zhou, Gaofei Huang, Dong Tang
abstract: This work explores a self-sustainable reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted mobile communication system, where a RIS powered by the energy harvested from ambient energy sources assists the communication between a transmitter and a mobile user. Unlike the conventional passive or active RISs in existing studies, we employ a hybrid RIS which is enabled to operate in three modes (namely passive, active and idle modes) in the studied system to effectively utilize the harvested energy at the RIS. Moreover, we propose a novel protocol by which the RIS can schedule its harvested energy only based on the user's location information, which is different from the existing studies where channel state information is required, as well as the state of its harvested energy, so that we can further improve the energy efficiency at the RIS in a practical manner. Under this hybrid RIS framework and the proposed protocol, we formulate a joint optimization problem that aims to maximize the average system throughput over different user's locations by optimizing the operating mode and phase shift of each RIS element, along with the amplification factors of active elements. Owing to the stochastic energy arrivals at the RIS and the discrete nature of mode and phase-shift variables, the problem is challenging to solve directly. To address this, we develop a deep reinforcement learning based approach and design a proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm to obtain a near-optimal solution. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed PPO algorithm.

title: Safeguarding Low-Altitude Integrated Sensing and Communications Networks via UAV-Mounted RIS
authors: Shanza Shakoor, Quang Nhat Le, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: In this paper, we investigate secure transmission in an integrated sensing and communication-cell-free network with the help of a low-altitude uncrewed aerial vehicle-mounted reconfigurable intelligent surface (URIS). The dual-functional access points (APs) simultaneously transmit communication signals to serve legitimate users and emit sensing signal to sense the target, who also acts as a potential eavesdropper (Eve) to intercept the communication signals. To enhance security, the artificial noise (AN) is employed by APs to degrade the Eve's reception. We formulate a sum secrecy rate maximization problem that jointly optimizes the APs' transmit beamformings, AN covariance, RIS's phase shifts, and URIS trajectory. Due to the non-convex nature of the formulated problem, it is divided into three tractable subproblems and solved by an iterative alternating optimization algorithm that incorporates the majorization–minimization framework, semidefinite relaxation, and successive convex approximation techniques. Numerical results confirm that the proposed scheme significantly improves secrecy performance compared with baseline schemes without using AN, fixed RIS, and without deploying URIS.

title: Photoconductive Semiconductor Switches: Uncertainty in the Material Properties
authors: Timothy Wolfe, Alejandro Strachan
abstract: The ambiguity surrounding key optical and electronic material properties in wide band gap semiconductors presents a significant obstacle to further advancing the state of the art in Photoconductive Semiconductor Switches (PCSS) development and integration. Most significantly, the ionization potentials of optically active crystal defects remain uncertain across a range of reported measurement results, with indications of a probable difference between the thermal and optical ionization potentials. The factors must be addressed to ensure purposeful design and fabrication of devices operating on the principle of the interactions between these doped defects, the optical excitation, and the host semiconductor substrate. This review compiles the current body of knowledge on optically addressable defects in wide band gap semiconductors and known implementations into prototype devices. Viable pathways toward resolving these unknown design spaces toward future breakthroughs are identified.

title: Improved Finite-Time Speed-Current Single-Loop Control of PMSM Considering the Limitations of High-Order State Observer
authors: Yixuan Gao, Zhonggang Yin, Yanping Zhang, Yanqing Zhang, Hui Yang, Dongsheng Yuan
abstract: High dynamic response is a key performance indicator of permanent magnet synchronous motor. In the classic dual-loop structure, the inner loop limits the response speed of the outer-loop. Therefore, an improved finite-time composite controller is proposed to be applied in the speed-current single-loop architecture to enhance the dynamic performance of the system target loop. This paper aims to enhance the performance of the outer loop, i.e., the target loop, directly in scenarios where precise control of the inner loop is not strictly necessary. First, after merging the speed and current loops, the order of the controlled objects increases. Compared with the low-order observer designed with the dual-loop structure, the estimated bandwidth of the high-order state observer decreases and the phase delay increases under the same design bandwidth. To this end, a finite-time state observer incorporating proportional differential terms is proposed to overcome the adverse effects of high-order systems. Second, in response to the problem of chattering near the convergence point caused by the inappropriate selection of exponential powers for finite-time functions, a speed-error and torque-current adaptive finite-time control strategy is proposed based on the characteristics of finite-time function and motor control requirements to effectively suppress chattering phenomena. Finally, the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method are verified on the constructed experimental platform.

title: Regime-Conditioned Horizon-aware Mixture-of-Experts for Spatio-temporal Wind Forecasting
authors: Bowen Zou, Ming Li, Laeeq Aslam
abstract: Accurate short-term wind speed prediction (WSP) reduces operational risk in wind-integrated grids because turbine power amplifies wind-speed errors through a cubic dependence on velocity. Improved WSP therefore supports tighter reserve scheduling and more reliable dispatch under regime-dependent variability. Existing deep learning forecasters often rely on a single backbone or step-invariant fusion, which enforces uniform computation across forecast steps and can reduce robustness when dominant dynamics shift across regimes. Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) offers capacity allocation, yet many recent hybrids keep inference-time fusion fixed or use MoE mainly for offline optimization rather than step-wise, regime-conditioned routing. This work proposes Regime-Conditioned Horizon-aware Mixture-of-Experts (RCHA-MOE) for spatio-temporal forecasting. RCHA-MOE fuses three complementary experts that encode persistence, dilated multi-scale temporal structure, and locality-preserving recurrent dynamics, and it learns a Horizon-Conditioned Regime Gate (HCRG) that predicts step-wise mixture weights from the input window to enable regime-adaptive fusion at inference. Experiments on three hourly NASA POWER datasets under a fixed 3 × 3 mesoscale sampling design show that RCHA-MOE achieves the best mean accuracy within this controlled benchmark, improving RMSE by 6.44%–6.93% and MAE by 7.49%–7.65% over the strongest baseline while increasing R2 by 0.77%–1.02%. Moreover, after power-curve mapping, RCHA-MOE reduces power and energy proxy errors, including in the ramp region where the power curve is most sensitive to wind-speed deviations.

title: A QUBO Formulation for Radio Base Station Deployment in Heterogeneous Networks
authors: Edson José Pacheco, Carlos Marcelo Pedroso
abstract: This paper proposes a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) formulation for the radio base station placement problem in heterogeneous 5G/6G networks. Deployment costs, coverage constraints, mutual exclusion, and Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) are encoded jointly into a single quadratic objective matrix. A SINR surrogate Hamiltonian captures the pairwise interference structure through signal-benefit terms on the diagonal and interference penalties on the off-diagonals,without linearization. The resulting two-phase decomposition separates discrete station selection (Phase I) from continuous position refinement (Phase II). Evaluation across scenarios with 50 to 500 demand points compares four pipelines: BIP+DE, QUBO+DE, QUBO+CD, and multi-scale QUBO refinement (QUBO-MS). All QUBO pipelines match the BIP cost-optimality ($3.80M) with 100% coverage. QUBO+DE achieves 12.34 dB network-wide SINR, exceeding the BIP+DE baseline by 2.71 dB due to SINR-aware Phase I selection. QUBO-MS progressively refines positions through cluster-constrained instances from50mto 0.5mresolution, reaching 10.77 dB and closing 62% of the gap relative to the continuous-space optimizer. The residual 2.11 dB gap stems from the pairwise cluster decomposition rather than grid quantization (<0.1 dB at 0.5 m). All multi-scale Phase II instances require fewer than 1,902 variables, ensuring compatibility with D-Wave Advantage quantum annealing hardware

title: Uncertainty-Aware State Estimation: A Multi-Modal Signal Fusion Framework
authors: Muhammad Babar Rasheed, Inam Ullah, Xiandong Ma
abstract: Despite significant advances in data-driven battery modeling, no existing framework simultaneously guarantees estimation accuracy, well-calibrated predictive uncertainty, and computational tractability for real-time battery management systems. This letter addresses this gap by proposing an uncertainty-aware multi-modal signal fusion framework that hierarchically extracts complementary features from voltage, current, and temperature measurements and adaptively combines them through linear synthesis, polynomial basis expansion, and kernel mapping. The fusion mechanism is underpinned by formal convergence guarantees, with weights converging at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\log k/k)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, ensuring provable robustness alongside computational efficiency. Experimental validation across three real-world electric vehicle datasets yields SOC RMSE of 0.07% and voltage RMSE of 0.1 p.u. (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V_{\rm{p.u.}}=V_{\rm{measured}}/V_{\rm{nom}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, corresponding to 0.36-0.42 V absolute error), and coverage deviation within 0.2 percentage points of the nominal 95% prediction interval, all within millisecond-scale cycle times. These results establish a new state-of-the-art in the joint optimisation of estimation accuracy, uncertainty calibration, and real-time BMS deployment.

title: A Fully Fault-Tolerant Multilevel Inverter for Uninterruptible Power Supply Applications
authors: Balram Kumar, Sankar Peddapati, VS Prasadarao K
abstract: For medical appliances and critical sectors, an uninterruptible power supply (UPS) is an indispensable electronic device that protects against power shortages for various loads. This article introduces a novel fault-tolerant multi-level inverter (MLI) designed to offer resilience against multiple switch faults and capable of operating in both symmetric and asymmetric voltage modes. Fault tolerance is achieved with the rated power by adding a redundant unit consisting of triacs and switches. The proposed converter is experimentally validated on a 500 W prototype, demonstrating robust performance under diverse fault conditions. In addition, a new parameter is proposed to assess the fault-tolerant capabilities of converter topologies, offering enhanced understanding of their reliability. Comparative analysis highlights the advantages of the proposed topology, showcasing its superior performance across various metrics. The fault-tolerant capability of the proposed converter presents a reliable solution for UPS systems in critical applications.

title: A Collaborative Neural Network With Adaptive Gradient Dynamics for Nonconvex Optimization
authors: Juliang Wang, Haoen Huang, Kun Deng, Zhigang Zeng
abstract: In this article, a collaborative neural network (CONN) with adaptive gradient dynamics is presented for addressing nonconvex optimization problems. Existing neural dynamics models often suffer from either prohibitive computational costs or unsatisfactory performance, which limits their practical effectiveness. To overcome this, an adaptive gradient neural dynamics (AGND) model is developed for local optimization in nonconvex problems, achieving reduced computational complexity and superior performance. Subsequently, a CONN is proposed that combines multiple AGND models for scattered searches with a metaheuristic rule that reinitializes neuronal states upon local convergence. Theoretical analysis establishes that the AGND model ensures local optimality, and further demonstrates the global convergence of the proposed CONN. Extensive experiments on numerous classical time-invariant nonconvex optimization benchmark problems illustrate the effectiveness and stability of the CONN. Moreover, the proposed method consistently achieves strong performance across diverse application problems, including both time-varying and time-invariant scenarios, such as robotic manipulator trajectory tracking, portfolio selection, and pressure vessel design.

title: Time-Domain Linear Model-based Framework for Passive Acoustic Mapping of Cavitation Activity
authors: Tatiana Gelvez-Barrera, Barbara Nicolas, Denis Kouamé, Bruno Gilles, Adrian Basarab
abstract: Passive acoustic mapping enables the spatial mapping and temporal monitoring of cavitation activity, playing a crucial role in therapeutic ultrasound applications. Most conventional beamforming methods, whether implemented in the time or frequency domains, suffer from limited axial resolution due to the absence of a reference emission onset time. While frequency-domain methods, the most efficient of which are based on the cross-spectral matrix, require long signals for accurate estimation, time-domain methods typically achieve lower spatial resolution. To address these limitations, we propose a linear model-based beamforming framework fully formulated in the time domain. The linear forward model relates a discretized spatiotemporal distribution of cavitation activity to the temporal signals recorded by a probe, explicitly accounting for time-of-flight delays dictated by the acquisition geometry. This model is then inverted using regularization techniques that exploit prior knowledge of cavitation activity in both spatial and temporal domains. Experimental results show that the proposed framework achieves enhanced or competitive cavitation map quality while using only 20% of the data typically required by frequency-domain methods. This highlights the substantial gain in data efficiency and the flexibility of our spatiotemporal regularization to adapt to diverse passive cavitation scenarios, outperforming state-of-the-art techniques.
