title: Enhancing Undergraduate Self-Improvement Through Big-Five Personality Prediction Using an Interactive Questionnaire
authors: Sanjana Attanayake, Shachini Thakshila, Kasunika Rathnayaka, Deepal Samarakoon, Subhash Ariyadasa, Shyama Wilson, Akila Bandara, Kamal Walgama
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Personality psychology aims to understand human thoughts, emotions, and behaviors, and the Five-Factor Model (FFM) is one of its most widely used frameworks. Although instruments such as the IPIP-NEO-120 provide reliable trait measurement, the questionnaire is long and can feel repetitive, which may reduce engagement and contribute to response bias. To address these challenges, this study developed an interactive version of the Big Five assessment that uses scenario-based items and improved UI/UX elements to make the questions easier to understand and more engaging to complete. To support automated trait feedback, a Deep Neural Network (DNN) was trained to reproduce the standard LOW, AVERAGE, and HIGH classifications produced by the percentile-based scoring system. Because the model was trained on a large and diverse dataset, it can infer trait categories directly from item responses without requiring populationspecific norms at deployment. A Knowledge Base System (KBS) was also implemented to provide psychologically grounded and personalized recommendations aligned with users' trait patterns. The interactive questionnaire showed strong internal reliability, with Cronbach's alpha values ranging from 0.80 to 0.89 across the five domains-comparable to established IPIP-NEO-120 benchmarks. Participants reported clearer questions and smoother navigation, although completion time remained similar to the original version. The DNN achieved over 91 % accuracy in replicating standard trait classifications, and users rated the KBS recommendations as useful and relevant. Overall, the study shows that combining interactive questionnaire design, machine learning, and structured psychological guidance can enhance user engagement while remaining aligned with established personality-assessment standards.

title: A Readiness Assessment Framework for Logistics 4.0 Adoption in Sri Lanka: An AHP-Based Evaluation
authors: K K D K T Yashodara, Ruwan Wickramarachchi, Chathumi Ayanthi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Industry 4.0 technologies has transformed logistics into a digitally enabled, integrated system known as Logistics 4.0. While developed economies have made significant progress in adopting these technologies, developing countries continue to face structural, organizational, and policy-related challenges. In Sri Lanka, despite the strategic importance of the logistics sector and the country's role as a regional maritime and transshipment hub, the adoption of Logistics 4.0 remains uneven and largely unstructured. The absence of a localized readiness assessment framework further limits organizations' ability to evaluate preparedness and plan effective digital transformation strategies. This study assesses the readiness of Sri Lankan logistics organizations for Logistics 4.0 adoption by developing and applying a context-specific readiness assessment model. Drawing on an extensive literature review and empirical data collected from industry stakeholders, the research evaluates readiness across key dimensions, including technological infrastructure, organizational processes, human capital, and external environmental factors. The findings reveal significant readiness gaps, particularly among small and medium-sized enterprises, in areas such as digital skills, system integration, and strategic alignment. Based on the results, the study proposes targeted recommendations for practitioners and policymakers to strengthen capabilities and accelerate Logistics 4.0 integration, contributing a practical framework for sustainable digital transformation in Sri Lanka.

title: Recent Advancements and Structural Analysis of Tunnel Field Effect Transistor
authors: Samarth Kumar Singh, Ashish Raman, Abhishek Chauhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: TFET (Tunnel field effect transistor) is the solution to the drawbacks faced by the conventional MOSFET device such as short channel effect, subthreshold swing limitations (60 mV/decade), scalability issue and higher Ioff current leakage. TFET has become as the suitable device due to its superior performance like lower power dissipation, reduced subthreshold swing and leakage current lower than 60mV/dec. TFET has a basic p-i-n structure where the gate terminal has reversed bias, which operates using the fundamental framework of band-to-band tunneling has also suffered some issues like low Ion current which are overcome by the variation in the structural parameters. This Paper reviews the different structure of the TFETs such as nanowire, gate-all-around, double gate, III - V Based, heterojunction, nanosheet, silicon nanotube, carbon nanotube, dopingless and junctionless TFETs.

title: Graph Neural Threat Intelligence: Real-Time Attack Path Prediction Using Event Correlation from Wazuh SIEM
authors: Shubham Chaurasia, Adarsh Singh, Sandeep Kumar Maurya, Divya Prakash Bajpai, Shobhit Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The evolving complexity of cyber threats, including fileless malware and advanced persistent threats (APTs), necessitates advanced detection frameworks that move beyond static signature-based systems. Traditional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) solutions such as Wazuh provide powerful log monitoring and rule-based alerting but lack contextual awareness to correlate multi-stage attacks in real time. This research introduces Graph Neural Threat Intelligence (GNTI) – a hybrid intelligence framework that employs Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to construct dynamic event graphs from Wazuh logs for attack path prediction and behavioral anomaly detection. The proposed system converts heterogeneous Wazuh alerts into structured graph entities representing processes, files, and network connections, allowing the GNN to learn complex inter-event dependencies. Using a Temporal Graph Attention Network (TGAT), GNTI captures temporal context, enabling early-stage prediction of possible attack chains. Experimental evaluation using simulated APT datasets demonstrates an accuracy of 95.6%, with a 25% reduction in false positives and an average detection latency of 1.4 seconds, significantly outperforming baseline models including Random Forest, LSTM Autoencoder, and rule-based Wazuh (p < 0.05). The framework effectively bridges SIEM logging and intelligent graph-based analytics, providing real-time explainability and predictive defense for enterprise systems, with promising potential for SOC automation.

title: Simulation-Based Comparative Study of NRZ and PAM4 Signaling for PCIe Gen6: Analysis of Eye Diagram, BER, Equalization, and Bandwidth Trade-offs
authors: Bandaru Akku Naidu, Nitesh Kashyap
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In the PCI Express 6.0 specification, the per-lane link speed increases to 64 GT/s by switching from binary NRZ (2-level) signalling to 4-level PAM4. This modulation shift doubles the effective data rate without expanding the Nyquist frequency (which remains 16 GHz),effectively halving the symbol rate. The resulting lower symbol rate greatly reduces channel loss (enabling reach comparable to PCIe5). However, PAM4’s multilevel format shrinks the eye opening and noise margin about 9.5 dB SNR, leading to a much higher uncoded bit-error rate (BER) than NRZ. Consequently, PCIe 6.0 mandates low-latency FEC and stronger equalization to meet its BER targets. We present a detailed simulation-based comparison of NRZ (PAM2) and PAM4 signaling for PCIe Gen6 channels. The link models include realistic PCB losses and receiver equalization (continuous-time linear equalizer, CTLE, plus a multi-tap feed-forward equalizer, FFE). We analyze eye diagrams and plot BER vs. SNR curves both before and after FEC decoding, and also we examine the power spectral density (PSD) of each modulation. The PSD results confirm that PAM4 carries two bits per symbol at the same Nyquist frequency, halving the symbol rate (and required bandwidth) relative to NRZ. The BER-vs.-SNR curves show that raw NRZ achieves far lower uncoded BER at any fixed SNR (reflecting its larger eye), but its doubled Nyquist bandwidth at 64 GT/s makes a loss-limited Gen6 link infeasible. By contrast, PAM4 can achieve 64 GT/s with similar channel loss; crucially, it only meets the stringent PCIe6 BER targets when low-latency FEC and aggressive equalization are applied. These findings highlight the fundamental trade-off in PCIe Gen6 design: PAM4’s bandwidth efficiency is essential to reach 64 GT/s over lossy channels, but it comes at the cost of tighter signal-to-noise requirements (reduced eye margin) and reliance on FEC and enhanced equalization compared to NRZ.

title: Enhanced Emotional Intelligence and Decision Making with Generative Artificial Intelligence, Fuzzy Sets, and Intelligent Tutoring Systems
authors: Deepak Singh Rana, Sanjay Oli, Tursunova Laylo, Aripov Shokirjon Olimovich, Matluba Yakubova, Mahliyo Khaydarova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: ITSs have achieved significant developments in personalized learning; however, most of the systems are largely focused on cognitive learning and do not consider the emotions of the learners. Such factors as stress, motivation, and frustration are emotional factors, which are strong in terms of the effects on the learning outcomes and decision making. The paper outlines a better emotionally intelligent tutoring system integrating Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), fuzzy set theory and ITS architectures in facilitating the situation aware affect aware decision making. Generative AI is implemented to solve the contextual and implicit emotional hints of the interaction between the learner, and fuzzy logic is implemented to solve the confusion and ambiguity in the emotional states via linguistic reasoning. The adaptive pedagogical interventions are translated into adaptive pedagogy systems based on a fuzzy inference-based decision (adaptive pedagogy) engine that takes emotional insights. The proposed hybrid framework is experimentally evaluated to be superior to the traditional, GenAI-based, and fuzzy-based ITS models regarding the accuracy of emotion intelligence detection and effectiveness of decision-making. The results stress the increased learner active involvement and emotional well-being and flexibility of instructions in favor of the suitability of the offered method in the digital-based learning classrooms of the next generation.

title: Review on Online Farming Route System for Farmers and Experts
authors: Harshit Tyagi, Rohit Kumar Singh, Swapnil Srivastava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Online farming route frameworks integrate precision agriculture detection with geographical information systems. The logistics are optimized, and decision support is improved to assist cultivators and specialists in designing and carrying out activities in the field, collecting supplies, and transporting farm produce to the market. This survey offers a comprehensive overview of the latest advancements in farm sensing and connectivity and agricultural routing and optimization models. These improvements will help in last-mile connectivity, freshness-aware delivery, and application platforms. We have also reviewed 33 related papers by comparing the techniques and datasets and identified key findings and gaps. The drawbacks include dynamic routing under field conditions, lack of agricultural routing benchmarks, and lack of studies on the UX perspective of farmers. The future research directions are IoT-based dynamic VRP, hybrid neuro-symbolic routing, data sharing with privacy, and cost-effective deployment techniques. The work is expected to inform researchers and practitioners who can construct useful online route systems to the benefit of farmers, agronomists, and logistics partners.

title: Intelligent Decentralized Web Proxy Caching Framework Using Sharpbelly Fish Optimization Algorithm
authors: Muruganantham U, Vaitheki Kanagaraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Cache memory significantly enhances the speed of web servers, particularly in the context of large data transfer, when reaction time is limited. It is essential to choose an efficient technique, such web cache. Because a good cache replacement technique may shorten users' access times while simultaneously enhancing system performance. The conventional weighted replacement approach neglects the size parameter, resulting in worse performance with larger datasets. This research offers an intelligent decentralized web proxy caching system using the Sharpbelly Fish Optimization Algorithm (SFOA), a nature-inspired metaheuristic that emulates collective foraging and adaptive movement behaviors. The proposed system features SFOA as an adaptive cache optimization engine that dynamically assesses appropriate cache content placement and eviction strategies by evaluating cache-related factors, including object popularity, size, retrieval delay, and bandwidth cost. The Performance evaluation, utilizing standard web cache metrics and a benchmark dataset, revealed that SFOA achieves a 93.8% cache find ratio with an average response time of 35 milliseconds.

title: Efficient Physical Design Flow for Modern VLSI Systems Using RTL-to-GDSII Methodology
authors: Chintala Mutyala Venkata Satya Murthy, Vrinda Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Modern chip designs have become so complex that meeting speed, power, and time-to-market demands is no longer possible without a well-organized physical design process. To build reliable digital hardware, designers must move from an RTL description all the way to a clean GDSII layout, and each step in between plays an important role. In this paper, we review a practical physical design flow that works well for common digital blocks, including finite state machines and small data path units. The flow starts with writing and testing the RTL, then continues through logic synthesis, floorplanning, placement, clock tree creation, and the routing stages. Physical checks at the end ensure that the layout is ready for fabrication. Throughout the discussion, particular attention is given to problems engineers usually after face timing failures, power waste, and routing congestion and how these issues can be handled. From the results reported in several design trials, the overall flow shows shorter iteration cycles, better timing stability, and predictable layout quality across different technology nodes.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Sensing Platform for Hydrogen Generation Analysis in a Down-Scaled Transformer Oil Fault Simulator
authors: Hui-Yang Chen, Lie-Zen Ho, Chi-Huang Hung, Ting-Chuan Lin, Hao-Wei Chen, Yu-Yan Chi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study presents an enhanced smart sensing platform capable of analyzing hydrogen gas generation in a down-scaled transformer oil fault-simulation environment. A 1L oil chamber reproduces early transformer fault behavior through controlled micro-arc discharge and thermal excitation. A semiconductor hydrogen sensor, PT100 thermometer, and electrical monitoring modules acquire synchronized measurements of gas concentration, temperature, and discharge energy. In the upgraded design, an IoT communication layer based on the MQTT protocol enables remote monitoring and real-time data access through cloud dashboards. Sensor data-including hydrogen concentration, oil temperature, and electrical load-are transmitted as structured packets for visualization and long-term logging. Experimental results and modeled estimations confirm that the system can qualitatively reproduce gas-formation patterns consistent with dissolved-gasanalysis (DGA) mechanisms. The proposed IoT-enabled platform provides a compact, low-cost foundation for intelligent transformer diagnostics, educational demonstration, and future development of multi-gas and AI-assisted monitoring systems.

title: Topological Reconfiguration as a Pathway to Alternative Stable States in Post Tsunami Coastal Recovery on the Sendai Coastline
authors: Arnob Bormudoi, Masahiko Nagai, Muhammad Daniel Iman bin Hussain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Conventional assessments of post-disaster coastal recovery often emphasize geometric metrics such as shoreline position, which may not fully capture underlying structural reorganization. This study investigated the long-term morphological evolution of the Sendai coastline following the 2011 Tōhoku tsunami by integrating Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) for geometric analysis with Persistent Homology (PH) for topological characterization. Using multispectral satellite imagery from Landsat and Sentinel-2 (2010-2024), we extracted instantaneous shorelines using the Modified Normalized Difference Water Index and reconstructed them as parametric B-spline curves. DTW analysis quantified progressive geometric stabilization, with distances decreasing from 90,927 m (posttsunami) to 59,584 m by 2024. In contrast, PH revealed a nonlinear topological trajectory where the number of 1-dimensional features (H1) surged from 1,127 in 2015 to over 8,000 by 20202024, indicating a phase transition toward heightened complexity. The Stable Distance of Persistent Homology peaked at 38,088 m during 2015-2020, marking a period of profound structural reorganization. A weak negative correlation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.362$</tex>) between DTW and PH-based metrics confirmed their complementarity, capturing orthogonal dimensions of coastal change. The findings challenged the paradigm of simple geometric restoration, demonstrating that post-tsunami recovery could lead to alternative stable states characterized by enhanced topological complexity. The integrated framework can provide a robust approach for detecting multi-scale morphological change and offer new insights into coastal resilience and long-term landscape adaptation following extreme disturbances.

title: A Scalable Location-Based P2P System for High-Load Environments: Design, Implementation, and Evaluation
authors: Masato Mitsuyama, Taku Yamazaki, Takumi Miyoshi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper proposes a scalable architecture for Geo-location Oriented Network (G-LocON), a system that performs nearby device discovery based on location information. Conventional approaches struggle to allocate server resources appropriately when device density is uneven across regions, resulting in degraded responsiveness under localized high-load conditions. To address this issue, the proposed approach designs the application layer to be fully stateless to enable horizontal scaling via Kubernetes and introduces Redis Stack as a shared data store to ensure consistent device information management across multiple servers. Four data management methods using Redis Stack were implemented, and the end-to-end latency for device information registration/update (PUT) and nearby device search (GET) was evaluated. Experimental results show that leveraging Redis's native geospatial management capabilities enables low-latency processing, demonstrating the suitability of the proposed system for real-time G-LocON communication.

title: Ultra-Early Filtering for Uncompressed Image Stream on Edge Device Using in-Kernel Frame Differencing of Partial Images
authors: Yuto Hamaguchi, Tatsuya Yamada, Hiroki Masuda
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In recent years, edge AI has become popular among consumers for high-resolution, low-latency inference processing near data sources like surveillance cameras. This trend is partly due to model compression techniques that enhance the feasibility of inference on low-end edge devices. However, as the amount of data processed by edge AI increases, overall power consumption is expected to rise. To address this, some research have been explored early filtering methods at the edge to reduce resource usage before data reaches cloud processing. These methods assess temporal data similarity and discard redundant data. However, the CPU load incurred from filtering during packet reception has not been sufficiently considered to maintain filtering accuracy. This study aims to reduce CPU load while maintaining filtering accuracy in edge devices handling time-series data. We propose an ultra-early filtering method that detects changes in uncompressed image streams upon packet reception. This approach minimizes CPU load by analyzing changes at the partial image level during network reception. Evaluations using GigE Vision® RAW image streams confirmed a CPU load reduction of up to 56% while maintaining filtering accuracy, with improvements achieving an average 60% reduction in CPU load during motion detection.

title: Importance-Aware Quantization Strategy for Layer-Sensitive YOLO Architectures
authors: Myke Valadão, Larissa Da Silva Pessoa, Mateus S. Pantoja, Victor R. M. Cavalcante, Ana Julia P. Correa, Douglas S. Melo, Edma Urtiga De Mattos Mattos, Andrey R. R. Bessa, Isabella A. Cabral, Diego Amoedo, Waldir Silva, Celso Carvalho, Anastacio Augusto Mota Cavalcante, Laura A. Martinho, Orlewilson B. Maia, Eddie B De Lima Filho, Rômulo Fabricio, Igor Bispo M. C. Correia, Stephany C. Silva, Mateus V. Said
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Deploying modern object detectors on edge devices is limited by strict compute and memory budgets. While quantization helps reduce cost, the resulting uniform diminished precision often harms accuracy. As a response, we propose an importance-aware, layer-sensitive quantization strategy for You Only Look Once (YOLO) models that estimates per-layer sensitivity using a 2-bit vs. 8-bit probe, maps sensitivity and importance to bitwidths, and refines assignments through iterative, goal-driven optimization. Using giga bit-operations to measure effective workload, our method reduces the YOLOv11n compute cost by 95 % while preserving 81 % of the baseline <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 50: 95$</tex>, with the best configuration at iteration 15. Early, high-impact layers retain higher precision, while low-impact layers-especially within the deep aggregation block (model.23.*)-are aggressively quantized with minimal accuracy loss. The approach is interpretable, hardware-aware, and well-suited for deploying YOLO detectors on resource-constrained edge platforms.

title: Personalized On-Device Stress Detection Using INT8 TinyML Models and EWC-Lite Adaptation
authors: Showkat Ahmad Bhat, Ming-Che Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Wearable stress monitoring demands models that are both lightweight and resilient to variations between individuals. In this work, we introduce a compact 1D-CNN architecture tailored for TinyML applications, designed to perform on-device stress recognition using electrodermal activity (EDA) signals. To enhance adaptability, we incorporate a lightweight personalization approach based on Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWCLite). The model is fully quantized to INT8 and fits within just <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 ~ k B}$</tex> of memory, making it suitable for deployment on CortexM4 microcontrollers. We evaluated the system using a leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) approach on the WESAD dataset. Despite the reduced precision, the INT8 model achieved performance nearly on par with its float32 counterpart, with mean accuracies of 0.6590 and 0.6635, respectively. Personalized fine-tuning further boosted performance, reaching a peak accuracy of 0.6930 using only 40 adaptation windows. These findings highlight the potential of personalized, on-device stress recognition for ultra-low-power wearable platforms.

title: Secrecy Outage Analysis of STAR-RIS-NOMA Systems Under Frequency-Selective Fading Channel
authors: Yi-Zhen Chung, Chien-Chun Wang, Szu-Liang Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Emerging consumer wireless services demand spectrum-efficient access and strong physical-layer security in dense and interference-prone environments. Consequently, downlink NOMA assisted by a simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) is considered a promising solution. In this paper, the secrecy outage probability (SOP) of NOMA users is analyzed in the presence of an external passive eavesdropper, where the STAR-RIS is employed to enhance the system performance. The impacts of eavesdropperinduced jamming noise and NOMA power allocation on the SOP are also examined. Simulation results show that the SOP of both users increases and eventually saturates as the jamming scale grows, while the adopted power-allocation strategy plays a key role in determining their secrecy performance. These results demonstrate that STAR-RIS-NOMA provides an effective and secure connectivity framework for future B5G/6G consumer communication systems.

title: Factors for an EOD Technician Readiness Framework: An Exploratory Study
authors: Amr Ehab Shokry, Nada Hany Sherief, Ahmed Mohsen Osman, Mohammed Abu Rizka
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Contemporary Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) operations in conflict zones involve increasingly complex and unpredictable Improvised Explosive Devices (IEDs), necessitating exceptional technical proficiency, psychological fortitude, cognitive adaptability, and effective decision-making under severe pressure. Despite the vital and high-risk nature of this duty, a thorough multi-factor methodology for evaluating EOD technician operational preparedness prior to deployment is missing. Current readiness evaluations are fragmented, typically focusing on singular factors like technical proficiency or physical condition, and neglecting the interconnected aspects essential for effective mission execution. This research conducted an exploratory qualitative study to systematically identify the essential non-technical and technical components that collectively influence EOD operational readiness, thereby addressing the foundational knowledge gap. The process entailed performing semi-structured interviews with ten highly skilled EOD technicians. Thematic analysis of the data revealed that operational preparedness is influenced by four essential and interconnected domains: (1) Psychological Resilience, (2) Technical Proficiency, (3) Personality Traits, and (4) Cognitive Abilities. These findings establish the empirical foundation and conceptual framework required for the following design and automation of a multi-factor assessment framework. This research contributes directly to enhancing the pre-mission selection and preparation methods for EOD specialists, crucial for mitigating operational risk and increasing mission outcomes as well as personnel safety.

title: Remaining Useful Life Prediction Driven by LLMs Using Few-Shot Learning
authors: Hany Hanafy Mahmoud Said, Ahmed Hanae Kassem
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Accurate prediction of remaining useful life (RUL) is critical for battery management systems to ensure battery safety, reliability, and longevity. Machine learning approaches for RUL prediction often require large amounts of labeled data, which are expensive and time-consuming to collect. This study explores the potential of using few-shot learning with large language models (LLMs) to address this limitation by enabling predictive modeling from minimal training samples. We investigate a range of LLMs, including Llama 3.1, Llama 3.2, Qwen2-Math, Qwen2.5, Phi4-reasoning, and GPT-OSS, evaluated with the battery RUL dataset. Study results indicate that a domain-specialized model such as GPT-OSS and Qwen2.5 achieve high accuracy RUL prediction. The results highlight the impact of dataset feature sizes, LLM's training approach and reasoning ability on RUL prediction performance. Overall, the study shows that domain-aligned LLMs, when combined with few-shot learning, offer a promising pathway toward adaptive, and efficient prognostics for battery management systems.

title: Advanced and Optimized MIMO Control Algorithms for Defensive Strategy of an Air to Surface Missiles
authors: Belkacem Bekhiti, Kamel Hariche, Abdel-Nasser Sharkawy
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: In this paper, the design of an optimal multivariable feedback compensator (OMFC) is proposed, where the selection of block roots of the associated matrix polynomial is carried out using the modern whale optimization algorithm (WOA). These set of block roots are systematically allocated through operator transformation techniques together with the right block Vandermonde formulation. The most suitable compensator is then obtained by solving a nonlinear convex optimization problem with WOA, ensuring stability and efficiency. As a representative application, a defensive air-to-surface missile system (ASMS) is investigated, and simulation experiments confirm enhanced tracking accuracy, reduced control effort, and improved dynamic performance.

title: A Hybrid AI-Based Model for Cyberattack Detection and Mitigation
authors: Calle Huaman, Diego Gianfranco, Dextre Alarcón, Jymmy Stuwart
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: In recent years, information security has gained critical importance, driven by an alarming surge in cyberattacks. In Peru alone, more than 15 billion attempts have been reported—a trend that continues to rise. Enterprise networks are among the most affected, particularly in critical sectors such as healthcare, where the protection of information is essential. In this context, a hybrid AI-based detection and mitigation model is proposed that is designed to deliver a lightweight, efficient, and low-cost solution capable of automatically identifying and responding to threats in real time without compromising network performance. Network traffic is analyzed on a Raspberry Pi 5 using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTMs), with tools such as TensorFlow/Keras, Flask, SQLite, and iptables. The architecture is tailored for resource-constrained environments, facilitating deployment in real-world networks. The methodology was structured into five phases: tool selection, preparation of the CICIDS2017 dataset, model training, integration in a controlled environment, and validation at a hospital in Lima. An accuracy of 97% was achieved, providing a solid foundation for future real-world deployments.

title: SPARNet: A Framework for Airway Invasion Tracking from Fluoroscopic Videos of Dysphagia Patients_supp1-3695144.pdf
authors: Uma Gopalakrishnan
abstract: The videofluoroscopic swallowing study (VFSS) is the clinical gold standard for evaluating dysphagia and detecting airway invasion. However, manual interpretation is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to rater variability due to the rapid and complex nature of swallowing events. Existing deep learning approaches for airway invasion are limited by relying on manual frame selection, a lack of interpretability, and black-box decision-making. In this study, we propose SPARNet (Swallowing Phase Airway Risk Network), AI pipeline integrates swallowing-phase classification, bolus and airway delineation, and airway-invasion tracking to provide a clinically interpretable assessment. SPARNet introduces a novel Airway Invasion Tracker (AIT) that partitions the visible laryngo-tracheal air column in VFSS into quadrants, tracks the location of the bolus, and computes temporal invasion parameters (event duration, total exposure time, and number of invasion episodes) to characterize airway invasion during the pharyngeal phase. We curated a VFSS-MultiLabel dataset comprising 125 clips from 75 patients, annotated for multiple tasks. SPARNet achieved strong performance across all components, including the pharyngeal phase detection (F1-score = 88%), bolus segmentation (Dice score = 0.83), laryngo-tracheal air column detection (mAP = 0.95), and airway invasion detection (F1-score = 92%). Unlike prior black-box models, SPARNet provides transparent spatial and temporal cues that align with clinical VFSS reasoning. These results demonstrate the feasibility of an system-level AI pipeline for VFSS analysis, highlighting the potential to support clinical decision-making in the management of dysphagia.

title: Hybrid Fault Isolation and Power Control of Wind Turbine Generators based on Stacked UIO
authors: Qi Yao, Kang Li, Sheng Huang, Zhonggui Li, Fang Fang, Jizhen Liu
abstract: The stable operation of megawatt-class wind turbines directly impacts grid connection performance. As complex electromechanical systems operating in variable environments, time-varying hybrid faults frequently occur, posing challenges for fault-tolerant control (FTC). This paper proposes novel approaches for hybrid fault diagnosis and fault-tolerant controller design. First, a collaborative architecture for a stacked unknown input observer (UIO) is constructed for integrated fault diagnosis of hybrid faults in wind turbine generators (WTGs). The designed stacked UIO strategy can decouple and separately estimate the dynamic of speed and torque, achieving effective time-varying hybrid fault diagnosis. Second, given the disturbances, uncertainties, and discrete-time nature of WTGs, the asymptotic stability of the proposed UIO is established using Lyapunov's theorem. Finally, combining fault information, a two-degree-of-freedom model predictive control strategy is adopted to achieve FTC while also considering power stability tracking. Simulation results show that the proposed method can estimate time-varying faults in WTG actuators and sensors, and maintain reliable control performance under hybrid fault conditions, thereby verifying its effectiveness as a comprehensive FTC solution.

title: Enhanced Non-Spherical Aerosol Scheme for Thermal-infrared Radiative Transfer Model: A Super-Ellipsoidal Scheme for Sea Salt and Mineral Dust Aerosol (RTM-SuperAero)
authors: Yizhen Meng, Lei Bi, Peiming Dong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Thermal infrared (TIR) observations, such as those from the Hyperspectral Infrared Atmospheric Sounder (HIRAS) onboard the Fengyun-3D (FY3D) satellite, provide valuable insights into aerosol remote sensing. However, conventional TIR radiative transfer models (RTMs) typically assume spherical aerosol particles, a simplification that can introduce errors in simulating radiative effects. To address this limitation, an enhanced non-spherical aerosol scheme for TIR simulation, referred to as RTM-SuperAero, has been developed. This scheme replaces the conventional assumption of spherical particles with a super-ellipsoidal model and employs the invariant imbedding T Matrix (IITM) method to accurately calculate the optical properties of non-spherical aerosol particles. Additionally, bimodal particle size distributions (PSDs) for sea salt aerosol (SSA) and mineral dust aerosol (DUS) have been implemented based on global in-situ measurements from the AErosol RObotic NETwork (AERONET). The RTM-SuperAero scheme was integrated into the Radiative Transfer for TOVS (RTTOV) model and evaluated using six marine DUS cases over the Bohai Sea and the Atlantic Ocean. Compared to the default Optical Properties of Aerosols and Clouds (OPAC) configurations within RTTOV, RTM-SuperAero provides improved agreement with observed brightness temperatures (BTs), with a reduction in root-mean-square-error (RMSE) of over 12.16% in these controlled cases. This framework demonstrates physically interpretable improvements in radiative transfer simulations under selected aerosol and atmospheric conditions, highlighting the benefits of incorporating non-spherical aerosol representations in TIR RTMs.

title: SRENet: Spectral Re-Entry Network for Point Cloud Action Recognition
authors: Qiuxia Wu, Jiarui Lan, Wenxiong Kang, Zhiyong Wang, Kun Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Recognizing human actions from point cloud sequences is critical for 3D perception driven applications such as autonomous driving and human-computer interaction. However, the irregular structure and temporal inconsistency of point clouds pose unique challenges for spatio-temporal representation learning, especially in capturing both global motion context and fine-grained temporal dynamics. We propose SRENet, a spectral-aware framework designed to explicitly learn both global context and fine-grained temporal dynamics of motion from a frequency perspective for action recognition. SRENet introduces a Spectral Decomposition Block (SDeBlock) that performs wavelet-based analysis along temporal and spatial axes, disentangling features into low- and high-frequency components with frequency-specific attention. To recover residual dynamics and re-align temporal frequency structures distorted during semantic fusion, a Spectral Re-entry Block (SReBlock) performs secondary temporal decomposition. Furthermore, a spectral-aware learning strategy is devised to enhance discriminability in both frequency subspaces via contrastive loss and a curriculum schedule that gradually shifts focus from low- to high-frequency spaces in line with coarse to detailed motion patterns. Extensive experiments on MSR-Action3D, NTU-RGBD and NTU-RGBD120 demonstrate that SRENet achieves state-of-the-art performance, validating the effectiveness of frequency modeling in point cloud-based action understanding. 1.

title: An Inductor Voltage Feedback-Based Active Damping Method for Electrolytic Capacitor-Less PMSM Drives
authors: Cheng Yuan, Yi Wang, Xuanqin Wu, Hong Wang, Xianbin Qi, Helong Li
abstract: In electrolytic capacitor-less permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drive systems, the resonance between the inductor and the dc-link capacitor degrades the grid current quality. In this letter, a novel inductor voltage feedback-based active damping method is proposed, which reduces the error and time delay of the resonant component extraction, expands the bandwidth of the active damping control loop, achieves enhanced suppression of grid current resonance and reduces both the required inductance and associated costs. Experimental results are presented to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Multi-System Error Separation-Based Coaxiality Measurement for High Depth-to-Diameter Ratio Casing Cavities in Aeroengines
authors: Haixu Yu, Chunyu Shao, Ruirui Li, Like Zhang, Jiean Li, Chuanzhi Sun, Jingzhi Huang, Yongmeng Liu, Jiubin Tan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The coaxiality of the installation and machining processes of aero-engine components, including the combustion chamber casing, fuel gear pump, and intermediate casing, is a critical factor influencing service life and operational stability. During the machining and measurement of workpieces with large depth-to-diameter ratios, measuring equipment inevitably introduces additional errors. During measurement, systematic errors—including workpiece mounting eccentricity, sensor radial offset, sensor pitch angle deviation, sensor column inclination, and turntable axis tilt—are mutually coupled, resulting in a significant reduction in measurement accuracy. The proposed method is specifically developed for inner cavity surface measurements with large depth-to-diameter ratios, where the depth exceeds 400 mm and the diameter is less than 100 mm. In the coaxiality measurement of inner cavities with large depth-to-diameter ratios, accessibility limitations and occlusion effects make it difficult for three-dimensional optical metrology systems to achieve reliable evaluation. Moreover, traditional measurement systems typically use the outer surface of stepped shafts as the measurement reference, making it difficult for probes to access deep inner cavities with large depth-to-diameter ratios; consequently, the corresponding measurement models are no longer applicable. In this study, a five-system-error measurement model is proposed based on a non-contact automated measurement device. The effects of the five systematic errors on measurement results are numerically simulated and analyzed. An optimization method based on the SMA algorithm is employed to estimate and separate systematic errors from measurement data obtained from shell components of varying sizes with large depth-to-diameter ratios. Simulation results indicate that the proposed model is more effective for workpieces with smaller dimensions and larger systematic error magnitudes. Finally, the validity of the proposed five-system-error model is experimentally verified. Measurements are conducted using a standard artifact with a nominal coaxiality of 11.64 μm. After error separation using the two-system-error model, a coaxiality value of 22.9 μm is obtained, whereas a reduced value of 17.02 μm is achieved using the five-system-error model. Overall, the proposed model improves measurement accuracy by 43.2%, corresponding to an improvement of 4.06 μm.

title: IEEE Microwave Theory and Technology Society Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology

title: Efficient Composition of Modal Experts for Versatile Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Tongtian Yue, Longteng Guo, Xuange Gao, Zijia Zhao, Hua Huang, Jing Liu
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown promise in comprehending various specialized modalities, yet developing a versatile model proficient in multiple modalities remains challenging and resource-intensive. In this paper, we explore efficient methods for training MLLMs to master various modalities. We first introduce a novel framework, the Multimodal Experts Composition (MixLLM), which enables the efficient and scalable integration of modality-specific MLLMs into a unified comprehensive model. Specifically, MixLLM adopts a modular Mixture of Experts (MoE) design with both modality-specific experts and shared experts. We propose a hybrid routing mechanism that adaptively coordinates the use of these two types of experts, enabling each token to benefit from both specialized modality knowledge and shared semantic representations. This design enables strong unimodal performance and flexible cross-modal reasoning. To further enhance the model's capability in following cross-modal instructions, we then propose the Multimodal Samples Composition (MixSample), a simple yet effective strategy for cross-modal instruction tuning. MixSample innovatively combines samples from different modalities into pseudo cross-modal datasets, thereby reducing the dependency on scarce and complex-to-acquire fully paired multimodal data. Implementing both MixLLM and MixSample, we develop MixtralX-11×7B , a model adept at handling inputs from various combinations of text, image, video, and audio modalities. Featured with a sparsely-activated MoE architecture, the model scales up parameters efficiently while maintaining computational costs. Extensive evaluations across various unimodal and cross-modal benchmarks demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance across all evaluated tasks with a single unified model. Both our model and code will be publicly available.

title: Uncertainty-Weighted Spectral Similarity for Hyperspectral Imaging Spectroscopy
authors: Dariusz Tanajewski, Katarzyna Jabłońska, Marcin Jakubowicz
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Spectral similarity measures such as the Spectral Angle Mapper (SAM) treat all spectral bands equally, an assumption difficult to justify for modern imaging spectroscopy data where measurement uncertainty varies strongly with wavelength. We introduce wSAM, an uncertainty-weighted extension of SAM that incorporates per-band observational uncertainty as inverse-variance weights. On 47 EMIT–RadCalNet observation pairs across five sites, wSAM (w = 1/σ2) reduces mean spectral angle by 49% relative to SAM and outperforms both hard band masking and alternative weighting schemes, while a cross-site discriminability analysis confirms that weighting sharpens rather than erases spectral differences between surface types.

title: Synthetic Priors for Real-World Detection: a Label-Free Framework for Identifying Ultra-Rare Objects_supp1-3693305.pdf
authors: Congsheng Li
abstract: Detecting ultra-rare objects (frequency < 10-6) without positive annotations remains a critical challenge in computer vision. We propose a Synthesis-Prior Driven paradigm that eliminates the need for real positive samples. Our core insight is that models trained on statistically and perceptually indistinguishable synthetic data can generalize effectively to real-world environments. To achieve this, we introduce a Differentiable Morphological Modeling framework, which embeds explicit physical and geometric priors into a differentiable synthesis engine. Unlike black-box generative models, this approach ensures high-fidelity imagery with perfectly accurate, objective annotations. We validated this paradigm on the safety-critical task of detecting Circulating Genetically Abnormal Cells (CACs). Perceptual assessments confirmed that synthetic data is indistinguishable from clinical imagery (expert accuracy ≈ 50%). Consequently, our model, trained exclusively on synthetic data, achieved state-of-the-art performance on real patient samples (98.43% accuracy, 97.40% sensitivity), outperforming both human experts and existing automated methods. Furthermore, the model demonstrated exceptional robustness across a multi-center cohort (CV < 0.8%) and achieved high-precision zero-shot transfer in industrial defect inspection (DAGM 2007). This study establishes a principled methodology for ultra-rare object detection, offering broad applicability across domains with definable geometric priors.

title: Not All Problems Are Nails, Not All Tools Should Be Hammers: A Position Paper on Agent Usage in Software Engineering Tasks

title: YOLO-Based Structure Optimization for Small-Object Detection of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Chao-Hsien Lee, Kun-Jhe Sun, Tan-Hsu Tan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: With the increasing and widespread deployment of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), also known as drones, one critical challenge is how to identify and track target objects rapidly and precisely. However, due to the limited computation and communication capacity, lightweight deep learning models for edge devices, e.g., drones, become promising solutions. This paper studies and optimizes the YOLO-based object-recognition model structure. Based on our experimental results using the VisDrone2019 dataset, the proposed model called MIX-YOLO can improve 12.25% precision, 11.88% recall, 12.21% F1-score, 12.23% parameters, and 10.56% model size.

title: Development of an Electricity-Saving Support System Using Simple Sensors
authors: Tatsuya Yamazaki
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In this paper, a system that aims to contribute to energy conservation in the home is proposed. The proposed system consists of simple sensors, a knowledge base, and a speaker. The sensors monitor the status of home appliances, the residents' behavior, and the environmental situation. The knowledge base plays a role of the information storage related with the target appliances. The speaker is just an output interface for the residents. A prototype of the proposed system has been implemented. An air conditioner and a television were selected as the target home appliance and audio information retrieval experiments were conducted. The collected data were processed by Fourier analysis and the results were stored in the knowledge base.

title: Non-Contact Monitoring of Dystocia in Dairy Cows Using Keypoint Detection and Semantic Segmentation
authors: Takumi Murayama, Thi Thi Zin, Ikuo Kobayashi, Masaru Aikawa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In the dairy industry, labor shortages and economic losses due to calving accidents are major challenges. To address these issues, we propose a non-contact monitoring system that employs 360-degree cameras combined with deep learning techniques. This study focuses on developing an automated workflow to detect cows, estimate their poses (standing or lying), and track individuals without attaching sensors. We used YOLO11 for cow detection and keypoint extraction and compared three models for pose estimation: Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and semantic segmentation (DeepLabv3+). Experimental results show that YOLO11 achieves high detection accuracy (mAP@0.50: 99.47%) for bounding boxes. For pose estimation, the semantic segmentation approach with a ResNet101 backbone achieves the highest accuracy of 85.1%, outperforming keypoint-based methods. These results demonstrate the potential of the proposed system for basic behavioral monitoring in calving barns.

title: Lightweight Regularized Wavelet-Domain Low-Frequency Color Compensation for Underwater Image Enhancement on Consumer Devices
authors: Wu-Chih Hu, Xiang-Rui Huang, Huan-Shuo Hu, Jun-Wei Hsieh, Liang-Bi Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: A lightweight underwater image enhancement framework is presented in which classical color-compensation techniques are integrated into a unified processing pipeline. Global color-cast estimation in the CIE Lab space is performed, followed by chroma normalization, histogram tail clipping, and YCbCr-domain chroma rectification. Chroma fidelity is further improved through 4:2:0 subsampling, block-based <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> DCT coding, adaptive quantization, and DC-component suppression. Wavelet-domain refinement is then applied, where the Low-Low (LL) subband obtained from DWT is adjusted using a regularized channel-wise gain to correct low-frequency luminancechrominance imbalance. Additional enhancement is achieved through gray-world white balancing, CLAHE luminance correction, multi-scale Gaussian/Laplacian fusion, and redchannel reinforcement. Through the combined use of DCT, DWT, chroma subsampling, and multi-scale filtering, a fully classical and computationally efficient underwater color-restoration pipeline is achieved for low-power consumer imaging devices.

title: A Real-Time Facial Pressure-Matrix Feedback System for Massage Skill Training
authors: Li-Feng Wang, Yen Hsu, Yueh-Guo Liou, Chi-Huang Hung, Wei-Chih Juan, Chia-Hsun Hsu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Traditional facial massage instruction relies on demonstration and imitation, providing no objective feedback on force or movement quality. This study presents a real-time facial pressure-matrix feedback system designed to support skill acquisition in facial massage training. A flexible <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 x 4}$</tex> pressure sensor array captures force distribution and directional motion across key facial regions. The data are transmitted wirelessly and visualized as heatmaps and motion trajectories, enabling learners to compare their performance with expert patterns. The system provides measurable feedback on force level, direction consistency, coverage, and rhythmic stability, thus addressing the limitations of conventional training and practice mannequins. This approach supports standardized, reproducible, and feedback-driven learning of massage techniques.

title: Reliability and Efficiency Analysis of Voting Methods in Parallel Volunteer Computing
authors: Zhiding Sun, Keiichi Inohara, Yota Kurokawa, Masaru Fukushi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Volunteer Computing (VC) is an Internet-based distributed computing paradigm. With recent research progress, it has shown the capability of handling not only distributed computing (i.e., distributed VC) but also parallel computing (i.e., parallel VC). However, parallel VC faces critical reliability issues due to worker dropouts and calculation errors. In this paper, we propose a new voting method for parallel VC in order to improve reliability and efficiency in parallel processing. The proposed method focuses on the property of server assisted communication to realize parallel VC and performs voting for communication data at a subjob level, synchronizing the communication data. Results show that the proposed method out-performs traditional voting methods, proving its suitability for parallel VC.

title: Real-Time Drug Recognition System Using Edge Computing: A Case Study on Medications Within Unit-Dose Packages
authors: Tsung-Sheng Cheng, Cheng-Siou Wu, Bo-Chen Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: The proliferation of diverse pharmaceutical products has made medication errors and the confusion of lookalike drugs a critical challenge in global healthcare. Traditional manual verification protocols (e.g., the “Three Checks and Five Rights”) are time-consuming and inherently prone to human error due to factors like drug similarity and personnel fatigue. To mitigate these risks and enhance efficiency, this research proposes a novel Real-Time Drug Recognition System utilizing low-cost Edge Computing. Our system is designed to immediately verify prescription information by capturing and analyzing the physical characteristics of the medication (such as shape, size, color, and texture) via a camera. This real-time captured data is cross-validated against an existing drug database and the prescription details. The system is programmed to alert the user instantly when discrepancies are detected, thereby providing a robust technical solution for improving accuracy and safety in drug dispensing.

title: An Adaptive Approach for Duck-Curve Enhancement Under Temporal and Spatial Divergence on Battery Swapping EV Services
authors: Yuka Tsushima, Tetsuya Shigeyasu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Recently, the development of photo-voltaic power generation (PV) and electric vehicles (EVs) has led to the emergence of the duck-curve in net demand. This unwanted duck-curve phenomenon increases the power generation cost at conventional power plants, and has attracted worldwide attentions as an urgent issue. Distributed energy resources (DER) have typically been utilized as a major solution to mitigate duck-curve effects. We have also investigated the use of battery swapping Electric Vehicles (BSEVs) as DERs to address this problem. Nevertheless, our previous studies revealed that simply utilizing BSEVs still has limitations in suppressing short-term fluctuations in net demand. To overcome this issue, this paper proposes a novel adaptive approach to duck-curve enhancement under temporal and spatial divergence in BSEVs by introducing both an adaptive Proportional-control (P-control) mechanism and a power-relocation strategy.

title: Potential of Human Mobility Data in Capturing the Actual Usage Patterns of Public Parks
authors: Tokuko Kawasaki, Fumitake Fujii, Mamiko Koshiba
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study examined the potential of utilizing human mobility data collected via smartphones as method for understanding usage patterns in local-authority-managed parks. The analysis revealed that incorporating information on park users' residential locations and lifestyle characteristics enables a clearer understanding of actual usage conditions and provides insights into strategies for promoting future utilization.

title: Comparison of Performability Analyses for Web-Based Oss by Deep Multimodal and Multitask Learning
authors: Yoshinobu Tamura, Shan Jiang, Shigeru Yamada
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: We compare the methods of OSS performability evaluation by the deep multimodal learning and multitask one. This paper focuses on the Web-based OSS such as cloud computing, edge computing, and Web server. Also, several practical execution examples based on the proposed deep models by actual big fault data. Furthermore, this paper discusses several deep multimodal and multitask learning for the OSS performability analyses.

title: Attention-Enhanced Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network for Lung Cancer Classification in CT Scans
authors: Arnav Mahajan, Nagendra Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Lung cancer is the most prevalent cancer worldwide, resulting in more than 1.8 million deaths per year. Early and accurate diagnosis through medical imaging is essential for increasing survival rates in patients. In present paper, we compare lightweight deep learning architectures for classifying lung cancer using medical imaging in CT-SCAN. The baseline MobileNetV1 model is presented along with its attention-enhanced versions: MobileNetV1 + SE (Squeeze-and-Excitation) and MobileNetV1 + ECA (Efficient Channel Attention). All models were evaluated on a publicly available Kaggle dataset, with all models trained with the same hyperparameters for comparisons. The results indicate that including attention mechanisms resulted in the marked improvement in classification accuracy and interpretability of features. The SE and ECA models achieved 95% accuracy and the best weighted F1-scores overall, with Grad-CAM visualizations demonstrating better attention paid to tumor regions of the CT-SCAN. In conclusion, the present findings showed that attention-based, lightweight CNNs achieved high accuracy and performance whilst utilizing a lens of interpretability through attention visualizations of features, suggesting they form a promising platform for real-time medical diagnostic deployable applications.

title: Flicker Noise Estimation of Pocket Doped NCFET in Existence of Interface Traps and Temperature
authors: Malvika Malvika, Jagritee Talukdar, Bijit Choudhuri, Kavicharan Mummaneni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The influence of noise on the operation of nanoscale arrangement is significant when it concerns the reliability of semiconductor devices. At lower frequencies, flicker noise dominates and affects various performance metrics of the device. This work explores the investigation of flicker noise of highly doped double pocket double gate NCFET (HDDP-DG-NCFET) in existence and non-existence of interfacial trap charges. The influence of Gaussian donor interfacial trap charges (ITC) at the silicon/oxide boundary has been taken into account. Furthermore, the impact of temperature fluctuations (200K-400K) is considered for various noise parameters such as gate voltage and drain current noise power spectral density (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">vg</inf> and S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">id</inf>). It has been observed that, at lower temperatures the existence of ITC adversely affects the noise power spectral densities. However, as temperature rises, the influence of ITC becomes insignificant, but noise performance deteriorates significantly.

title: A Survey report on Tunnel Field Effect Transistors (TFET) based Biosensors, its internal Physics and future advancements
authors: Sahil Hamid, Soumya Sen, Imon Shahriar, Pankaj Kumar, Nipun Sharma, Abhishek Chauhan, Ajay Kumar, Ashish Raman, Mohit Kumar Sharma, Malik Mubasher Hassan, Subarna Mondal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The Field Effect Transistor (FET) Biosensor, or BioFET, has attracted the attention of academics for its transformative impact on the medical field. From reduced power to cost, including the identification of biomolecules using label-free approaches. The compatibility of the BioFET with CMOS technique makes it suitable for fast, rapid and next gen sensor applications. However various complications occurs in FET based sensors due to short channel effects at advanced technological nodes and subthreshold swing limitation by which it cannot be reduced below the 60 mV/dec threshold. The Tunnel Field Effect Transistor-based biosensors address the aforementioned challenges with superior capabilities, effectively managing sensitivity and power dissipation problems that have been significant drawbacks for previous technologies. This study will provide a comprehensive overview of TFET biosensors, including their analysis and prospective enhanced approaches for their development and production, aimed at enhancing performance while ensuring cost-effectiveness.

title: COMSOL-Based Material Optimization of MEMS Capacitive Pressure Sensors for Biomedical Use
authors: S.Teena Mrudula, K.E.Srinivasa Murthy, Rayala Bhavya, Rakesh Bhavanasi, Shaik Sai Sadhik, Bhargav Alla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The project presents the simulation and material optimization of a MEMS-based capacitive pressure sensor for biomedical applications using COMSOL Multiphysics. This study analyzes the major effects of diaphragm material, geometrical representation, and packaging stress on the sensor performance parameters. These include displacement, capacitance variation, sensitivity, and thermal stability. These characteristics have been evaluated under varying biomedical pressure conditions. The diaphragm displacement, capacitance change, sensitivity, and thermal effects are studied under different conditions using simulation. The results will inform sensor optimization for reliable biomedical usage. The final results provide insights for optimizing sensor design to achieve high sensitivity, stability, and reliability in wearable and implantable biomedical devices.

title: Multi-band EEG Feature Optimization for Sleep Deprivation Classification
authors: Syeda Hina Gillani, Shcharyar Khan, Sadam Hussain Noorani, Aamir Arsalan, Sanay Muhammad Umar Saeed, Muhammad Tariq Javed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: Sleep deprivation affects memory, attention, emotional regulation, and overall health, creating a need for reliable detection methods. This study presents an EEG-based classification framework using data from 71 participants (2 Sessions). Following pre-processing, features were derived from the frequency bands, together with diverse nonlinear features, yielding a High dimensional feature space. The mRMR method was subsequently employed to select the most relevant features, and the highest-performing subset was retained for further analysis. Among the evaluated classifiers, XGBoost with mRMR-selected features achieved 97.83% accuracy close to the 97.91% accuracy of the full feature set demonstrating the effectiveness of optimized feature selection for accurate sleep deprivation detection.

title: MLRFC-MIFSA: A Hybrid Feature Selection and Classification Method for Sustainable IoT and WSN Applications
authors: Mai Hussain Ahmed Hussain, Mohamed R.M. Rizk, Ali Gaber M. Ali
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are frequently employed in Internet of Things (IoT) applications. However, their limited battery life makes energy efficiency a major concern. This study presents MLRFC-MIFSA, a machine learning framework for selecting the best features. It reduces communication costs, saves energy consumption, and improves predictive performance by sending only the most relevant features. The framework is tested with benchmark datasets from Kaggle and the UCI Machine Learning Repository in five experimental scenarios. It is compared with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6}$</tex> leading methodologies from prior research. The findings indicate that MLRFC-MIFSA consistently achieves superior accuracy, precision, and recall with fewer features. These findings highlight its superiority over existing approaches and its appropriateness for energy-constrained IoT and WSN applications.

title: Leveraging Integrated Knowledge for Enhanced Multimodal Lung Cancer Prediction
authors: Rodina Ahmed, Mohamed Abo Rizka, Noura Elmaghawry
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Accurate lung nodule assessment requires integrating imaging features with clinical context. This paper focuses on the multimodal training component of the broader proposed framework, which unifies multi-source data curation, a medical vision-language model (VLM), a domain knowledge graph, and inference. The paper presents a VLM that jointly predicts nodule malignancy, attenuation texture, and size from 3D CT (Computed Tomography) patches' intensity and segmentation mask, and leakage-safe textual radiological cues. The architecture couples a 3D vision encoder with a clinical text encoder, fuses modalities through multi-layer cross-attention, and employs task-specific prediction heads. The attenuation head is further refined by ROI features and an auxiliary solid versus subsolid signal. Trained and evaluated on the LIDC-IDRI dataset, the model achieves high performance: for malignancy prediction, with AUC 0.9619, balanced accuracy 0.8813, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 10.8784$</tex>; for attenuation texture, balanced accuracy 0.9513 and F 10.8654; for size regression, MAE 0.76 mm with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2} 0.95$</tex>. These results demonstrate that cross-modal fusion with controlled text features enhances discrimination, texture characterization, and size estimation within a unified network. This model provides a robust foundation for reliable, multi-attribute nodule assessment and subsequent knowledge-graph reasoning.

title: Deterministic and Fault-Tolerant Embedded Systems With Edf-Pi and Tdma-Can
authors: Wael Badawy
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Safety-critical embedded systems in modern industries demand architectures that ensure both functional correctness and strict temporal reliability, even under unpredictable workloads and fault conditions. These systems must remain available despite transient and permanent failures that naturally arise in real-world environments. Applications such as automotive electronic control units, unmanned aerial vehicles, and medical devices all depend on meeting deterministic deadlines while sustaining resilient communication. This paper proposes a unified framework that integrates Earliest Deadline First scheduling with Priority Inheritance (EDF-PI) and a Time Division Multiple Access overlay applied to Controller Area Networks (TDMA-CAN). The integration addresses two longstanding limitations: uncontrolled priority inversion at the scheduling layer and nondeterministic arbitration delays at the communication layer. The architecture was implemented on STM32 microcontrollers running FreeRTOS and validated using both hardware experiments and large-scale simulations with fault injection. Results demonstrate that EDF-PI reduced deadline misses by up to 42 % compared to plain EDF, while the TDMA layer reduced latency variance by 73 % compared to arbitration-based CAN. Availability exceeded 98.6 % under sustained fault scenarios. These findings confirm that integrating dynamic task scheduling, inversion control, deterministic communication, and fault recovery into a single framework significantly enhances the reliability of distributed embedded systems.

title: An Artificial Intelligence–Driven Framework for Early-Stage Skin Cancer Detection and Performance Evaluation
authors: Rashmi Sinha, Anil Kumar Sagar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Skin cancer has been considered among the most prevalent and dangerous diseases in the world where the study on the diagnosis of the disease at the tender age has been paramount in increasing the survival of the patients and reducing the depth of the treatment procedure. The traditional approaches to diagnosis have much emphasis on the visual examination and clinical expertise that might produce the inter-observer inconsistency and claim slow diagnosis particularly during the early stages. To eliminate these limitations, the present study will suggest the idea of Artificial Intelligence-Based Framework of Skin Cancer Detection and Performance Assessment in the Early Stage. The specified framework is an integration of systematising image processing approaches, feature detecting, enhanced with the assistance offered by a deep learning framework with the help of a convolutional neural network, and a proper classification system to distinguish between exactly malignant and benign skin lesions. The model is trained and evaluated on dermatoscopy images datasets and compared to each other based on the clinically significant measures of performance, including accuracy, precision, and recall. The results of the experimentation have revealed that the proposed framework boasts of excellent diagnostic accuracy and possesses a large degree of precision and sensitivity thereby reducing the amount of false positives and false negatives. The usefulness of proposed solution is also confirmed by the performance of the performance in comparison with the traditional machine learning and the standard deep learning models. The inferences imply that the framework may be deployed as the effective decision support tool that will assist the dermatologist to detect the skin cancer in the earliest stage and therefore, contribute to the overall clinical outcomes.
