title: Panoptic Segmentation for Intelligent Fishing Port Environment Recognition Using Mask2Former
authors: Yu-Mou Liu, Yi-Zeng Hsieh
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: With the rapid development of intelligent technologies, automation in fishing port operations has become an important direction toward realizing smart fisheries. However, the fishing port environment is highly complex and dynamic, posing challenges to the perception and navigation capabilities of Autonomous Guided Vehicles (AGVs). To address this issue, this study proposes a Mask2Former-based fishing port environment recognition system that integrates semantic and instance segmentation tasks through panoptic segmentation, enabling effective identification of various “thing” and “stuff” objects in fishing ports, such as personnel, baskets, and roads. The system is trained on a self-collected fishing port image dataset and compared with methods such as Panoptic-FPN and MaskFormer. Experimental results show that Mask2Former performs excellently in metrics including Panoptic Quality (PQ), Segmentation Quality (SQ), and Recognition Quality (RQ), particularly demonstrating advantages in recognizing small objects and handling complex backgrounds. Although its inference speed is slightly slower, it provides more precise segmentation results, making it highly promising for automation applications requiring high accuracy.

title: A Novel Hybrid Content Delivery Method Based on the Content Request Pattern on NDN-MANET
authors: Ayumi Akahori, Tetsuya Shigeyasu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: A MANET (Mobile Ad Hoc Network) is designed for environments where no fixed communication infrastructure is available. In a MANET, portable devices such as smartphones communicate with one another using wireless interfaces, enabling multi-hop end-to-end communication. Because of its independence from fixed infrastructure, a MANET is expected to improve end-to-end communication reachability in emergency situations where pre-installed communication systems are damaged by disasters. However, a MANET consists solely of battery-powered portable devices and therefore suffers from limited operational lifetime due to energy constraints. This shortcoming significantly degrades the reliability of MANETs in emergency scenarios. Hence, this paper discusses how to extend the lifetime of a MANET in which all nodes are battery-powered and proposes a novel hybrid content delivery method. The proposed method optimizes communication by selecting an appropriate delivery strategy between push-based and pull-based approaches based on the content request patterns.

title: Smart Water Meter Analytics: Consumption Pattern Mining, Leak Detection, and Short-Term Forecasting
authors: Khadar Mahdi, Ming-Sung Wang, Showkat Ahmed Bhat, Ming-Che Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive, data-driven case study of a single residential smart water meter using a high-frequency dataset. The study's methodology includes robust data preparation to parse non-standard timestamps, exploratory data analysis to identify consumption patterns, a targeted anomaly detection algorithm to identify leaks, and the development and comparison of machine learning models for both feature analysis and forecasting. Our results reveal distinct daily usage patterns and weekly consumption patterns. Most critically, our leak detection model identified non-zero water flow during “deep sleep” hours (1:00 AM-4:59 AM) on 91.4% of the analyzed days, providing strong evidence of a persistent physical leak. The forecasting component evaluated LightGBM across three training windows: 31, 180, and 365 days, showing that extending the window significantly improves accuracy. LightGBM trained on the full 365-day window achieved the best performance, with an MAE of 0.053 and an RMSE of 0.155. Finally, our modelling shows that recent behaviour (e.g., lag_1_day) is the most powerful predictor of consumption, and sliding window forecast models can reliably predict daily usage, providing a strong baseline for demand planning.

title: Layer-Encoded VR Stream Provisioning Over Massive MIMO Networks
authors: Wen-Hsing Kuo, Ming-Jin Hsu, Hsiao-Chun Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Wireless multimedia streaming services represent a key application for next-generation networks, enabling users with head-mounted goggles to experience immersive binocular viewing. Delivering customizable wireless streaming to multiple devices in specific areas addresses emerging entertainment needs in future cinemas, theme parks, and museums. To ensure high-quality coverage for group multimedia enjoyment, technologies like 5G/6G offer a viable solution. However, integrating physical transmission with streaming requires a cross-layer scheme for flexible and efficient radio resource management across multiple user devices. This paper introduces a novel problem: provisioning binocular streaming to devices (e.g., VR goggles) via efficient allocation of resources from a Massive MIMO antenna array. Considering each user's bandwidth demands, available resources, and channel characteristics, the system optimizes transmission allocation to maximize overall utility. Simulation results demonstrate outstanding performance of our scheme.

title: An Application Calculating Contact Frequency to Promote Interpersonal Interaction
authors: Ryoya Inaba, Kazuaki Sugiura, Takahiro Yamaguchi, Teruaki Kitasuka, Toru Nakanishi, Zhuotao Lian
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Establishing new social connections in daily life is often hindered by psychological barriers. Although online communication services may reduce these barriers, they typically require personal information to be transmitted to external servers, raising privacy concerns. Existing decentralized encounter detection systems such as Exposure Notification focus on infection tracking and still involve indirect data sharing, which may lead to public distrust. We propose a decentralized smartphone-based encounter detection system that promotes interpersonal interaction without external data sharing. The system periodically generates time-varying pseudonymous identifiers, called nicknames, using a key derivation function to prevent long-term tracking. Encounter verification is achieved using Private Set Intersection (PSI), which enables users to identify shared encounters without revealing unrelated contact history. To prevent impersonation attacks, ring signatures are employed to authenticate nicknames while preserving anonymity. In addition, a nickname list transfer mechanism is introduced to efficiently detect known contacts with reduced communication overhead.

title: A Study on Preparing Disaster Electricity Source by Infrastructure-Centric Energy Refilling on Battery Swapping EV Services
authors: Mayu Hatamoto, Tetsuya Shigeyasu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: It is important to stock electricity for emergency situations during ordinary days. For this purpose, Battery Swapping Stations (BSSs) with sufficiently charged spare batteries for Battery Swapping Electric Vehicle (BSEV) services work as distributed electricity resources (DERs). Commonly, an EV with a depleted battery changes it at a BSS selected by the EV itself. However, it is difficult to adjust the State of Charge (SOC) to the desired level under such a user-centric battery swapping approach. Hence, this paper proposes a novel BSScentric battery swapping method in which a BSS broadcasts battery-change requests. Performance evaluations confirm that the BSS-centric battery-change requests effectively increase SOC levels and clarify that this approach contributes to building a flexible emergency infrastructure for electricity storage.

title: A Study of Fully Autonomous UAV Based on Singleagent and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wei-Tsong Lee, Zi-Qi Ying, Hsin-Wen Wei, Hao Hsu, Chia-Jung Chuang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and autonomous control technologies, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have been widely utilized in applications such as agricultural inspection, reconnaissance, environmental monitoring, and mountain rescue. This study aims to develop an autonomous UAV system designed for mountain search missions, utilizing a reinforcement learning framework for training. The single-agent Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm is employed to achieve stable flight control and obstacle avoidance, while the Multi-Agent Posthumous Credit Assignment (MA-POCA) algorithm enables cooperative exploration and collision prevention among multiple drones. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed observation and reward design, based on the reinforcement learning framework, enables drones to perform autonomous and highly efficient cooperative search missions in complex terrains.

title: UAV-Based Early Detection of Mosquito Larvae on Water Surfaces from Limited Training Images
authors: Gaku Masuda, Kentaro Kumagai, Thomson Ngumbira, Dylo Foster Pemba, Hitoshi Kawada
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: If water surfaces are left untreated, mosquito larvae can develop into adult vectors within a few weeks, posing a significant risk of infectious disease. Consequently, technology for weekly on-site detection of mosquito larvae is required to control such situations effectively. Using autonomous UAVcaptured images based on water surface mapping, this study establishes a technological foundation to detect mosquito larvae using deep learning, targeting automated larval source management for vector control. To enable local entomologists to build and adapt models using free, resource-limited cloud services due to economic constraints, determining the minimum viable training dataset is essential. Therefore, we built an object detection model using Google Cloud Platform's Vertex AI with an extremely limited training dataset (8 images, 300 annotations) to evaluate feasibility under such resource-constrained conditions. The model was tested on two external datasets: controlled indoor images (JIHS, Japan) and field photographs from natural breeding sites (Malawi). Results demonstrated that while the model achieved high recall (75–100%) and moderate F1 scores (0.64) in controlled environments, precision dropped significantly to approximately 10% in turbid field conditions due to false positives from environmental noise, such as floating particles. These findings indicate that dataset diversification incorporating diverse backgrounds is essential for real-world deployment.

title: Evaluating Emotion-Adaptive Non-Player Characters: Player Perception, Immersion, and Systemic Challenges in Sentiment-Driven Interaction
authors: Amr Mamdouh, Leila Alsharkawy, Faris Sharara, Doaa Shawky
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: A central challenge in game design is maintaining immersion and emotional engagement, yet non-player characters (NPCs) in most genres still rely on rigid, predetermined dialogue or behavior trees. This limitation reduces believability and makes interactions feel mechanical rather than dynamic. Narra-tive-driven games provide a particularly suitable testing ground, as dialogue and character interaction are central to the experience. This study investigates whether adapting NPC behavior to the emotional tone of player input enhances immersion and engagement in interactive narratives. Two contrasting systems were developed: a static NPC with predetermined dialogue trees and an emotion-adaptive NPC that adjusted responses based on cumulative emotional scoring from player choices. A within-subjects experiment with 36 participants evaluated player preferences, immersion levels, and perceived believability. Results show that 86.1% of participants preferred emotion-adaptive NPCs, with significantly higher ratings for immersion (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=5.1$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SD}=1.2)$</tex>, believability <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{M}=5.0, \text{SD}=1.4)$</tex>, and emotional engagement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(M=5.1, S D=1.5)$</tex>. Qualitative feedback revealed that players valued unpredictability and emotional responsiveness, though technical issues occasionally disrupted the experience. These findings demonstrate the potential of emotion-aware NPC systems to enhance interactive storytelling while highlighting implementation challenges for future research.

title: End-to-End Deep Learning Framework for Video Super-Resolution
authors: Amarah Abdullah, Vardah Mazhar, Shelva Shareen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: The quality content of video content is on a rapidly growing demand due to the development of digital display and streaming structures. Nonetheless, a substantial part of the video content is of low quality because of the old recording technology and bandwidth constraints. Video Super-Resolution (VSR) is a technique that fills this gap by restoring high-resolution (HR) frames using low-resolution (LR) ones. Although deep learning has made enormous progress towards VSR, it is clear that the current approaches frequently fail to achieve the consistency in time and efficiency. In the current paper, we suggest Deep Residual Spatio-Temporal Network (DRSTN) an end-to-end architecture that is going to be effective in extracting features across consecutive frames. Our architecture has used 16 ResBlocks and global residual learning on the backbone to learn fine-grained textures. On the Vimeo-90K Septuplet data, our framework obtains a Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) of 31.52 dB which is higher than the usual bicubic baselines. We also explain how VSR architectures have improved [by becoming CNNs and then Transformers], and the essential ethical issues such as privacy and dataset bias.

title: Experimental Analysis of Mini-Slot-Based Latency Reduction in 5G NR
authors: Mukesh Kumar Kurdiya, Indu Saini, Neetu Sood
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Although the 5G radio access network is utilised for mission-critical applications with low latency requirements, delay caused by resource allocation, scheduling, retransmission, and protocol processing limits the low latency achievement. This paper introduces a technique for optimising the low latency further on the basis of a simulation-based experiment for comparative analysis further between enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB) and Ultra-Reliable Low Latency Communication (URLLC) use cases. In particular, URLLC further suggested using a modified frame structure. The test was performed for both ingress gNB to User Equipment (UE) and egress UE to gNB. The analysis was conducted with a simulation using OpenAirInterface platform. Result shows that- 5G NR eMBB as a comparison baseline with modified mini-slot frame structure achieves improved latency resulting in significant improvement around 61.74%. The proposed approach indicates low latency benefit for real-time latency-sensitive applications like gaming and vehicle-to-vehicle communication, where data traffic is moderate, but the low latency requirement is critical.Decoding parameter optimisation is more instead of coding parameters, offering further study.

title: Decentralized Intelligence-Modeling Adaptive Security Through the WanderMind-Ledger Blockchain Architecture
authors: Junaid Nasir Qureshi, Muhammad Zaeem Zafar, Talha Maqsood, Aitizaz Ali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: WanderMind Ledger is a new version of the WanderMind AI Travel Concierge that incorporates the blockchain technology to guarantee transparency, trust, and security in the travel planning and booking process. On one side, WanderMind Ledger ensures immutability and verifiability through a system of smart contracts deployed on a blockchain network to automate the travel booking process, secure payments, and formalize the traveler-vendor transaction. On the other side, the personalization of the system is carried out by AI-based large language models LLM, which ensures immutability and verifiability of the transaction and user relations. Every operation, which includes flight bookings, hotel bookings, or user reviews, is authenticated and saved on-chain, which prevents manipulation of data and holds everyone responsible. Moreover, WanderMind Ledger allows users to obtain self-sovereign digital identities that enable users to have full access to the personal information and travel history and to promote the token-based loyalty schemes that can reward those traveling in a sustainable manner. Through this integration of AI and blockchain, WanderMind Ledger is offering a secure, autonomous, and verifiable travel ecosystem that revolutionizes the way one plans, books, and experiences travel in the digital age.

title: SceneFake Detection using Cepstral Features and LSTM Networks
authors: Sakshi, Mohit Dua
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The rapid advancement of synthetic audio techniques and manipulation of environmental sounds presents increasing risks to voice authentication systems. As a result, reliable identification of spoofed audio has become critical for ensuring the credibility of speech-based security solutions. This study investigates the SceneFake dataset, which focuses on audio recordings where background sounds are manipulated to appear realistic. Three cepstral feature extraction techniques, Gamma-tone Cepstral Coefficients (GTCC), Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC), and Linear Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (LFCC), are evaluated in combination with a Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) network for classification. Each feature type captures distinct spectral and temporal characteristics, while the LSTM effectively models time dependent patterns in speech signals. Experimental results show Equal Error Rates (EER) of 6.01% for GTCC-LSTM, 5.10% for MFCC-LSTM, and 3.08% for LFCC-LSTM. Among the evaluated methods, LFCC-LSTM achieves the best performance, demonstrating strong potential for detecting spoofed audio with manipulated background noise.

title: Edge-Intelligent Blockchain Framework for Ultra-Secure and Energy-Efficient Real-Time Patient Monitoring in IoMT Using Hierarchical Federated Learning
authors: Rana Hassam Ahmed, Muhammad Sarfraz Khan, Amirmohammad Delshadi, Naseer Ahmad, Majid Hussain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) provides the possibility to conduct continuous monitoring of health, perform intelligent diagnostics, and make a clinical decision based on data. Nonetheless, there are security, privacy, scalability, latency, and energy issues with large-scale deployment. Although Federated learning (FL) provides less exposure to data, and blockchain provides trust, current solutions that combine both blockchain and FL have high consensus overhead, fixed privacy, and adversarial resilience. To handle them, we present an Edge-Intelligent Hierarchical Blockchain-IoMT framework that integrates Hierarchical FL (HFL), Adaptive Differential Privacy (ADP), Lightweight Homomorphic Encryption (LHE), Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) authentication, and an Energy-Aware PoS with Edge Learning (PoS-EL) consensus. Hierarchical aggregation minimizes bottlenecks in communication. ADP minimizes security vs utility. ZKP achieves authentication and PoS-EL minimizes energy consumption. Experiments on real-world data demonstrate 99.21% accuracy of detecting anomalies, 34% decreased latency, 41% decreased energy usage, 52 percent lower blockchain overhead and 97 percent resistance to adversarial attacks, which justifies the framework in real-time, mission-critical IoMT systems.

title: A Comprehensive Study On FET-Based Gas Sensors
authors: Sai Tejeswar Reddy Talakanti, Ashish Raman, Abhishek Chauhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Gas sensors are commonly equipped in household safety, environmental control, clinical evaluation, and workplace hazard management. Although various gas sensors have been developed and reported, FET-based gas sensors are a good choice because they consume low power, have a small size and high sensitivity. Therefore, it is important to review FET gas sensors to understand the evolution of recent research trends. This paper reviews FET-based gas sensor technologies, special emphasis on thin-film, catalytic-gate, tunnel, suspended-gate, capacitively coupled, and GAA nanowire FETs. This paper surveys and summarizes recent progress in device engineering and optimization, explaining the device structure and functioning of FET-based sensors, along with the challenges and future scope.

title: Advances in Research and Development of Passive Internet of Things Communication
authors: Mahesh Mudavath, Sammaiah Thurpati, Panugoth Hussain, K.Suresh Babu, A.Satyanarayana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Passive Internet of Things (IoT) has emerged as a key enabling technology supporting industrial digital transformation and modernization. Owing to characteristics such as minimal cost, battery-free operation, and simple deployment, passive IoT communication methods particularly RFID have been widely adopted for asset tracking and management applications in recent years. Against this background, this paper reviews the latest advancements in passive IoT communication technologies, analyzes their potential future development trajectories, and discusses the major technical challenges and existing limitations. Furthermore, it proposes novel passive IoT communication solutions and identifies critical research directions aimed at achieving seamless integration with cellular networks, thereby providing new perspectives for the continued evolution of passive IoT systems.

title: Spatially-Aware Contrastive Representation Learning for CSI Fingerprint Indoor Localization
authors: Muhammad Junaid Tahir, Mu Zhou, Wilford Arigye, Muddasar Laghari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: WiFi channel state information (CSI) fingerprinting is a promising solution for indoor localization, but learning robust location representations from multi-access point (AP) measurements remains challenging due to heterogeneous AP observations and high similarity between neighboring reference points. This paper presents a multi-AP CSI localization framework that combines AP-specific convolutional encoding, attention-based feature fusion, and spatially guided contrastive embedding learning. CSI amplitudes from multiple APs are first processed through independent convolutional branches to preserve AP-dependent propagation characteristics, and the resulting features are adaptively fused based on an attention mechanism to form a compact representation. To obtain a more discriminative localization space, the network is trained with ArcFace, i.e., Additive Angular Margin Loss, together with a spatially weighted supervised contrastive objective that incorporates physical distances between reference points. This spatial contrastive design is the key novelty of the framework, as it encourages the learned embedding to preserve not only class separability but also the geometric structure of the localization space. During inference, reference-point scores are converted into continuous coordinates using probability-weighted top-(K) decoding. Experiments conducted in two real-world indoor environments, namely a corridor and a meeting room, demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively learns spatially consistent CSI representations. The system achieves mean localization errors of 0.30 m and 0.45 m, respectively, outperforming conventional CSI fingerprinting approaches.

title: A Comprehensive Study of Channel Height and Spacer Length Scaling in Nanosheet Field-Effect Transistors
authors: Chinta Rahul Reddy, Dharmendra Singh Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: A vertically stacked gate-all-around (GAA) nanosheet (NS) FET designed and analyzed the performance with effective channel width at a 5-nm technological node (N5). The device achieves a balanced trade-off between switching speed and power consumption, exhibiting a threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>) of 0.32 V, achieving high drive current capabilities (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>) while maintaining a minimized OFF-state leakage current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>) resulting in an I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio that exceeds the International Roadmap for Devices and Systems (IRDS) benchmarks by a factor of 4.1. The device also achieves better subthreshold performance of 60.8 mV/dec, which closely approximates the ideal fundamental limit subthreshold value of 60 mV/dec. The Sentaurus TCAD suite is used to analyze the performance of the device in which the channel heights (NS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">H</inf>) are varied from 3 nm to 7 nm. We also explored symmetric and asymmetric spacer studies by varying the spacer lengths from 3-7nm. The results highlights the critical role of channel height and spacer length engineering in nanosheet FETs for advanced nodes.

title: Digital DNA Sequences Mechanism and Malware Detection for Encrypted Images Using CNN
authors: Samar Sami Ahmed, Nada M. Abdel Aziem, Aliaa Youssif
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Data security nowadays became essential need to protect sensitive and important data from suspicious and unwanted interventions, malwares and phishing actions, especially after the modern technology era we witness and the emergence of malicious and effective attacks that jeopardize the security of data and information's whether for households or businesses. Cryptography technique emerged as a savior from the unwanted changes and malicious attacks through transforming data into a secure format. This study proposes an encryption algorithm for image that leverages Deoxyribonucleic Acid (DNA) method to produce a key sequence for security. The efficiency of the proposed algorithm has been tested and validated on group of images on a dataset; the validation test reveals the resistance of attacks using NPCR, UACI and Entropy. The results show high performance metrics, including an average NPCR of 99.6677 %, UACI of 33.49698 %, and an entropy of 7.99743, indicating strong security against various attacks. In addition to this study integrates Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) techniques to analyze images and detect malware, thereby increases the security of proposed DNA algorithm and reducing limitations.

title: AI-Driven Adaptive Selection Modes for Enhanced Performance in Fog-Assisted RANs
authors: Eman A. Anowr, Heba M. Abdel-Atty, Mohamed A. Mohamed, Mona Nashaat
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Fog-enabled Sixth-Generation (6 G) Radio Access Networks (RANs) are expected to support heterogeneous services, enhanced mobile broadband, ultra-reliable low-latency communications, massive machine-type communications, and legacy voice under tight energy and delay constraints across sub-6 GHz to sub-terahertz bands. However, existing mode-selection approaches rarely provide a lightweight, uncertainty-aware policy that jointly balances reliability, energy, and latency on edge hardware. We build an end-to-end, reproducible framework that integrates 3GPP TR 38.901 Urban Macrocell and Urban Microcell channels with probabilistic line-of-sight and non-line-of-sight, log-normal shadowing, and optional beamforming and atmospheric absorption. User-level statistics are obtained by Monte Carlo sampling with 95% confidence intervals. From signal-to-noise ratio, spectral efficiency, and capacity, we derive energy-per-bit and latency using mode-specific budgets for centralized, high-power-node, distributed, and device-to-device modes. Using these physicsgrounded metrics, we design a physics-to-policy hybrid Artificial Intelligence (AI) selector that combines a standards-driven rule, logistic-regression imitation, and a deep Q-network policy. On the evaluated dataset, the hybrid policy increases mean capacity by 8.2% over the centralized-RAN (C-RAN) baseline and reduces energy-per-bit and latency by 91% and 74.1%, respectively; the imitation module achieves 88.1% holdout accuracy. These results show that standardized propagation with uncertainty quantification and lightweight learning yields practical gains for energy-and delay-aware mode selection in fog RANs.

title: Survey of Emerging Trends in Artificial Intelligence for Bioinformatics
authors: Sahar I. Ghanem
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: This survey explores the revolutionary intersection of artificial intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT) devices, along with mobile health (mHealth) in bioinformatics. We examine the transition from static genetic information to multi-modal, dynamic health tracking systems that combine clinical records and real-time physiological data. The paper methodically discusses fundamental AI techniques, real-world implementation obstacles, and personalized medicine applications ranging from drug discovery to medical imaging. Three key obstacles to clinical adoption are identified by our research: model interpretability, computational scalability, and data privacy. While mHealth platforms show great promise for offering personalized healthcare findings through AI-driven bioinformatics, these limitations must be addressed in order to create clinically viable, secure, and equitable systems that can convert complex data into useful health interventions.

title: Eddy Current Probe Impedance Response to Residual Stress and Small-Scale Surface Roughness Variations
authors: Kalle Kinnunen, Raine Viitala
abstract: Surface roughness can create noise and errors in eddy current measurements. This study investigates the effect of small-scale roughness variations on the eddy current probe response, which are known to influence the current flow when the skin depth is close to the roughness height. Small roughness variations were made using silica carbide paper on two test pieces made from steel. Treated areas were measured using eddy current probe with frequencies ranging from 0.125 to 8 MHz. High frequencies and ferromagnetic test pieces lowered the skin depth close to the roughness height. Significant eddy current probe responses were observed in the treated areas. Changes in surface roughness were measured using the white light interferometer. The treatments also induce residual stresses and work hardening, which were estimated using X-ray diffraction measurements. Test pieces were annealed after the measurements to remove the residual stress and work hardening. Consequently, the response magnitudes were significantly reduced. The results implied that the residual stresses had the most significant influence, and the roughness changes themselves had a weak frequency-dependent effect on the probe response, which was observed after annealing. It was concluded that the influence of small-scale roughness variations itself is weak enough to be relevant only in specific high-accuracy applications.

title: A System Design for LEO Satellite Covert Communication Mechanism Against Multiwarden With Unknown Transmit Power
authors: Yanan Gao, Wen Tian, Rongrong He, Huaifeng Shi, Wanjun Wu, Sijie Lu
abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication, characterized by its low latency and extensive coverage, has become a necessary complement to 6G communication and Internet of Things technology. In order to enhance the security of LEO satellite communication, existing covert communication schemes often rely on assumptions such as single-warden known transmit power or channel state information but neglect the situation where there are multiple collaborative wardens who lack knowledge of the transmit power. To address this, we propose a system design for the LEO satellite covert communication mechanism (LCCM). Specifically, this LCCM can counter powerful multiple wardens who use a soft-decision fusion scheme-coordinated generalized likelihood ratio test, which allows distributed wardens to collaboratively detect transmission behavior without prior knowledge of Alice’s power by fusing each warden’s statistics at the fusion center, improving the robustness of wardens’ side detection and reducing the overall error probability. On the transmitter side, we propose strategies to estimate the covertness rate and enhance the throughput of covert communication. The simulation results confirm that the proposed system-level LCCM considerably outperforms single-warden detection, and the strategies can enable higher covert throughput while satisfying the covertness constraint.

title: A Semi-Supervised Physics-Based Deep Learning Model for Precise Fault Distance Prediction in Transmission Lines with Distributed Generators
authors: Pavan Kumar Bais, Narendra D. Londhe
abstract: Accurate fault distance estimation is essential for ensuring reliable protection of transmission systems, particularly in modern grids with high penetration of distributed generators (DGs). However, the diverse characteristics of DGs and their interaction with conventional grids alter power flows and fault characteristics, making fault distance prediction more challenging. Traditional methods, such as traveling wave, impedance based, and supervised machine/deep learning, rely on high sampling rates, precise line parameters, and manual feature extraction with fully labeled data. This study addresses these challenges by developing a physics-based deep convolutional neural network (DCNN) within a semi-supervised teacher–student framework to estimate fault distances in the IEEE 9-bus system incorporating DGs. The proposed model effectively extracts spatial and local temporal features, enabling accurate prediction even with limited labeled data. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance, achieving a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.8143 and a 26.15% reduction in loss using a physics-based regularizer in the proposed model. Extensive ablation studies further validate the model's robustness against noise, sensitivity to line parameters and load variations, DG interactions, wind speed intermittency, fault near sensing terminal, sampling rates, data window size, scalability on 39 bus system, computational comparison, and also benchmarking against existing approaches. Finally, the proposed framework is validated for real-time applicability using the OPAL-RT simulator and Raspberry Pi microcontroller, confirming its practicality for modern power system protection.

title: Prediction of Lower-Limb Kinematics Using Ground Reaction Force and Machine Learning Models
authors: Sithara Mary Sunny, Arun P Parameswaran
abstract: Machine learning (ML) advancements have made it possible to predict certain aspects of human locomotion. ANNs and other machine learning algorithms can be used to identify subject gait trajectories. The models that were built are the Multi-layer Perception (MLP) Neural Network, the Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and the Long-Term Short-Term Memory (LSTM) Model. The ground reaction force (GRF) was used by the models as an input constraint when they were attempting to make predictions regarding the kinematics of the lower limbs. For this study, data are analyzed with respect to the sagittal plane. To determine how accurate the models were, a statistic called root-mean-square error (RMSE), and Coefficient of determination (R2) were utilized. It was determined that the root mean square error (RMSE) was ≤5.5°, and the (R2) value was obtained ≥0.8. On evaluating the results, the LSTM had the best performance, followed by the MLP and the RNN. The models that were produced have the potential to be used in the development of assistive equipment for those who are disabled. These technologies will make it easier for the disabled to lead regular lives and will assist them in reintegrating into society, aligned with Sustainable Development Goals (SDG-3) of good health and well-being.

title: Safety-Critical Multi-Agent Flocking via Motion-Aware Control Barrier Functions_supp1-3695359.mp4
authors: Junyan Hu
abstract: This paper presents a safe and scalable control framework for heterogeneous multi-agent flocking using motion-aware control barrier functions. A nominal flocking controller is designed for cohesion and velocity alignment, while inter-agent separation and collision avoidance are enforced through pairwise safety constraints in local quadratic programs (QPs). Agent heterogeneity is explicitly considered, allowing for different physical radii and actuation limits. To reduce conservatism and computational burden, explicit reaction radii are derived for a distance-only safety activation policy, which characterise when pairwise safety constraints are automatically satisfied and can be removed from the QP. A motion-aware activation policy is then proposed that exploits both relative distance and approach velocity to activate safety constraints only when there is a potential collision risk. Simulations validate the effectiveness and scalability of the proposed framework, while real-world Crazyflie experiments demonstrate its practical feasibility.

title: High-precision OEO-based Respiratory Monitoring System with Agarose-coated FBG Sensor
authors: Tong Li, Qiuyi Shen, Tongtong Xie, Cheng Wang, Yu Wang, Hongyan Fu, Hao Chen, Daru Chen
abstract: In this paper, a respiration monitoring sensor based on an Agarose-coated fiber Bragg grating (AC-FBG) and an optoelectronic oscillator (OEO) is proposed and experimentally demonstrated, which achieves high sensitivity and high-speed respiration signal detection by combining fiber optic sensing and OEO-based demodulating technology. The proposed sensor captures subtle respiratory variations with exceptional precision. The experimental investigation reveals that the absolute sensitivity of the proposed sensor reaches 0.07391%RH, which is 19.9 times higher than that measured with the optical method. The response frequency of the proposed OEO-based demodulation system is more than 6.69 Hz. Owing to its excellent sensing performance, the sensor not only monitors the human body's regular breathing mode and special breathing modes (e.g., rapid respiration, deep respiration, and apnea), but also realizes the monitoring of the weak respiratory signals at a distance. The measured data from the respiratory monitoring system proposed in this paper show a high level of agreement with the ground-truth breathing data, which provides new technological means for the evaluation of respiratory function in a variety of key medical scenarios.

title: Numerical simulation study on the influence of Rutherford cable with parallel winding on the accuracy and uniformity of magnetic field
authors: Aihua Xu, Kaihong Wu, Chao Yu, Fuhai Cai, Yue Tang, Kunju Shi, Chen Yang, Yongsheng Wu, Yan Ding
abstract: Rutherford cables are commonly used in ultra-high field Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) magnet systems. In order to solve the problem of whether the toroidal Rutherford cable twist affects the accuracy and uniformity of magnetic field, the parameter equation of the toroidal Rutherford cable is constructed, and the calculation formula of the magnetic field of the toroidal Rutherford cable is derived. The magnetic field calculation results are verified by OPERA software. Secondly, the single-turn Rutherford cable coil model and parallel circular coil model are constructed, and the harmonic components of each order are obtained by left deconvolution method, and the harmonic and field diagram characteristics of the two are compared. The results show that the accuracy of the calculation method meets the application requirements, and the influence of strand transposition in Rutherford cables on the magnetic field uniformity is in the acceptable range. The research results provide a theoretical basis for investigating the uniformity loss caused by the three-dimensional structure deviation of superconducting coils.

title: Fuzzy-Logic-Based Coevolutionary Neural Dynamics for Nonconvex Optimization With Application
authors: Yun Liao, Jiazheng Zhang, Liangming Chen, Juntao Liu, Long Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Solving nonconvex optimization problems is crucial in scientific research and industrial applications. However, many real-world applications often encounter challenges in parameter selections and limited robustness. To address these issues, this paper proposes a fuzzy-logic-based coevolutionary neural dynamics (FLCND) algorithm for nonconvex optimization problems. Specifically, the proposed FLCND algorithm incorporates a coevolutionary mechanism and a fuzzy neural dynamics (FND) model. The constructed FND model employs a convergence parameter that can be adaptively adjusted to enhance the search efficiency and convergence of the proposed FLCND algorithm. Furthermore, the FND model exhibits noise tolerance, which enables the proposed FLCND algorithm to efficiently search the global optimum under constant and bounded random noises. Theoretical analyses are provided to prove the FLCND algorithm's noise tolerance and global convergence characteristics. In addition, the effectiveness of the FLCND algorithm is assessed through a series of numerical experiments. Finally, the FLCND algorithm is applied to two engineering optimization problems, including the design optimization of an I-beam and the manipulability optimization of a multi-manipulator system, demonstrating its practical applicability.

title: Intent Identification in VAT and Tax Policy Discussions Using a Hybrid Data-Driven Multi-Task Transformer
authors: Heera Patwal, Saurabh Dhanik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: A lot of unstructured social media data is created when people talk about Indian tax policies, like the VAT and Income Tax systems. Sentiment Analysis and Intent Recognition are treated as separate tasks in traditional NLP pipelines. This leads to insights that are not complete and extra computation. This study introduces a cohesive Multi-Task Learning (MTL) framework that concurrently predicts sentiment and intent from a singular tax-related tweet, utilizing a shared DistilBERT encoder with dual classification heads.Because there isn't enough domain-specific annotated data, we made a hybrid dataset of 149,594 instances by combining filtered real VAT/tax tweets with 100,000 synthetically generated samples. Experiments on a stratified test set (n = 14,960) show that the system works well, with macro F1-scores of 0.95 for Sentiment Analysis and 0.99 for Intent Recognition. The model is useful for automated grievance redressal and policy-feedback monitoring because it has high precision in actionable categories like Complaint (F1 = 0.99) and Query (F1 = 1.00).The findings indicate that hybrid data engineering, when integrated with multi-task transformers, provides an effective framework for extensive financial policy analytics.

title: Optical Reconfigurable Distributed Sources and Reflecting Surfaces for Distributed Optical Wireless Communications
authors: Haibo Wang, Hao Jiang, Bingcheng Zhu, Zaichen Zhang
abstract: Optical reconfigurable intelligent reflecting surface (ORIS), as a new type of programmable optical communication device, can deflect, split, and shape incident light, thereby intelligently reconstructing the optical wireless communication (OWC) environment. The application of ORIS has effectively expanded the application scenarios of OWC and improved system performance and robustness, which has attracted widespread attention. However, the existing ORIS is limited by its basic structure, and has problems such as low stability and poor signal quality in long-distance transmission. Aiming at the pain points of the existing ORIS, this paper proposes an optical reconfigurable distributed sources and reflecting surface (ORDSS) for distributed OWC. By arranging distributed sources in partial areas of the metasurface, users’ performance optimization is achieved based on power compensation and beam adaptive optimization algorithms. In this work, we performed physical modeling of an ORDSS-assisted OWC system, incorporating factors such as the characteristics and spatial distribution of ORDSS sources and reflection units, surface jitter of the ORDSS, pointing errors, and atmospheric attenuation. We derived the expression for the receiving performance of the ORDSS-assisted OWC system and compared it with that of conventional ORIS-assisted OWC systems to evaluate the performance gains brought by ORDSS. Building on the physical model and performance analysis, we investigated an adaptive power control algorithm for the distributed sources in ORDSS. In addition, we explored power compensation mechanisms based on ORDSS distributed sources to enhance system performance in mobile communication scenarios.

title: Analysis of the Interplay Between Nutrient Transport Processes and Oceanic Eddies in the Southern Java Sea
authors: Made Kamasan, Takahiro Osawa, Keiji Imaoka
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study examines coupled physical and biological variability in the southern Java Sea using Complex Empirical Orthogonal Functions applied to wind-forced transport, eddy kinetic energy, and chlorophyll-a. The study finds two distinct regimes. Inshore waters follow an upwelling driven cycle set by monsoon winds that strengthen alongshore transport and vertical motion, shoal the thermocline, and elevate chlorophyll, with eddies playing a secondary and often damping role. Offshore waters follow an advection driven regime in which chlorophyll co-varies with horizontal transport, the upwelling proxy loads weakly or with opposite sign, and an additional eddy dominated mode shows little biological imprint, indicating that energetic eddies can fluctuate independently of biomass. Together these results show that seasonal winds set the coastal productivity baseline while mesoscale processes redistribute and export production to the open ocean. The framework clarifies coastal-offshore coupling and supports practical monitoring of wind anomalies and transport to anticipate blooms and fishery conditions.

title: Explainable Memory-Based Malware Detection with Time-Aware Features and Ablation Insights
authors: Amr M. El-Husseiny, Ahmed Abdelrahman, Ahmed M. Mattar
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: This research introduces an improved framework for identifying obfuscated malware, incorporating innovative time-based features, Explainable AI (XAI), and statistical validation. Our method uses CIC-MalMem-2022 dataset which closely mimics polymorphic threats. Several machine learning algorithms were tested-Random Forest, and XGBoost proved to be the most effective. The proposed pipeline framework includes: data preprocessing, feature engineering, cross validation, ablation study, and finally SHAP (SHapley Additive explanations) validation, results in optimal performance. The design of time-based features (differences, rolling means, Zscores) computed within malware categories is key to classify polymorphic malware more efficiently. Stratified 5-fold crossvalidation was used. The culmination of all these phases in the proposed framework resulted in near-perfect accuracy (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 99.99 \%$</tex>) which demonstrates robustness and overfitting-free results. Feature-category classification, reinforced by ablation study demonstrates that handle-related features alone achieve near-optimal performance; <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 99.97$</tex>, the classification reduces dimensionality while maintaining efficiency and interpretability. The framework provides a strong, understandable, and sound design to classify malware, which leaps malware analysis a huge step forward.

title: Two-Dimensional Frontal Yoga Pose Assessment Method Based on Hybrid Structural Similarity
authors: Cing-Jyun Chen, Tzu-Yi Hung, Yu-Cheng Huang, Irin Tri Anggraini, Yanqi Xiao, Nobuo Funabiki, Chih-Peng Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: To address the limitations of high-cost threedimensional (3D) models and noise-sensitive two-dimensional (2D) angle-based methods in home self-practice yoga systems, in this work, a lightweight 2 D assessment methodology is proposed to optimize the frontal view movements. By focusing on common balance and hip-opening poses, OpenPose is used for keypoints extraction and the global normalization is applied to handle the various body types. Crucially, the “local structural similarity” metric within a hybrid scoring function is also introduced to overcome the shortcomings of comparison by pure angles. The experimental results demonstrate that this approach effectively mitigates the detection jitter in front-view scenarios, quantifies geometric symmetry accurately, and offers a robust and computationally efficient solution for self-practice yoga systems.

title: Impact of Back Barrier Layers on the Performance AlGaN/GaN HEMTs
authors: Ravi Jyoti, Nitesh Kashyap, Ashish Raman, Soumya Sen, Nipun Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This work provides a comparison between AlGaN/GaN HEMTs using an AlGaN, InGaN, or Fe-doped AlGaN back barrier layer based on published device data and results. The addition of a back barrier layer modifies the conduction band structure and improves the carrier confinement in the channel, hence improving the overall performance of the device. A comparison is carried out on the basis of reported figures of drain current, transconductance, and cut-off frequency. Devices with AlGaN back barrier layer show a balanced and moderate improvement, showing 400 mA/mm drain current and 15.2 GHz cut-off frequency. Those with InGaN back barrier layer exhibit superior radio-frequency performance due to strong polarization-enhanced electron confinement, attaining 450 mA/mm drain current, 200 mS/mm transconductance, and a 25 GHz cut-off frequency. The maximum cut-off frequency ever observed with Fe-doped AlGaN back barrier layer reaches as high as 190 GHz under a 2 µm gate length, with improved electrostatic stability due to effective electric field control. The results epitomize the fact that the selection of a specific back barrier layer depends on the application and becomes very crucial for tailoring the performance of AlGaN/GaN HEMTs toward high-frequency or high-reliability applications.

title: Early Prediction of Chronic Diseases Using Machine Learning
authors: Sameera Sadaf, Parul Agarwal, Bhavya Alankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Healthcare systems are strained by chronic diseases, which cause about 71% of deaths worldwide. Machine learning (ML) models utilizing EHRs, omics, lifestyle, and wearable data can identify pre-disease states months to years before clinical onset, contributing to the shift from reactive to predictive care. The current state of machine learning for early diabetes, heart disease, and hypertension prediction is summarized in this review. Strong discrimination is consistently achieved by deep learning and tree-based ensembles (such as XGBoost); several studies report accuracies/AUCs in the 0.90–0.98 range when rigorously trained and validated. This paper gives a comparative review of few past papers on techniques used, data source, method used of the key findings. We conclude with practical suggestions for models that are generalizable, explainable, and clinically useful. The results highlight the critical role that machine learning plays in enabling early diagnosis, customized treatment, and efficient chronic disease management.

title: Enhancing Smart City Transparency using Fuzzy Logic-Based Decision Dashboards and Data Visualization
authors: B.P. Joshi, Jaydeep Kishore, Shweta Goyal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Smart cities are an ongoing discussion among city key players. A smart city is a future urban way of living that people await. Because of the fast-paced and worldwide economy, several cities have initiated or are planning to begin smart city projects. Dashboards display an organized collection of information for a specific function, allowing people to observe what is occurring and take action. Dashboard development remains difficult and time-consuming, despite the advantages of the dashboard in a Smart city for enhanced transparency. Internet of Things (IoT) systems are forming an important infrastructural part of smart cities. Deep learning (DL) techniques have enabled it to be possible for machines to analyze and classify big data in recent years, as well as to generate trends that can aid decision makers. A robust hybrid system is produced by merging the structured feature extraction powers of DL with the interpretation and uncertain control of fuzzy logic. This combination addresses challenges in comprehending fuzzy, distorted, or unclear data, making it particularly helpful in complicated decision-making and forecasting situations. This paper proposes a dashboard-based smart city decision-making system that combines deep reinforcement learning (DRL) with fuzzy decision-making to maximize energy efficiency and improve transparency. With a RMSE of 14.8, a MAE of 12.1, and a MAPE of 2.2%, the fuzzy logic and DRL (FDRL) model outperformed other algorithms in forecasting energy production, usage, and transparency.

title: Texture Feature Descriptor Based Retinal Disease Classification using OCT Modality
authors: Sonam Singh, Sushil Kumar Saroj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Analysis of the retinal OCT layers is a crucial task for ophthalmologists to diagnose different eye disorders, such as choroidal neovascularization, diabetic macular edema, Drusen, and age-related macular degeneration. The analysis done manually of the retinal layers shows a great challenge for professinals work in the medical area because of the complex nature of the eye, the complexity of the retinal layer’s structure, and the difference between the layers. Recently, different automated techniques for the extraction of retinal layers have been proposed in recent studies, which provide great assistance to ophthalmologists to easily identify and diagnose eye disorders. This paper proposes an architecture that analyzes and tests the results of 33 existing pre-trained state-of-the-art machine learning architectures. The objective of the proposed model is to extract the detailed features from OCT scans and classify images using OCT images. The proposed architecture extracts features from the retinal OCT images using the SURF feature descriptor. After the experimental analysis, we used the pre-trained machine learning classifiers to identify the four retinal OCT layers’ related abnormalities, which are Choroidal neovascularization, Diabetic macular edema, Drusen, Age-related macular degeneration, and Normal retina. Finally, we obtained an accuracy of 99.1% with Cubic SVM using a resubstitution validation of 30%.

title: Design and Analysis of A Circularly Polarized Cross Dipole with Circular Ring Antenna for 5g NR46 and NR47 Frequency Bands
authors: Abhishek Mishra, Nitesh Kashyap, Ajay Kumar, Vineet Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper presents a circularly polarized cross dipole with circular ring antenna for 5G new radio (NR) applications covering the NR46 and NR47 frequency bands. The antenna employs a reflector-backed configuration with an optimized air spacing to improve radiation performance. Simulation results demonstrate strong impedance matching, with the reflection coefficient remaining better than -20 dB at the resonant frequency of 5.56 GHz and a -10 dB impedance bandwidth extending from 5.36 GHz to 5.71 GHz. The antenna achieves a peak realized gain of approximately 6.7 dBi at resonance while maintaining stable radiation characteristics and it have >97% efficiency. The proposed antenna maintains a <3dB axial ratio for whole band, indicating high polarization purity. The proposed antenna offers a simple structure, efficient polarization reconfigurability, and performance suitable for sub-6 GHz 5G NR wireless communication systems.

title: Deep Learning Based Face liveness detection with a Lightweight CNN and depth detection using MiDaS
authors: Niladri Das, Madhurendra Pratap Singh, Priyanka Biswas, Nirmalya Kar, Swanirbhar Majumder
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Face liveness detection plays an essential role in modern biometric authentication systems, especially as these systems are increasingly targeted by presentation attacks such as printed photographs, replayed videos, and high-quality screen displays. To address this challenge, this paper presents a lightweight and deployment-friendly face liveness detection approach that combines a compact convolutional neural network (CNN) with depth-based analysis. The proposed model operates on standard RGB images and requires only minimal preprocessing, making it suitable for real-time and latency-sensitive applications on consumer devices.The CNN is designed to effectively capture discriminative facial features that help separate real faces from spoofed ones, while monocular depth estimation is used to identify flat or non-3D facial artefacts commonly observed in spoof attacks. The system produces probabilistic outputs, enabling flexible threshold selection based on security requirements. Experimental evaluation is conducted on a dataset of 3866 samples, where the proposed approach achieves an overall accuracy of 93.59% and a ROC-AUC score of 0.9861. In addition, the model demonstrates strong and balanced per-class precision and recall for both real and fake classes. These results indicate that the proposed method offers a practical balance between accuracy, interpretability, and computational efficiency, making it suitable for real-world face authentication scenarios.

title: An End-to-End Methodology for Deploying PyTorch Models on Xilinx FPGAs: A VCOR Case Study
authors: Rithwik Vallabhan TV, Sreejeesh SG, Nanditha N Varma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper presents an end-to-end workflow for deploying Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) applications on Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs), with a focus on the ZCU104 evaluation board. Using the Vitis-AI 3.5 stack, we implement a ResNet-18 Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) trained on the Kaggle Vehicle Color Recognition (VCOR) dataset. The approach spans the entire development pipeline: configuring the host system via Docker, training the network in PyTorch, quantizing the model from floating-point to INT8, compiling the graph for the Deep Learning Processor Unit (DPU), and finally deploying the application on the target platform. We discuss practical implementation issues such as Docker setup, handling cross-compilation artifacts, and configuring IP networking. Experimental evaluation on the ZCU104 shows a maximum inference throughput of 421.05 FPS and a Top-5 accuracy of 99.0%, demonstrating the suitability of the Vitis-AI flow for edge-oriented computer vision workloads.
