title: DFFT-Net: dual-path feature fusion transfer network for power electronics lifetime prediction under diverse operating conditions
authors: Shuhan Deng, Hao Lan, Ke Yue, Zhuyun Chen, Zhicong Huang, Weihua Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Power MOSFETs, owing to their exceptional high-frequency switching characteristics, have become pivotal components for enhancing energy efficiency in charging stations. However, accurately predicting their remaining useful lifetime (RUL) under dynamic operating conditions remains a significant challenge. Due to limited consideration of multi-hierarchical features and data distribution variance, existing artificial intelligence (AI)-based methods have low accuracy and poor generalization. Herein, to address such issues, a dual-path feature fusion transfer network (DFFT-Net) is proposed for lifetime prediction of power MOSFETs. Specifically, the denoising autoencoder (DAE) unit is developed to eliminate data noise. Then, a dual-path feature fusion scheme is designed to create hierarchical deep fusion feature representation by the correlation between local sensitive features extracted by the convolutional neural network (CNN) branch and global trend features extracted by the Transformer branch. Finally, a conditional domain adversarial (CDA)-based unsupervised learning strategy is embedded to realize domain knowledge transfer cross-working conditions of power MOSFETs. Experimental validation is carried out on the NASA MOSFETs and SCUT MOSFETs datasets. The results show that average scoring function (SF) and root mean square error (RMSE) metrics of DFFT-Net are 4.18 and 0.20, which outperforms other advanced methods.

title: Force Control of an Underactuated Tendon-Driven Manipulator
authors: Nicolas J. S. Testard, Christine Chevallereau, Philippe Wenger
abstract: This article investigates the application of force controls on a tendon-driven underactuated robotic arm. A direct use of classical hybrid force/motion control based on computed torque control can lead to instability for this type of system. Reformulating the hybrid force/motion control using the pseudo-computed torque control (PCTC) framework leads to a stable control strategy. Finally, it is shown that applying admittance control in combination with PCTC also results in stable behavior when exerting a force against a wall or in a cobotics context. These results are then validated through experimentation.

title: A Semi-analytical Framework for Noncoherent Detection Performance Evaluation of Targets with Compound Fluctuations
authors: Huageng Liu, Xinliang Chen, Wei Ren, Quanhua Liu, Tao Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Evaluating radar detection performance for complex, non-stationary targets is challenging. To address this problem, we propose a semi-analytical detection performance evaluation framework for noncoherent radars that leverages the efficiency of analytical methods and the adaptability of statistical simulations. First, we formulate the framework by adopting a compound fluctuation model to decompose the detection probability into tractable conditional terms. Then, within this framework, we develop an adaptive semi-analytical (ASA) method, which uses adaptive importance sampling to efficiently approximate the conditional probability summation over the joint parameter space without exhaustively enumerating all parameter combinations. Next, we derive a theoretical upper bound on the estimation error of ASA, providing guarantees on its reliability. Finally, we validate the proposed framework and ASA through numerical experiments on representative scenarios and a real-data example, showing that ASA achieves lower root mean square error than conventional methods. The proposed framework and ASA thus provide an accurate and efficient tool for noncoherent detection performance evaluation in advanced radar system design.

title: AFGBStream: Adaptive Fuzzy Granular-Ball Framework for Stream Clustering with Sliding Windows
authors: Qijia Wang, Mingjing Du, Weiping Ding
abstract: Existing sliding window-based data stream clustering methods often struggle with fixed-granularity representations and delayed responses to abrupt pattern changes, leading to reduced adaptability and clustering accuracy. To address these limitations, we propose an <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>daptive <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">F</u>uzzy <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</u>ranular-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</u>all framework for <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Stream</u> clustering with sliding windows (AFGBStream), a novel sliding window-based data stream clustering framework that integrates fuzzy set theory with granular-ball computing to support adaptive and incremental updates. Specifically, a window-guided data granulation strategy is developed using a Fuzzy C-Means (FCM)-based granular-ball generation approach, which enhances representation robustness and reduces sensitivity to micro-cluster parameters. In addition, a window-aware temporal decay mechanism is employed to improve the model's responsiveness to evolving data by dynamically filtering outdated information. Our comprehensive experimental evaluation on 17 benchmark datasets demonstrates that AFGBStream achieves superior performance over five competing methods.

title: Novel Deep Learning Architectures for Soybean Kernel Defect Classification_supp1-3688499.pdf
authors: Dheeraj Kumar
abstract: Reliable identification of soybean kernel defects is essential for automated quality inspection in grain processing and export systems. However, current approaches face challenges when dealing with subtle defect patterns and heavily imbalanced class distributions. This study introduces a soybean kernel defect classification framework built on a high-resolution dataset of 9866 red, green, and blue images spanning eight defect categories, including underrepresented classes, such as purple-mottled and stained, stinkbug damage, and tear damage. To address performance degradation in rare categories, we propose a novel unified training pipeline that combines data augmentation and class-weighted focal loss. The proposed pipeline is evaluated using three deep learning architectures: EfficientNet-B0, ResNet-50, and the hybrid convolution–attention model CoAtNet0. Experimental results demonstrate that CoAtNet0 achieves the highest performance, with 83% accuracy and a 6%–9% improvement in F1-score over convolutional neural network baselines. gradient-weighted class activation mapping analysis further confirms improved localization of defect-relevant regions in CoAtNet-0, supporting stronger recognition of minority classes. Overall, this work highlights the benefit of combining hybrid modeling, imbalance-aware training, and visual interpretability to enable practical, scalable deployment for real-world soybean kernel defect inspection.

title: GCMamba: A Mamba-based Network with Graph Attention and Contrastive Learning for Remote Sensing Image Change Detection
authors: Bin Fu, Guo Cao, Boshan Shi, Youqiang Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In recent years, change detection in high-resolution remote sensing images has received widespread attention. However, due to the influence of complex scenes and differences in imaging conditions, pseudo-change interference is prevalent in images, which easily leads to false detections and affects subsequent applications. To address the problem, this paper proposes a novel change detection framework: GCMamba. The framework adopts Mamba as the backbone network to model global long-range dependencies and combines a Graph Attention Module (GAM) to optimize low-level feature distribution, thereby enhancing the ability to focus on changing regions. Furthermore, an Anchor-Guided Contrastive Learning Module (ACLM) is introduced to promote intra-class aggregation and inter-class separation of high-level semantic features through contrastive loss, effectively suppressing pseudo-changes caused by factors such as illumination and seasons. Experiments on five public datasets, LEVIR-CD, SYSU-CD, WHU-CD, CDD and DSIFN-CD, show that the proposed method outperforms current mainstream methods in multiple evaluation metrics, verifying its effectiveness and robustness in high-resolution remote sensing image change detection tasks.

title: Gaze-Foot Hybrid Upper Limb Assistive Robotic System: An Innovative Approach to Aid Individuals with Limb Impairments
authors: Bo Yang, Zejia Zhang, Yu Cao, Jun Huo, Jie Zuo, Xiling Xiao, Wei Luo, Jian Huang
abstract: Objective: Individuals with hemiparesis rely on assistive robotic systems to complete grasping tasks. Among the various modalities used for human-robot interaction, the eye-gaze movement has emerged as a promising approach due to its simplicity, intuitiveness, and effectiveness. However, gaze-based assistive robots still face limitations in ensuring effective human-robot interaction and grasping. Our objective is to design an assistive system capable of overcoming these limitations and enabling natural, intuitive control of assistive robots. Methods: In this study, we have designed a gaze-foot hybrid upper limb assistive robotic system that integrates gaze-based grasping intention recognition, object detection and localization, and foot pose-based grasping orientation control. This system is capable of recognizing user intentions through gaze and foot signals and executing grasping tasks to assist participants with hemiparesis in performing daily activities. Two typical daily tasks, assistive grasping and cooperative tasks, were identified to establish the workflow of the assistive robot system. Results: Eight healthy individuals and two Individuals with hemiparesis were recruited and the results reveal high success rates between subjects in assistive grasping (95.83%) and cooperative tasks (94.00%), with average task completion times of 30.41 seconds and 43.10 seconds, respectively.The comparative experiments quantitatively demonstrate the advantages of the designed gaze-foot hybrid system. Moreover, assessment through the System Usability Scale indicates the system's user-friendliness and ease of learning. Conclusion: The designed assistive system can help users effectively complete grasping tasks and is intuitive and user-friendly. Significance: The designed system offers a novel approach to assistive robotic control and can be applied to other robotic interaction control systems.

title: A Region-Wise based Tool for AC Islanded Microgrids Steady-State Security Assessment
authors: Wesley Peres, João Alberto Passos Filho, João Marcos R. Baccarini
abstract: This paper introduces a Static Security Region (SSR)-based method for the analysis of alternating current islanded microgrids, accounting for steady-state voltage and frequency variations, as well as droop control operation. The SSR is a set of two-dimensional graphics with axes representing the summation of power from loads, generation units operating under droop control mode, and Renewable Energy Resources-based generation units (RER). The principal contribution of thiswork is an Optimal Power Flow formulation designed to maximize the distance between the base case and the security border across a defined set of search directions. Comparative analyses conducted on two islanded microgrids reported in the literature show that the proposed method achieves an accuracy similar to traditional techniques, while delivering computational savings ranging from 20% to 65%. In addition, a probabilistic analysis is introduced to quantify the effects of load and RER generation uncertainties - modeled using typical probability distribution functions - on security margins, confirming that greater uncertainty leads to a smaller conservative security margin.

title: Building LLM-Based Voice Agents for Requirements Elicitation: An Experience Report on Early Prototypes
authors: Oshani Weerakoon, Tuomas Mäkilä, Erkki Kaila, Shola Oyedeji
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Workshop on Agentic Engineering
abstract: None

title: An Intelligent Skin Cancer Detection Model with Performance Analysis Using Accuracy, Precision, and Recall
authors: Rashmi Sinha, Anil Kumar Sagar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Skin cancer is among the most prevalent diseases and even life threatening in the globe, and thereby timely and accurate diagnosis in the disease is relevant in boosting the chances of survival of the victims. The classical approaches to professional diagnosis are largely dependent on the manual examination and dermatoscopy examination of dermatologists, which is largely biased, time- based and is limited by the availability of specialists. To overcome such drawbacks, this paper proposes an intelligent design of skin cancer detection based on artificial intelligence to classify the skin lesions in a functional and automatic way. The proposed framework integrates processing of images, lesion recognition, and application of deep learning to acquire features that prove helpful in the process of categorizing malignant and benign skin lesions. The discriminatory features of dermatoscopy images are generated automatically through the use of convolutional neural network and they are used to complete the process of classification. Performance, accuracy, precision and recall are clinically significant measures that are used to comprehensively evaluate the performance of the proposed model and this has rendered the proposed model a balanced and real assessment. Through experimentation, the proposed solution can be demonstrated to capture the progress of higher performance compared to the conventional machine learning and the generally better models of deep learning, and more particularly the recall, the most significant measure of reducing the missed cases of cancer. As per the findings, the proposed intelligent model can be used as a valid computer-aided diagnostic to diagnose skin cancer in the early stages and has great potential to be incorporated into the actual clinical decision support and tele-dermatology devices.

title: Optimizing 3D Object Detection with LiDAR-Camera Fusion Using a Single Backbone Approach
authors: Adnan Baig, Umair Muneer Butt, Imtiaz Hussain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: This paper presents an early-fusion approach for 3D object detection that integrates LiDAR and camera data through a unified backbone. Unlike dual-backbone designs, the proposed LiDAR-Camera Fusion (LCF-3D) framework directly extracts and fuses point-wise features from raw RGB images and point clouds, reducing computational overhead while preserving realtime performance. The pipeline first generates object proposals from LiDAR, projects them to image space to obtain regions of interest, and then uses a convolutional neural network (CNN) to extract multi-scale features for 3D box estimation. A voxelization stage aligns point cloud and image cues to reduce information loss. On KITTI, LCF-3D shows consistent gains across Easy, Moderate, and Hard settings, with the largest improvements in medium- and long-range detection. Overall, the framework offers an efficient and practical solution for multimodal 3D object detection.

title: Digital Twins Framework for Modeling Human Decision-Making
authors: Meenakshi Munjal, Aarfa Rajput, Nripendra Narayan Das, Deepak Pancholi, Neha Gupta, Mohit Garg
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Digital Twins(DTs) have emerged as significant cyber-physical systems to provide real-time mapping of physical environments into virtual environments. Although their abandonment in engineering, manufacturing, and healthcare domains is already rather well-developed, the use of DTs in the context of mortal decision-making remains an emerging field. A Digital Twin frame For decision-making attempts to reflect these dynamics by harmonizing artificial intelligence, behavioral modeling, and data-driven simulations. This paper presents arguendo base of such a frame, its armature and its implicit to maximize prophetic analytics, adaptive systems, and justified decision support. Through acquisition of real-time data aqueducts, cognitive modeling and machine literacy algorithms, the proposed frame will fill in the gap between mortal cognition and computational intelligence. The paper demonstrates, on the one hand, the issue of data sequestration, ethical, and interpretability of models; and, on the other hand, the potential of resources in healthcare, stand-alone systems, and the organizational strategy.

title: Multimodal Transport Integration in Maritime Logistics: A Barriers and Readiness Perspective for Sri Lanka
authors: Gayathri Katupitiya, Chathumi Kavirathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: In maritime logistics, multimodal transportation (MMT) is recognized as a sustainable solution for improving freight efficiency and port-hinterland connectivity. But in many developing countries, its integration is still limited. This study uses a survey-based approach where data were collected from related industry professionals to examine the significant barriers and present state of readiness for MMT integration within Sri Lanka's maritime logistics sector. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling was employed to test the impact of five barrier categories identified through a systematic review and an adapted Balanced Readiness Level (BRL) framework was employed to measure the current readiness levels across seven identified dimensions. To facilitate the phased implementation of MMT in Sri Lanka and other similar developing contexts, the findings offer practical recommendations for prioritizing investments and policy interventions.

title: A VR-Based Piano Practice Support System for Self-Learning Beginners
authors: Tsuyoshi Moriyama, Pham Hoang Giang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper presents a virtual reality (VR)-based piano practice support system designed for self-learning beginners, aiming to enhance STEAM education and life mechatronics within smart city contexts. The system integrates visual guidance, MIDI-based fingering assistance, and real-time performance feedback to mitigate the difficulties beginners face such as fingering acquisition and practice continuity. Comparative experiments with an existing learning tool, Synthesia, demonstrate that the proposed system significantly improves fingering accuracy and user motivation. The findings highlight the potential of VR technologies as effective educational tools in smart cities and also motivates future extension of the proposed preliminary technology to support musical practice.

title: Foreground-Background Separation Method for Noise Removal in Low-Illuminance Images
authors: Naruki Inoue, Yasuyuki Miura
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In the field of video processing, background subtraction is often required to separate the background from the foreground for applications such as video editing, motion recognition, and machine learning. In low-illuminance image recognition, the relative intensity of random noise to the signal value of the image is strong, so the problem is how to deal with the noise. Our research has been aimed at optimizing images in extreme low-illuminance conditions. In this paper, we propose and evaluate a noise/signal separation technique based on region extraction. It is shown that the proposed technique can properly remove noise and improve image quality.

title: VisionAid: An OpenCV-Based System Integrating Object Detection and OCR for Real-Time Scene Analysis
authors: Allyssa Grace Echevarria, Joan Kirstien Borja, Violdan Bayocot
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: People with visual impairments often face difficulty in understanding their surroundings, especially in dynamic or unfamiliar environments. This study presents VisionAid, a realtime scene interpretation system that integrates object detection and optical character recognition (OCR) with a context-aware natural language generation pipeline to produce spoken descriptions of the environment. Built using OpenCV, YOLOv8, and EasyOCR, the system processes images captured from wearable and mobile cameras. Unlike many existing assistive systems that focus primarily on either object detection or text recognition, VisionAid performs OCR within detected object regions, enabling targeted text extraction from semantically relevant areas of the scene. The detected objects and extracted text are integrated and passed through a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) module, which generates coherent natural language scene descriptions delivered through text-to-speech. Performance was evaluated under controlled and real-world conditions using mobile cameras and a low-cost ESP32-CAM module. Results show that the system maintained reliable performance across varying lighting and backgrounds when higher-quality cameras were used, while accuracy declined under constrained hardware conditions. These findings highlight practical trade-offs between performance and hardware cost and demonstrate VisionAid's feasibility as a hands-free assistive vision system.

title: Methodology for Building a Silver Arabic Corpus for Segmentation
authors: Hussein Awdeh, Mai Khalil, Omar Saadiyeh, Gilles Bernard, Mohammad Hajjar, Alaaeddine Ramadan
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: The aim of this paper is to present guidelines to build the best and biggest possible silver corpus for word segmentation in Modern Standard Arabic, which should be open data, the most verified and correct possible, and built with a clear and reproducible methodology. Such a corpus can help train machine learning models for segmentation. This paper will present such a corpus and the methodology used to build it in order to satisfy these requisites. Our survey shows that Kalimat is the only morphologically tagged corpus that can be used to initiate this process. We have converted a small part of it to a gold corpus, used it for selecting the best segmenters available, and show how to combine them in order to diminish the number of misses, thus providing a silver corpus freely available for scientific purposes.

title: A Unified Multi-Metric Optimization Framework for Equal and Unequal Random Clustering in VANET
authors: Rounish Chandra, Jalaj Biswas, Deepti Kakkar, Amrit Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: VANETs utilize clustering for scalable, reliable communication, but current approaches isolate performance parameters like energy, latency, and interference, resulting in frequent re-clustering and unstable cluster topologies. This research introduces a multi-metric clustering framework for VANETs, allowing for equal and unequal random cluster generation over a two-lane road layout. The technique blends per-cluster models for energy consumption, clustering time overhead, Signal-to-Interference Ratio (SIR), and end-to-end latency into a composite efficiency score for cluster head election and member association. Equal clustering uses a fixed cluster radius, whereas unequal clustering adapts cluster size to the distance from the roadside unit (RSU) to mitigate energy hotspots and enhance link reliability near infrastructure. Simulation results on a two-lane highway scenario show that the proposed equal and unequal random clustering schemes, driven by a unified multi-metric cost J, outperform a baseline VANET clustering protocol in terms of per-round energy consumption, end-to-end delay, and SIR. A quantitative comparison table further highlights the gains of the proposed framework over the baseline across these key performance indicators, emphasizing the benefit of using a unified multi-metric model for cluster formation and maintenance in VANETs.

title: Advanced Computational Intelligence for Satellite Imagery and Industrial Systems
authors: Kiran Singh, Rahul Singh, Rudra Kumar Sinha, Saijshree, Shobha Chaudhary, Sangita Ranjan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Industries powered by geoanalysis are changing with artificial intelligence and machine learning replacing image processing methods that have taken decades before they were developed in satellite data analysis. The current AI technologies are capable of processing large amount of high-resolution imagery faster and with better precision than ever before, allowing more sophisticated processes, namely object classification, object recognition, and pixel-by-pixel analysis. Combining AI with satellite images, organizations will be able to monitor the changes in the environment with greater accuracy, improve the disaster preparedness and response, as well as contribute to the creation of smarter and more sustainable cities. The presence of these technologies enables faster interpretation of visual sequences of space captured images, which enables detection of land-use change, monitoring of greenhouse gasses, and assessment of animal living habitats more affordable and more reliable. The analysis has greatly enhanced the use of data to make decisions and support environmental sustainability movements in various industries using AI. The extent of AI-driven Earth observation is growing as new nations are launching satellites and implementing open-access policies in sharing data. The availability of more data is likely to lead to significant progress in the resource management, environmental monitoring, and the development of the world, making artificial intelligence a characteristic of the future of Earth observation by satellites.

title: Design and Analysis of Graphene-Based High-Efficiency Heterostructure Solar Cell
authors: Imon Shahriar, Shanmim Karim Tonmoy, Tribid Mazumder, Sahil Hamid, Soumya Sen, Ashish Raman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In this study, we proposed and optimized a CdTe solar cell based on the cuprous oxide (Cu<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O) and Graphene (GO) as an HTL layer. In our proposed structure, the NiO layer is replaced by the Cu<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O, and the Go HTL layer is utilized by using the SCAPS-1D software. We studied the effects of the HTL (Cu<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O) and the Graphene layer on the Capacitance and voltage curve, J-V curve, and carrier concentration. We also examined how the device's solar cell performance was affected by the operating temperature. In this work, the maximum optimized power conversion efficiency (PCE) is achieved 24.05%, with a Voc equal to 0.99V, Jsc equal to 28.85mA/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and a fill factor of 83.93% which is much higher compared to the baseline structure. This combination presents a favourable avenue for the growth of future high-efficiency CdTe-based Photovoltaic cells.

title: Design and Comparative Analysis of PN and MOS Varactors for IRS-Enabled Wireless Systems
authors: Amit Kumar Rana, Mohit Kumar, Ashish Raman, Dinesh Singh, Rohit Singh, Shreya Swaraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: A comprehensive TCAD analysis and comparison of MOS and PN junction varactors for use in an Intelligent Reflecting Surface (IRS) are provided in this paper. IRS technology is mainly based on the use of tunable reactive components for dynamic control of the phase of reflected electromagnetic waves. In such systems, varactors act as the most critical tuning blocks. In order to conduct a fair and accurate analysis and comparison, the PN and MOS varactors are designed to have the same lateral sizes and are compared in zero DC current operation. The analysis and comparison is conducted in relation to the most important parameters such as suitability for IRS, linearity, sensitivity to Bias, Tuning ratio, and Capacitance Voltage curves. As per typical RF communications in an IRS technology, each of the Capacitance Voltage curves is tested at a frequency of 10 GHz. The simulation results clearly illustrate that MOS varactors possess a larger Tuning ratio by virtue of accumulation and depletion effects and hence are preferable for use in IRS-based beamforming and phase modulation. However, PN varactors are useful for their stability in reverse-biased operations.

title: Cloud and Fuzzy Technology in Social Life: Era of Digitalization in handling large Datasets for Decision Making Process
authors: Neha Tripathi, Nagendra Singh Gaira, Niyokhon Toshpulatova, Bobosharipov Feruzjon, Umrbek Karimov, Mirzayeva Gulchehra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The term "digital technologies" refers to a broad category of methods, instruments, and systems that gather, analyze, and send data digitally using electronic signals. Digital technology can enable data-driven decision-making and process optimization, but integrating these advancements fully into the sustainability framework remains a major problem. By offering insights into how digitalization can successfully achieve sustainability through process optimization and decision-making, this study seeks to close this gap. In order to facilitate inclusive and well-informed decision-making in contemporary social settings, this study suggests a fuzzy multi-criteria decision-making (FMCDM) strategy that can be used in the digital services sector. It has been demonstrated that fuzzy logic can be used in highly skilled systems. The characteristic function is converted to the membership function in fuzzy sets, which are crisp sets. The suggested framework is better adapted to deal with experts' reluctance, subjectivity, and the ambiguity present in their viewpoints since it offers a more thorough and adaptable expression of unclear information.

title: Design and Development of a Seven Stage Pipelined RISC-V Processor
authors: Rupani Mahendra, Vrinda Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a seven-stage pipelined RISC-V processor aimed at achieving high instruction throughput, high performance and optimised power for embedded and academic computing platforms.The seven stages of pipeline include fetch cycle 1, fetch cycle 2, decode cycle, execute cycle 1, execute cycle 2, memory cycle and write back cycle. The proposed architecture extends the conventional five-stage RISC-V pipeline by introducing additional stages for fetch and execute, thereby reducing critical path delays and improving clock frequency. The processor supports the RV32I instruction set and incorporates hazard detection, forwarding mechanisms and pipeline flushing to ensure correct and optimised execution.The seven-stage RISC-V pipelined processor is simulated and synthesised in Xilinx Vivado Design Suite, achieving a frequency of 250MHz with a worst negative slack (WNS) of 1.47ns, which indicates that, maximum frequency can go up to 395MHz. Power consumption has been optimised to 59mW. A modular Verilog HDL implementation is simulated and synthesised, demonstrating significant improvements in operating frequency and power compared to non-pipelined and traditional five-stage designs. Experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the seven-stage pipeline in balancing complexity, power consumption, and speed, making it suitable for high-performance educational and low-power embedded applications.

title: Nexa: Automatically Surfacing Business Impacting Insights in E-commerce Applications
authors: Smart Sun, Sayan Sinha, Haijie Wu, Joel Goldfoot, Aditya Ganjam, Jibin Jhan, Evan Chan, Qichu Gong, Vipul Harsh, Bo Lin, Pawas Ranjan, Wei Wang, Zhan Yang, Ningning Hu, B. Aditya Prakash, Vyas Sekar, Hui Zhang

title: An Empirical Study on Thermal Image Recognition Accuracy with Different Datasets
authors: Kentaro Kumagai, Gaku Masuda, Masaki Shuzo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: A method that uses thermal images taken by UAVs is useful for outdoor search and rescue operations of a person in distress or imminent danger. To ensure saving lives under time constraints, it is necessary to improve the dependability of this method. A machine learning algorithm is used to analyze the thermal images. This involves preparing images of a heat source to be searched for as training data and test data, training a model, and using the pre-trained model to detect objects in newly captured unseen data. Dataset used for training can be selected from datasets which are publicly available on the web open sourced, or datasets that have been acquired by a user his or herself. This research is an empirical study on the intercomparison of thermal image recognition accuracy with different datasets.
