title: SparKV: An Overhead-Aware KV Cache Loading for Efficient Inference for On-Device LLMs
authors: Hongyao Liu, Liuqun Zhai, Junyi Wang, Zhengru Fang
abstract: Efficient inference for on-device Large Language Models (LLMs) remains challenging due to limited hardware resources and the high cost of the prefill stage, which processes the full input context to construct Key-Value (KV) caches. We present SparKV, an adaptive KV loading framework that combines cloud-based KV streaming with on-device computation. SparKV models the cost of individual KV chunks and decides whether each chunk should be streamed or computed locally, while overlapping the two execution paths to reduce latency. To handle fluctuations in wireless connectivity and edge resource availability, SparKV further refines offline-generated schedules at runtime to rebalance communication and computation costs. Experiments across diverse datasets, LLMs, and edge devices show that SparKV reduces Time-to-First-Token by 1.3×-5.1× with negligible impact on response quality, while lowering per-request energy consumption by 1.5× to 3.3×, demonstrating its robustness and practicality for real-world on-device deployment.

title: Atmospheric Methane Monitoring Using Satellites: Bibliometric and visual analytics
authors: Botao He, Yong Xue, Wenhao Liu, Pei Li, Peng Wang, Liying Han, Kaiyuan Li
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: As the world’s second-largest greenhouse gas, methane has a global warming potential more than 28 times greater than that of carbon dioxide. Consequently, reducing methane emissions is considered one of the most effective short-term mitigation strategies for addressing global warming. To achieve this goal, the accurate identification of methane emission sources is critically important. Methane monitoring serves as a fundamental basis for pinpointing emission sources and supporting the implementation of mitigation actions. Currently, methane monitoring primarily relies on three principal approaches: ground-based observations, aerial monitoring, and satellite remote sensing. Among these, satellite remote sensing technology, owing to its advantages of wide coverage, high spatiotemporal continuity, and noncontact observation, has gradually overcome the limitations of traditional ground-based monitoring in spatial representativeness, coverage breadth, and cost efficiency. It has become an essential tool for monitoring methane emissions, quantifying methane emission sources and sinks, and evaluating the effectiveness of emission reduction measures at both global and regional scales. This study systematically reviews academic progress in the monitoring of atmospheric methane using satellite remote sensing techniques. Based on 621 relevant publications from the Web of Science database, bibliometric and visualization-based methods were employed to analyze research trends in satellite-based methane remote sensing. First, currently available satellite sensors used for methane monitoring were systematically cataloged. Second, influential core publications, key authors, leading countries, institutions, and journals within the field were identified and visually analyzed. Furthermore, by analyzing the citation structures of referenced articles, authors, and journals, the theoretical foundations and primarily knowledge sources of this research were revealed. Additionally, through keyword evolution paths and co-occurrence networks, the developmental trajectory of satellitebased methane remote sensing research and its projected future trends were traced. The findings of this analysis aim to provide researchers with comprehensive insights into the current state of the field and practical guidance for identifying future research directions.

title: PCGRLLM: Large Language Model-Driven Reward Design for Procedural Content Generation Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3695197.pdf
authors: Kyung-Joong Kim
abstract: Reward design plays a pivotal role in the training of game AIs, requiring substantial domain-specific knowledge and human effort. In recent years, several studies have explored reward generation for training game agents and controlling robots using large language models (LLMs). In the content generation literature, there has been early work on generating reward functions for reinforcement learning agent generators. This work introduces PCGRLLM, an extended architecture based on earlier work, which employs a feedback mechanism and several reasoning-based prompt engineering techniques. We evaluate the proposed method on a story-to-reward generation task in a two-dimensional environment using two state-of-the-art LLMs across various reasoning-based prompting methods. Our experiments provide insightful evaluations that demonstrate the capabilities of LLMs essential for content generation tasks. The results demonstrate a substantial performance improvement over the previous structure, achieving performance comparable to that of humans. Our work demonstrates the potential to reduce human dependency in game AI development, while supporting and enhancing creative processes.

title: New Finite Memory-Based Sliding Mode Control for Quadcopter
authors: Kwan Soo Kim, Hyun Duck Choi, Peng Shi, Choon Ki Ahn
abstract: In this article, we propose a new finite memory-based sliding mode control (FM-SMC) for robust quadcopter trajectory tracking. The new FM-SMC was developed by designing a finite memory-based disturbance observer (FM-DOB) for accurate and robust disturbance compensation and a finite memory-based neural network learning algorithm (FM-NNLA) for approximating unknown nonlinearities. Unlike conventional infinite-memory approaches that suffer from error accumulation and sensitivity to initial conditions, the proposed FM-DOB estimates and compensates for disturbances within a finite time horizon, while the FM-NNLA updates neural network weights using only recent state information to approximate nonlinear dynamics without long-term error accumulation. By leveraging these FM structures, the new FM-SMC enhances robustness against disturbances and system uncertainties while ensuring stable performance. Rigorous stability analysis is carried out using Lyapunov theory, and real-time quadcopter experiments on boustrophedon and ascending helical trajectories verify the robustness and superior effectiveness of the new FM-SMC.

title: A Unified Bayesian Framework for Topology Inference, Blind System Identification, and Source Localization in Edge-Cloud LAWNs
authors: Nan Wu, Jiayin Zhang, Tingting Zhang, Mehul Motani
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: Distributed data analysis tasks-including topology inference, blind system identification, and source localization-are critical for Low-Altitude Wireless Networks (LAWNs) architected on an edge-cloud paradigm. While recent efforts have explored joint estimation, they often infer deterministic graphs, overlooking the inherent stochasticity of wireless links, or are limited to specific sub-tasks, lacking a unified, end-to-end formulation. To overcome these limitations, we propose a unified Bayesian framework that cohesively addresses these interconnected challenges. We formulate a single hierarchical generative model integrating a probabilistic graph structure, a composite signal prior for smoothness and sparsity, a generalized graph operator, and observation noise. Building on this model, we derive a versatile and distributed variational inference framework, termed UBIG, which features a dual-scheme posterior approximation to efficiently handle different inference objectives. The UBIG framework is systematically instantiated to solve the three core tasks, and is naturally suited for edge-cloud architectures. Through extensive experiments simulating LAWNs, we demonstrate that our framework achieves superior accuracy and robustness compared to state-of-the-art methods, while providing principled uncertainty quantification.

title: Enhanced Integrated Decision and Control for High-Level Automated Vehicles and Its Experiment Verification
authors: Yang Guan, Liye Tang, Yao Lyu, Shengbo Eben Li, Yujie Yang, Kehua Sheng, Bo Zhang, Keqiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Learning through experience is essential for high-level autonomous driving systems, as it has the potential to enhance driving performance in corner cases. However, current decision and control modules adopt an empirical design paradigm for engineering efficiency, relying heavily on expert rules or real-vehicle data, failing to fully cover and optimize edge scenarios. To address this gap, we propose an enhanced integrated decision and control method that leverages reinforcement learning as the optimal control problem solver, endowing high-level automated vehicles with experience data usage. Specifically, a constrained mixed policy gradient algorithm is developed, which combines and dynamically adjusts the application ratio of experience data and the environmental model during training. This approach achieves fast convergence while maintaining high performance even with inaccurate analytic models. Furthermore, an attention based encoding network is designed to accommodate diverse driving states in urban traffic, integrating an embedding network for feature extraction and a weighting network for feature fusion, realizing order-insensitive encoding and importance differentiation of road users. The trained policy is deployed on a fully functional autonomous vehicle. Experiments at a signalized intersection show that the proposed method can accurately identify critical surrounding obstacles and execute safe, efficient, and intelligent driving behaviors across 32 scenarios.

title: P3SL: Personalized Privacy-Preserving Split Learning on Heterogeneous Edge Devices_supp1-3695367.pdf
authors: Wei Fan
abstract: Split Learning (SL) is an emerging privacy-preserving machine learning technique that enables resource constrained edge devices to participate in model training by partitioning a model into client-side and server-side sub-models. While SL reduces computational overhead on edge devices, it encounters significant challenges in heterogeneous environments where devices vary in computing resources, communication capabilities, environmental conditions, and privacy requirements. Although recent studies have explored heterogeneous SL frameworks that optimize split points for devices with varying resource constraints, they often neglect personalized privacy requirements and local model customization under varying environmental conditions. To address these limitations, we propose P3SL, a Personalized Privacy-Preserving Split Learning framework designed for heterogeneous, resource-constrained edge device systems. The key contributions of this work are twofold. First, we design a personalized sequential split learning pipeline that allows each client to achieve customized privacy protection and maintain personalized local models tailored to their computational resources, environmental conditions, and privacy needs. Second, we adopt a bi-level optimization technique that empowers clients to determine their own optimal personalized split points without sharing private sensitive information (i.e., computational resources, environmental conditions, privacy requirements) with the server. This approach balances energy consumption and privacy leakage risks while maintaining high model accuracy. We implement and evaluate P3SL on a testbed consisting of 7 devices including 4 Jetson Nano P3450 devices, 2 Raspberry Pis, and 1 laptop, using diverse model architectures and datasets under varying environmental conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that P3SL significantly mitigates privacy leakage risks, reduces system energy consumption by up to 59.12%, and consistently retains high accuracy compared to the state-of-the-art heterogeneous SL system.

title: FT-AFM: A Hybrid Transformer-Factorization Framework for Interpretable Click-Through Rate Prediction
authors: Bubacarr Jallow, Jin-Taek Seong
abstract: Click-through rate (CTR) prediction requires balancing predictive performance with model interpretability, which is essential for accountability in industrial advertising systems. While transformer-based architectures achieve high accuracy through implicit learning of complex feature interactions, they often operate as black boxes with limited transparency in decision-making. This paper presents FT-AFM, a hybrid framework that combines a transformer-based feature tokenization backbone for context-aware representation learning with an Attentional Factorization Machine (AFM) for explicit attention-weighted pairwise interaction modeling. Through shared embeddings across two coordinated branches, FT-AFM captures global field-wise dependencies while revealing the relative importance of individual feature interactions. Evaluations on three large-scale benchmarks—Criteo, Outbrain, and Avazu—show that FT-AFM achieves competitive AUC scores of 0.8076, 0.7003, and 0.7386, respectively, while remaining close to strong transformer-based baselines. We further introduce a faithfulness-based evaluation protocol to examine whether the learned attention weights are meaningfully related to the model’s reliance on specific interactions. Results suggest that FT-AFM offers a practical accuracy–interpretability trade-off for large-scale CTR prediction by enabling explicit interaction-level analysis while maintaining competitive predictive performance.

title: RIS-Assisted Bistatic Collaborative MIMO ISAC: Joint Beamforming and Reflection Coefficient Design Under Severe Weather Scenarios
authors: Hongyu Xu, Haoye Chai, Yaxi Liu, Boxin He, Meng Gu, Fangxin Wang, Wei Huangfu, Haijun Zhang
abstract: Joint beamforming and reflection coefficient matrix design is an essential task in reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems. However, existing monostatic RIS-assisted and cell-free ISAC systems struggle to provide robust link redundancy and collaborative functionality with acceptable overhead under severe weather scenarios. Motivated by this, we propose a novel RIS-assisted bistatic collaborative MIMO ISAC system that exploits the complementary strengths of RIS-assisted systems and cell-free architectures. Specifically, two spatially separated base stations and their associated RISs perform collaborative joint transmission for multi-user communications, while operating in a bistatic mode to acquire both angle-of-departure (AoD) and angle-of-arrival (AoA) for target positioning. We derive a Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for multi-target positioning and formulate a joint cooperative beamforming and RIS reflection coefficient matrix design problem to maximize the achievable sum-rate, subject to sensing CRB constraints. Various mathematical techniques are adopted to solve this non-convex problem, including the fractional programming, majorization–minimization, semidefinite relaxation, and Schur complement. Experiments in an envisioned rainstorm scenario demonstrate that the proposed system exhibits robustness and achieves a superior dual performance compared to conventional RIS-assisted and bistatic baselines.

title: RIS-Assisted Terahertz Clustered HetNets: Coverage and Rate Analysis
authors: Hadeel Obaid, Yongxu Zhu, Bo Tan
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Terahertz (THz) transmission technologies hold significant potential for enabling ultra-broadband in next-generation networks. Despite the vast bandwidth offered by the THz, it faces significant challenges stemming from its limited transmission range. A feasible scenario to mitigate the THz limited transmission range is to employ THz in clustered heterogeneous networks (HetNets) using Poisson cluster process (PCP) and placing a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) at the center of each cluster. In this paper, we investigate THz communication and RIS in clustered HetNets using stochastic geometry analysis. We derive tractable analytical expressions for association probabilities, distance distributions, Laplace transform of interference, coverage probability, and average achievable rate, which are validated via Monte Carlo simulation. We analyze the impact of various deployment parameters on the network performance to give insights on the THz system design. Simulation results show that RIS enhances user association to line-of-sight small base stations (SBS) in sparse-to-moderate clusters by offering alternate two-hop LoS paths. A non-monotonic trend is observed, where RIS benefits peak at moderate SBS densities. Moreover, SBS densification with tighter clustering is more effective than macro base stations (MBS) densification in THz networks, and user proximity to the RIS hotspot significantly impacts performance. In addition, the proposed system outperforms other benchmarks, including RIS-free, RIS not at the center, and Poisson point process (PPP) based THz HetNets.

title: Constraint-Oriented State-of-Charge Balancing and State-of-Power Prediction of Modular Battery Energy Storage Systems
authors: Enrique Nunes, Gaowen Liang, Ezequiel Rodriguez, Hein Wai Yan, Glen G. Farivar, Amer M. Y. M. Ghias, Josep Pou
abstract: State-of-charge (SoC) balancing is commonly utilized for modular battery energy storage systems (MBESSs). However, the existing methods assume that the MBESS power demand can always be met without violating constraints (for example, battery voltage limits). Hence, the existing methods may violate battery constraints, which can jeopardize the MBESS and trigger its protections. Moreover, the literature has largely overlooked a viable methodology for predicting the maximum power that a constrained MBESS can process over a given interval, a quantity referred to as the state-of-power, which is essential for effective MBESS scheduling. Addressing these gaps, this article presents a constraint-oriented SoC balancing method that guarantees the safe operation of MBESSs. In particular, the proposed method minimizes the MBESS SoC imbalance as permitted by constraints, and curtails the MBESS output power minimally if the power demand cannot be met without violating constraints. Furthermore, a computationally affordable state-of-the-power prediction algorithm for MBESSs operating under the proposed method is developed. The proposed method and algorithm are validated via simulations and experiments.

title: A Multiple Operation Modes GaN-Based Avalanche Phototransistor for UV Wideband Detection
authors: Hongyun Xie, Ziming Liu, Dong Han, Xiaoyan Yi, Yudong Ma, Weizhao Jiao, Yudi Liu, Tieyi Li, Wanrong Zhang
abstract: A top-illuminated GaN/InGaN avalanche heterojunction phototransistor (AHPT) based on heterojunction amplification and avalanche multiplication was proposed to overcome limitations in responsivity and sensitivity of GaN/InGaN detectors for ultraviolet (UV) detection. The top-illuminated structure was designed to enhance the current gain and response speed in HPT mode, ensure the breakdown voltage and carrier multiplication efficiency in APD mode, and meanwhile expand the spectrum bandwidth of the response. Based on a developed analytical model, the electric field distribution, energy band characteristics, and optoelectronic performance of the top-illuminated GaN/InGaN AHPT were analyzed when the phototransistor operated in HPT, APD, and AHPT modes, respectively. The simulation results demonstrate that the top-illuminated GaN/InGaN AHPT achieves a peak responsivity of 1.3 A/W in HPT mode, a responsivity of 4.0 A/W in AHPT mode, and a responsivity exceeding 100 A/W in APD mode due to avalanche multiplication. The dark current remains at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-{7}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> A and the maximum specific detectivity achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\times 10^{{12}}~\text {cm}\cdot \text {Hz}^{{0}.{5}}\cdot \text {W}^{-{1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The 3-dB bandwidth increased from 50 MHz in HPT mode to 200 MHz in APD mode, achieving an excellent balance between response speed and gain. In AHPT mode, the top-illuminated structure extended the spectral response to a wider band range of 350–418 nm, significantly improving UV detection capabilities.

title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning Empowered Tweet Sentiment Analysis
authors: Nida Hafeez, Abdullah, Maryam Shabbir, Fatima Shabbir, Muhammad Ateeb Ather
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: This is occasionally difficult to analyze vast amounts of textual and numerical data, especially when it comes to social media text like tweets and comments. In order to provide insights into public opinion and emotional cues, sentiment analysis (SA) attempts to automatically classify the sentiment of user-generated content. Using a dataset gathered from Kaggle, this study compares a number of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models for ternary sentiment classification ("neutral," "negative," and "positive"). Preprocessing and feature transformation techniques are applied to prepare the text for analysis. Experimental results show that the Bi-Directional LSTM model outperforms traditional approaches, achieving the highest accuracy of 97%, while models such as logistic regression (LR) and support vector machine (SVM) achieve lower performance, highlighting the advantage of sequential and contextual modeling for sentiment analysis.

title: FOC: Performance-Aware Intelligent Fuzzy-Based Optimization Clustering Framework for VANETs
authors: Deepika Singh, Sakshi Jain, Deepti Kakkar, Amrit Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) entails Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) as one of the primary elements of this communication, which ensures the reliability of vehicle-to-vehicle and vehicle-to-infrastructure communication in safety-critical and non-safety scenarios. However, the high network mobility, dynamic network topology, and unreliable wireless connections are major obstacles to scalable and reliable network performance. This paper intends to solve these problems, and a performance-conscious intelligent fuzzy-based optimization clustering (FOC) system of the dynamic VANET environments will be proposed. The prototype framework is based on the use of fuzzy logic to select intelligently cluster heads, based on a combined evaluation of residual energy, relative mobility, inter vehicle distance, link quality and local vehicle density. Moreover, a lightweight optimization assisted cluster refinement system is built into it to increase cluster stability and decrease re-clustering rate, especially in dense traffic and high-mobility situations. Numerous simulation outcomes reveal that the proposed FOC model has always shown superiority over the commonly used fuzzy-based and non-fuzzy clustering models in terms of packet delivery ratio, end-to-end delay, energy efficiency, cluster lifetime, and control overhead, which affirm its suitability in large and dynamically changing VANET environment.

title: Design and Analysis of Optimized Classifier for Prediction of Heart Diseases
authors: Ashish Digambar Sawant, M. S. Ali, L. S. Muthneja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Early identification of heart disease plays a vital role in reducing mortality and improving the quality of life of patients. This work presents a comparative study on the design and evaluation of optimized machine learning classifiers for heart disease prediction using structured clinical data. Several supervised learning algorithms, including Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, K-Nearest Neighbours, Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, and Extreme Gradient Boosting, are implemented and evaluated using a common preprocessing and validation framework. Emphasis is placed on data preprocessing, feature selection, handling class imbalance, and hyperparameter tuning to ensure fair comparison among models. Experimental results obtained from publicly available benchmark datasets indicate that ensemble-based methods generally achieve higher predictive performance, while simpler models retain advantages in interpretability and stability. The study highlights methodological considerations, performance trade-offs, and practical limitations relevant to real-world clinical deployment.

title: Environmental Implications of Semiconductor Industrial Expansion: An Empirical Investigation
authors: Priya Chaudhuri, Soumya Sen, Sahil Hamid, Ashish Raman, Imon Shahriar, Atanu Chowdhury, Abheek Gupta, Jyoti Anand, Mohd. Aslam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The semiconductor industry plays a crucial part in technological innovation, although there are also severe environmental issues concerning the substantial use of resources and the emission of emissions. In this respect, the paper outlines the empirical assessment regarding the environmental effects concerning the use of energy, water, and GHGs in the production of semiconductors. Fab-operations at less than 28 nm technology nodes require some magnitude of over 500 GWh power and around 1,500,000 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> ultra-pure water consumption annually per fab. GHG emissions vary from 2.8 to 4.5 kg CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-eq per wafer, with process gases during etching and deposition being the main contributors. A significant positive relationship (r>0.82) was found between production quantity and emission intensity. The study's results provide a strong incentive for semiconductor manufacturers to continue and enhance their sustainable production processes and governance.

title: Stress Detection and Decision Making Using Natural Language Processing and Fuzzy Sets to Prevent Burnout in Transformed Online Learning Platforms
authors: Preeti Badhani, Suresh Kumar M, Amina Ruzikulova, Muydinova Durdonakhon, Jumanazarov Sultanboy, Gulnoz Turaeva
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The fast shift to online learning platforms has made education more adaptable and accessible but has also presented such issues as the stress levels of learners, emotional exhaustion, and academic burnout. Such issues as the extended screen time, individual requirements of learning, lack of socialization, and the pressure of the performance also contribute to the increase of psychological stress among the learners. Early identification of stress and a timely response to that is hence important in maintaining the engagement and academic performance. The given framework examines textual data, which is created by learners during discussion forums, chats, feedback, and reflective submissions and applies techniques of Natural Language Processing to get linguistic, sentimental, emotional, and stress-related features. Natural language is naturally ambiguous and thus, fuzzy logic is employed to deal with ambiguity in interpretation of emotions. Depending on the levels of stress identified, the system allows students to implement adaptive interventions: learners with low levels of stress proceed with normal learning, those with moderate stress levels get motivational support and workload related adjustments, and learners with high risks activate immediate interventions. Experimental evidence indicates that the hybrid NLP-fuzzy model is also superior in terms of accuracy, precision and in recall, and thus it can be used in intelligent online learning systems.

title: LATON: A Quantum-Resilient Unified Compression and Encryption Technique For IoT
authors: Khan Farhan Rafat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: This paper presents LATON, a compression-encryption primitive that maps 3-bit inputs to 1-bit outputs using an 8 × 8 (3-bit) Latin square over ℤ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</inf>. The scheme achieves 3:1 bandwidth reduction while enabling perfect reconstruction for parties sharing a 256-hit master secret. For each 3-bit message block, a fresh 3-bit subkey is derived using a NIST SP 800-90A DRBG (CTR_DRBG with AES-256). With a subkey length equal to the message length, each ciphertext is equally likely for any plaintext. LATON operates at the presentation layer (Layer 6) and assumes a pre-shared key framework. Shannon’s source coding theorem, which presumes no decoder side information, does not apply, as LATON deliberately distributes two of three information bits through the key channel, a legitimate choice at Layer 6 where key material is presumed available. Exhaustive validation across all 64 possible (key, input) pairs confirms perfect reconstruction. By comparison with published implementations of lightweight ciphers (such as PRESENT at ~1,500 gates), we estimate LATON would require approximately 120 gate-equivalents in 65nm CMOS at 100 MHz, with 3-cycle latency, 1.67 pJ/bit energy, and 64-byte ROM. The 66% transmission reduction proportionally lowers radio energy consumption, the dominant power draw in IoT devices. Security derives from the 256-bit master secret and NIST-approved DRBG, consistent with Kerckhoffs’s principle. LATON is thus well-suited for ultra-low-power IoT applications requiring mathematically guaranteed reconstruction and bandwidth efficiency.

title: Dynamic Threat Detection and Response Mechanisms for Cloud Security Enhancement
authors: Prashant Srivastava, Sushil Kumar Saroj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The organizations are adopting the cloud because of its various key features like scalability, cost-efficiency, and adaptability, but it has various security challenges too especially around dynamic threat detection and its response mechanism. For example, various techniques are based on signature-based IDS, which do not guarantee the threats that are dynamic in nature. This research paper identifies these gaps and proposes the framework, which is based on an integrated advanced threat detection mechanism, as well as highlights the response mechanism also. The proposed architecture has real time analytics, machine learning algorithms and an automated response mechanism, which minimizes the insider threats and advanced persistent threats. After analyzing and continuously monitoring the cloud traffic based on the behavior and anomalies, organizations will definitely provide strengthen their security.

title: Comparing Efficiency-Oriented and Flow-Theory-Informed Design Approaches for Support Functions in Remote Robot Operation
authors: Takumi Kawamura
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: When operators with severe motor dysfunction collaborate with semiautonomous teleoperated robots, traditional assistive interventions that prioritize task efficiency may reduce satisfaction and increase anxiety. To develop a flow theory–informed design approach that balances efficiency and positive psychological states, this study constructs a hierarchical model linking the conceptual goal of enhanced flow intensity to specific design conditions and operational metrics through intermediate objective variables identified in previous research. A secondary analysis of expanded datasets identified three categories of design elements: (1) synergistic-bird's-eye view feedback and reduced rotational operations were consistently associated with enhanced flow intensity and improved efficiency; (2) conflicting -avoiding high-speed mode, which generally enhances efficiency, was associated with increased flow intensity; (3) ambivalent—perspective-emphasized feedback and the number of translational operations were associated with increased and decreased flow intensity. These findings advance design approaches that integrate performance and psychological well-being by extracting key flowinducing factors from a vast design space.

title: Integrating Haptic Gloves with Cutaneous Feedback and Virtual Reality for Medical Training
authors: Aaron Raymond See, Thad Jacob T. Tiong, Vence Jumar A. Sasing, Arianne Clyde Inoc, Joan Marie Odango, Tsung-Lu Michael Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study presents the development of a haptic glove integrated with a virtual reality (VR) system to enhance medical training through immersive tactile feedback. The glove incorporates high-resolution pin arrays that deliver detailed cutaneous feedback, providing realistic tactile sensations. The VR environment, created using Unity and integrated with the Meta Quest Oculus, features an interactive autopsy lab with detailed anatomical models of organs, enabling users to perform virtual dissections and medical procedures. System evaluations demonstrated seamless communication between the glove, VR environment, and hand-tracking components, ensuring low-latency interactions and precise tactile responses. Compared to existing haptic devices, this system emphasizes a compact design with high tactile resolution, overcoming limitations related to external hardware dependencies and restricted feedback fidelity. The findings highlight the system's potential to improve procedural training and anatomical education by offering a realistic simulation experience. Future enhancements will focus on improving hand-tracking precision and increasing the glove's range of motion to support more complex medical simulations. This study underscores the role of advanced haptic technology in bridging the sensory gap in virtual environments, paving the way for more effective VR-based medical training solutions.

title: CS2AnalyticsAPP: A Multi-Player Shooter Game Analytics Framework
authors: Youssof Eshrah, Yomna M.I. Hassan
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Game analytics play a crucial role in understanding and enhancing player performance by transforming raw gameplay data into actionable insights. In this paper, a novel approach is presented to Counter-Strike 2 (CS2) analytics that extends beyond traditional performance metrics to incorporate behavioral patterns and decision-making analysis. By leveraging machine learning and natural language processing, the system provides insights into how player psychology manifests in gameplay across various in-game situations. The research develops an end-to-end pipeline for automating replay collection via web scraping, extracting behavioral features through replay parsing, and applying tree-based ensemble models and neural networks for round outcome prediction. Three distinct machine learning approaches-XGBoost, LSTM, and Random Forest-were implemented and compared, with XGBoost achieving the highest accuracy at 95.84 %. Validation through professional match analysis demonstrated both the system's strengths in assessing standard gameplay scenarios and its limitations in capturing complex cooperative strategies. The developed desktop application provides players with an interactive interface to visualize performance metrics, understand predictive factors, and receive personalized improvement recommendations.

title: Predictive Model for Detecting Traffic Accident Risks Using Random Forest in the Transport Sector
authors: Herme Gianni Del Pino Mejía, Miguel Angel Espinoza Surichaqui, Jymmy Stuwart Dextre Alarcón
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Accidents in the transportation sector are a critical public safety issue in Peru, especially in densely populated urban areas. Traditional response systems are often insufficient to prevent incidents due to their reactive nature and the lack of real-time risk analysis. To overcome this limitation, a predictive model based on the Random Forest algorithm is proposed to identify and monitor traffic accident risks with greater accuracy. The proposed solution analyzes large volumes of data from multiple sources, such as GPS coordinates, Geospatial maps, weather conditions, and historical records, to detect hazardous patterns and generate early warnings. The system processes data in real time using machine learning techniques, considering factors such as vehicle density, poor road conditions, and adverse weather. The proposal is structured into six main components aligned with the data pipeline methodology: data acquisition, storage, processing, analytics and predictive modeling, exploitation, and visualization. Together, these phases enable a complete flow from collecting and organizing heterogeneous data sources to generating predictive insights, deploying early warning mechanisms, and delivering visualization tools such as heat maps and automated alerts for decision-makers. The solution integrates mechanisms to ensure data quality and a continuous learning approach, enabling the model's accuracy to improve as new data is incorporated. The expected outcome is a measurable decrease in risk exposure, with accident-prone hotspots potentially showing up to a 25% reduction in critical incidents when compared to traditional monitoring methods.

title: SteinEmbed: Learning Word Representations Through Geometric Space Optimization
authors: Youssef S. Soliman, Moataz A. El Zayat, Amr M. Amin
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Word embeddings play an important role in natural language processing by mapping semantic relationships into dense vector spaces. In this work, we present a comprehensive framework for studying the geometric and statistical properties of embedding spaces. Our analysis covers dimensionality-reduced projections, clustering patterns, distributions, variances, and covariances, providing detailed insights into how embeddings encode semantic information. Building on these observations, we introduce SteinEmbed, a research-oriented framework that leverages Stein Variational Gradient Descent to adapt embedding training to specific geometric and cluster structures. Experiments demonstrate that SteinEmbed achieves performance comparable to the baselines in both intrinsic and extrinsic evaluation tasks, while also exhibiting better cluster quality and lower entropy, highlighting its utility as a tool for systematically investigating and optimizing the geometric organization of word embeddings.

title: Bluetooth RSSI-Based Autonomous Following Cart with Hand Gesture Control
authors: Ting-Ting Yang, Po-An Tsai, Ze-Wei Chen, Hsueh-Wen Tseng
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study presents a low-cost, multimodal smart cart designed for autonomous following and natural user interaction via hand gestures. The system integrates RSSI-based localization using Bluetooth, hand gesture recognition on Jetson Nano via MediaPipe, and low-latency command execution on ESP32 via UART. A cloud-assisted prototype featuring Apache Flink and Kafka was developed to evaluate RSSI-based direction inference using real-time data streams from an Android device. Experimental results indicate that UART communication achieves a stable round-trip latency of under 2 ms, contributing to an estimated end-to-end delay under 120 ms, suitable for responsive control tasks. The system demonstrates potential for deployment in home, healthcare, and logistics environments.

title: Design and Analysis of Hybrid Precoding Schemes for THz Massive MIMO in Emerging Wireless Networks
authors: A. Sammaiah, M. Pranay Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Owing to the extremely large available spectrum, terahertz communications is becoming a key enabling technologies for upcoming 6G wireless systems. THz communications serves as the foundation for the wireless networks of the future. This paper aims to comprehensively examine the primary Terahertz precoding technique’s for 6G network, focusing on the key challenge they present. Initially, we provide an overview of a typical THz application scenario. Subsequently, we explicitly outline the major distinctions among mm-wave and terahertz channel’s, which form the basis for understanding the key challenges faced in THz precoding, However, THz systems suffer from issues such as beam squint and high energy consumption. To address these limitations, two major THz precoding approaches—hybrid precoding and delay–phase precoding are introduced. These methods are comprehensively examined with respect to their architectures, design principles, recent developments, as well as their strengths and weaknesses.

title: Design and Optimization of JL-NSFETs for DC, Analog, RF and Circuit-Level Applications Under Temperature and Nanosheet Engineering at sub-5 nm Technology Node
authors: Vadthya Bheemudu, Dhandapani Vaithiyanathan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This article provides a comprehensive TCAD based investigation into the impact of nanosheet count and temperature variations on DC, RF/Analog properties of JL-NSFETs. Digital performance metrics are analyzed over a temperature range of 250K to 450K for a two-nanosheet configuration, while the analog and RF characteristics are evaluated for nanosheet numbers ranging from one to four under the same temperature span. In addition, circuit-level assessments are carried out using CMOS inverter with two and three nanosheet devices. The results reveal that lower operating temperatures, particularly at 250K, effectively reduce leakage current and enhance electrostatic integrity. Consequently, substantial improvements are observed in key DC performance including the I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio, SS and DIBL. At lower temperatures, the analog and RF figures of merit also exhibit significant enhancement, as evidenced by improvement of g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>, g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>, f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>, TFP and GTFP. These performance enhancements highlight the suitability of JL-NSFETs for cryogenic electronics, satellite systems, deep-space exploration, and aerospace applications. Furthermore, nanosheet count improves drive current, g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf> and gate control due to the enlarged effective channel width. However, excessive nanosheet stacking introduces additional parasitic capacitances, which adversely affect propagation delay and energy efficiency. Circuit-level analysis using CMOS inverter configurations confirms that the two-nanosheet architecture delivers the most balanced performance, achieving improved NM gain, whereas devices with a larger number of nanosheets suffer from increased delay and EDP. Overall, the study identifies two-nanosheet JL-NSFETs operating at lower temperatures as promising candidates for energy-efficient and high-performance digital, RF and analog integrated circuit applications.

title: Sneak Path Mitigation in Memristor Crossbar Arrays Using Hybrid Adaptive Biasing and Sectioned Read Architecture
authors: Dinesh Singh, Ashish Raman, Ravita
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: For high-density non-volatile memory and in-memory computer systems, memristor-based crossbar arrays are frequently considered as promising solutions. However, the sneak path currents significantly reduce read reliability, restrict array scalability, and increases power consumption. In this paper, a novel sneak path mitigation method based on sectioned read architecture and hybrid adaptive biasing is proposed. The proposed approach suppresses sneak path currents while maintaining array density, unlike conventional selector-based or 1T1R array. Circuit-level analysis and behavioral simulations demonstrate significant improvement in read margin and energy efficiency compared to traditional read schemes. The proposed technique is suitable for large-scale memristive memories and neuromorphic computing systems.

title: Next-Generation IoT Health Sentinel: Continuous Monitoring with Instant Alerts for Patient Safety
authors: Swapnil Srivastava, Pankaj Kumar Keshri, Rahul Singh, Shobha Chaudhary, Sangita Ranjan, Ashish Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This proposed system is a smart patient health monitor system using Internet of Things (IoT) that monitors physiological and environmental variables of critical importance and in real-time. It is meant to be applied in remote health facilities, such as post-surgical facilities, geriatrics, and rural health services. It addresses the problem of inconsistent or insufficient access to conventional healthcare. The core element of the system is an ESP32 microcontroller, chosen due to an inbuilt Wi-Fi/Bluetooth connection, low power consumption, and dual-core computing. The device has relatively cheap, non-invasive sensors to monitor body temperature (digital thermistor), the ambient temperature and humidity (DHT11/DHT22), and the heart rate (optical pulse sensor). Due to this, patients and carers are able to track their health indicators without additional equipment since they localize processing and real-time display of data collected by these sensors on a small screen display (OLED). The system is also linked to the Blynk IoT cloud platform that offers real-time synchronization of data, visualization of data in a dashboard and personalized warnings delivered by the web interface or the mobile application. The system is automatically activated by sending signals in extreme cases when the child has a high fever, fast pulse rate or in bad weather to take speedy measures. This enhances prevention and early intervention, which is why the solution is preferable in the setting where getting the medical staff may not be possible immediately.

title: Peeking Under the Hood of Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Tie Ma, Yixi Chen, Vaastav Anand, Alessandro Cornacchia, Amândio R. Faustino, Guanheng Liu, Shan Zhang, Hongbin Luo, Suhaib A. Fahmy, Zafar A. Qazi, Marco Canini
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Conference on AI and Agentic Systems
abstract: None

title: Toward Enhanced Hardware IP Security: Community Evaluation of Logic Locking, State Space Transformation, Redaction, and NoC Routing Table Configuration
authors: Sudipta Paria, Zhaokun Han, Aritra Dasgupta, Habibur Rahaman, Dipal Halder, Rasheed Almawzan, Daniel Xing, Kostas Amberiadis, Sandip Ray, Ankur Srivastava, Jeyavijayan Rajendran, Swarup Bhunia
publish: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems

title: Woodbury–ADMM for Sparse Beamforming in Large-Scale MIMO Radar
authors: S. B. M. Priya, V. Shahul Hameed, S. Nadaraja Pillai
abstract: This paper studies sparse beamforming for radar systems using uniform planar array (UPA) with half-wavelength spacing. The array receives signals from targets, clutter, and noise. The beamformer is designed to minimize interference while enforcing unit-gain on targets and nulls on clutter, maximizing output SINR. Sparsity reduces hardware complexity and improves robustness. For large arrays, conventional ADMM is computationally costly due to repeated high-dimensional matrix inversions. We address this using the Sherman–Morrison Woodbury (SMW) identity within ADMM, reducing per-iteration complexity from cubic to nearly linear.This paper presents the application of SMW-accelerated ADMM in the context of sparse MIMO radar beamforming with structured target and clutter constraints. Simulations confirm effective clutter suppression, interference mitigation, reliable target detection, and orders-of magnitude runtime reduction, making it suitable for large-scale radar systems.
